verses.slok: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच |धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः |मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ||1||
verses.transliteration: dhṛitarāśhtra uvāchadharma-kṣhetre kuru-kṣhetre samavetā yuyutsavaḥmāmakāḥ pāṇḍavāśhchaiva kimakurvata sañjaya
verses.translation: BG 1.1:Dhritarashtra said: O Sanjay, after gathering on the holy field of Kurukshetra, and desiring to fight, what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do?

verses.slok: सञ्जय उवाच ।दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा ।आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत् ।। 2।।
verses.transliteration: sañjaya uvāchadṛiṣhṭvā tu pāṇḍavānīkaṁ vyūḍhaṁ duryodhanastadāāchāryamupasaṅgamya rājā vachanamabravīt
verses.translation: BG 1.2:Sanjay said: On observing the Pandava army standing in military formation, King Duryodhan approached his teacher Dronacharya, and said the following words.

verses.slok: पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणामाचार्य महतीं चमूम् ।व्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता ।। 3।।
verses.transliteration: paśhyaitāṁ pāṇḍu-putrāṇām āchārya mahatīṁ chamūmvyūḍhāṁ drupada-putreṇa tava śhiṣhyeṇa dhīmatā
verses.translation: BG 1.3:Duryodhan said: Respected teacher!  Behold the mighty army of the sons of Pandu, so expertly arrayed for battle by your own gifted disciple, the son of Drupad.

verses.slok: अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधियुयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथ: || 4||
verses.transliteration: atra śhūrā maheṣhvāsā bhīmārjuna-samā yudhiyuyudhāno virāṭaśhcha drupadaśhcha mahā-rathaḥdhṛiṣhṭaketuśhchekitānaḥ kāśhirājaśhcha vīryavānpurujit kuntibhojaśhcha śhaibyaśhcha nara-puṅgavaḥyudhāmanyuśhcha vikrānta uttamaujāśhcha vīryavānsaubhadro draupadeyāśhcha sarva eva mahā-rathāḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.4-6:Behold in their ranks are many powerful warriors, like Yuyudhan, Virat, and Drupad, wielding mighty bows and equal in military prowess to Bheem and Arjun.  There are also accomplished heroes like Dhrishtaketu, Chekitan, the gallant King of Kashi, Purujit, Kuntibhoj, and Shaibya—all the best of men.  In their ranks, they also have the courageous Yudhamanyu, the gallant Uttamauja, the son of Subhadra, and the sons of Draupadi, who are all great warrior chiefs.

verses.slok: अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधियुयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथ: || 4||
verses.transliteration: atra śhūrā maheṣhvāsā bhīmārjuna-samā yudhiyuyudhāno virāṭaśhcha drupadaśhcha mahā-rathaḥdhṛiṣhṭaketuśhchekitānaḥ kāśhirājaśhcha vīryavānpurujit kuntibhojaśhcha śhaibyaśhcha nara-puṅgavaḥyudhāmanyuśhcha vikrānta uttamaujāśhcha vīryavānsaubhadro draupadeyāśhcha sarva eva mahā-rathāḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.4-6:Behold in their ranks are many powerful warriors, like Yuyudhan, Virat, and Drupad, wielding mighty bows and equal in military prowess to Bheem and Arjun.  There are also accomplished heroes like Dhrishtaketu, Chekitan, the gallant King of Kashi, Purujit, Kuntibhoj, and Shaibya—all the best of men.  In their ranks, they also have the courageous Yudhamanyu, the gallant Uttamauja, the son of Subhadra, and the sons of Draupadi, who are all great warrior chiefs.

verses.slok: अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधियुयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथ: || 4||
verses.transliteration: atra śhūrā maheṣhvāsā bhīmārjuna-samā yudhiyuyudhāno virāṭaśhcha drupadaśhcha mahā-rathaḥdhṛiṣhṭaketuśhchekitānaḥ kāśhirājaśhcha vīryavānpurujit kuntibhojaśhcha śhaibyaśhcha nara-puṅgavaḥyudhāmanyuśhcha vikrānta uttamaujāśhcha vīryavānsaubhadro draupadeyāśhcha sarva eva mahā-rathāḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.4-6:Behold in their ranks are many powerful warriors, like Yuyudhan, Virat, and Drupad, wielding mighty bows and equal in military prowess to Bheem and Arjun.  There are also accomplished heroes like Dhrishtaketu, Chekitan, the gallant King of Kashi, Purujit, Kuntibhoj, and Shaibya—all the best of men.  In their ranks, they also have the courageous Yudhamanyu, the gallant Uttamauja, the son of Subhadra, and the sons of Draupadi, who are all great warrior chiefs.

verses.slok: अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम |नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते || 7||
verses.transliteration: asmākaṁ tu viśhiṣhṭā ye tānnibodha dwijottamanāyakā mama sainyasya sanjñārthaṁ tānbravīmi te
verses.translation: BG 1.7:O best of Brahmins, hear too about the principal generals on our side, who are especially qualified to lead. These I now recount unto you.

verses.slok: भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जय: |अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च || 8||
verses.transliteration: bhavānbhīṣhmaśhcha karṇaśhcha kṛipaśhcha samitiñjayaḥaśhvatthāmā vikarṇaśhcha saumadattis tathaiva cha
verses.translation: BG 1.8:There are personalities like yourself, Bheeshma, Karna, Kripa, Ashwatthama, Vikarn, and Bhurishrava, who are ever victorious in battle.

verses.slok: अन्ये च बहव: शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविता: |
verses.transliteration: anye cha bahavaḥ śhūrā madarthe tyaktajīvitāḥnānā-śhastra-praharaṇāḥ sarve yuddha-viśhāradāḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.9:Also, there are many other heroic warriors, who are prepared to lay down their lives for my sake. They are all skilled in the art of warfare, and equipped with various kinds of weapons.

verses.slok: अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम् |
verses.transliteration: aparyāptaṁ tadasmākaṁ balaṁ bhīṣhmābhirakṣhitamparyāptaṁ tvidameteṣhāṁ balaṁ bhīmābhirakṣhitam
verses.translation: BG 1.10:The strength of our army is unlimited and we are safely marshalled by Grandsire Bheeshma, while the strength of the Pandava army, carefully marshalled by Bheem, is limited.

verses.slok: अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिता: |भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्त: सर्व एव हि || 11||
verses.transliteration: ayaneṣhu cha sarveṣhu yathā-bhāgamavasthitāḥbhīṣhmamevābhirakṣhantu bhavantaḥ sarva eva hi
verses.translation: BG 1.11:Therefore, I call upon all the generals of the Kaurava army now to give full support to Grandsire Bheeshma, even as you defend your respective strategic points.

verses.slok: तस्य सञ्जनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्ध: पितामह: |सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चै: शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान् || 12||
verses.transliteration: tasya sañjanayan harṣhaṁ kuru-vṛiddhaḥ pitāmahaḥsiṁha-nādaṁ vinadyochchaiḥ śhaṅkhaṁ dadhmau pratāpavān
verses.translation: BG 1.12:Then, the grand old man of the Kuru dynasty, the glorious patriarch Bheeshma, roared like a lion, and blew his conch shell very loudly, giving joy to Duryodhan.

verses.slok: तत: शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखा: |सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् || 13||
verses.transliteration: tataḥ śhaṅkhāśhcha bheryaśhcha paṇavānaka-gomukhāḥsahasaivābhyahanyanta sa śhabdastumulo ’bhavat
verses.translation: BG 1.13:Thereafter, conches, kettledrums, bugles, trumpets, and horns suddenly blared forth, and their combined sound was overwhelming.

verses.slok: तत: श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ |माधव: पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतु: || 14||
verses.transliteration: tataḥ śhvetairhayairyukte mahati syandane sthitaumādhavaḥ pāṇḍavaśhchaiva divyau śhaṅkhau pradadhmatuḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.14:Then, from amidst the Pandava army, seated in a glorious chariot drawn by white horses, Madhav and Arjun blew their Divine conch shells.

verses.slok: पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनञ्जय: |पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदर: || 15||
verses.transliteration: pāñchajanyaṁ hṛiṣhīkeśho devadattaṁ dhanañjayaḥpauṇḍraṁ dadhmau mahā-śhaṅkhaṁ bhīma-karmā vṛikodaraḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.15:Hrishikesh blew his conch shell, called Panchajanya, and Arjun blew the Devadutta. Bheem, the voracious eater and performer of herculean tasks, blew his mighty conch, called Paundra.

verses.slok: अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिर: |नकुल: सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ || 16||काश्यश्च परमेष्वास: शिखण्डी च महारथ: |धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजित: || 17||द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वश: पृथिवीपते |सौभद्रश्च महाबाहु: शङ्खान्दध्मु: पृथक् पृथक् || 18||
verses.transliteration: anantavijayaṁ rājā kuntī-putro yudhiṣhṭhiraḥnakulaḥ sahadevaśhcha sughoṣha-maṇipuṣhpakaukāśhyaśhcha parameṣhvāsaḥ śhikhaṇḍī cha mahā-rathaḥdhṛiṣhṭadyumno virāṭaśhcha sātyakiśh chāparājitaḥdrupado draupadeyāśhcha sarvaśhaḥ pṛithivī-patesaubhadraśhcha mahā-bāhuḥ śhaṅkhāndadhmuḥ pṛithak pṛithak
verses.translation: BG 1.16-18:King Yudhishthir, blew the Anantavijay, while Nakul and Sahadev blew the Sughosh and Manipushpak. The excellent archer and king of Kashi, the great warrior Shikhandi, Dhrishtadyumna, Virat, and the invincible Satyaki, Drupad, the five sons of Draupadi, and the mighty-armed Abhimanyu, son of Subhadra, all blew their respective conch shells, O Ruler of the earth.

verses.slok: अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिर: |नकुल: सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ || 16||काश्यश्च परमेष्वास: शिखण्डी च महारथ: |धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजित: || 17||द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वश: पृथिवीपते |सौभद्रश्च महाबाहु: शङ्खान्दध्मु: पृथक् पृथक् || 18||
verses.transliteration: anantavijayaṁ rājā kuntī-putro yudhiṣhṭhiraḥnakulaḥ sahadevaśhcha sughoṣha-maṇipuṣhpakaukāśhyaśhcha parameṣhvāsaḥ śhikhaṇḍī cha mahā-rathaḥdhṛiṣhṭadyumno virāṭaśhcha sātyakiśh chāparājitaḥdrupado draupadeyāśhcha sarvaśhaḥ pṛithivī-patesaubhadraśhcha mahā-bāhuḥ śhaṅkhāndadhmuḥ pṛithak pṛithak
verses.translation: BG 1.16-18:King Yudhishthir, blew the Anantavijay, while Nakul and Sahadev blew the Sughosh and Manipushpak. The excellent archer and king of Kashi, the great warrior Shikhandi, Dhrishtadyumna, Virat, and the invincible Satyaki, Drupad, the five sons of Draupadi, and the mighty-armed Abhimanyu, son of Subhadra, all blew their respective conch shells, O Ruler of the earth.

verses.slok: अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिर: |नकुल: सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ || 16||काश्यश्च परमेष्वास: शिखण्डी च महारथ: |धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजित: || 17||द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वश: पृथिवीपते |सौभद्रश्च महाबाहु: शङ्खान्दध्मु: पृथक् पृथक् || 18||
verses.transliteration: anantavijayaṁ rājā kuntī-putro yudhiṣhṭhiraḥnakulaḥ sahadevaśhcha sughoṣha-maṇipuṣhpakaukāśhyaśhcha parameṣhvāsaḥ śhikhaṇḍī cha mahā-rathaḥdhṛiṣhṭadyumno virāṭaśhcha sātyakiśh chāparājitaḥdrupado draupadeyāśhcha sarvaśhaḥ pṛithivī-patesaubhadraśhcha mahā-bāhuḥ śhaṅkhāndadhmuḥ pṛithak pṛithak
verses.translation: BG 1.16-18:King Yudhishthir, blew the Anantavijay, while Nakul and Sahadev blew the Sughosh and Manipushpak. The excellent archer and king of Kashi, the great warrior Shikhandi, Dhrishtadyumna, Virat, and the invincible Satyaki, Drupad, the five sons of Draupadi, and the mighty-armed Abhimanyu, son of Subhadra, all blew their respective conch shells, O Ruler of the earth.

verses.slok: स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत् |
verses.transliteration: sa ghoṣho dhārtarāṣhṭrāṇāṁ hṛidayāni vyadārayatnabhaśhcha pṛithivīṁ chaiva tumulo abhyanunādayan
verses.translation: BG 1.19:The terrific sound thundered across the sky and the earth, and shattered the hearts of your sons, O Dhritarasthra.

verses.slok: अथ व्यवस्थितान्दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रान् कपिध्वज: |प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसम्पाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डव: ||20||हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते |
verses.transliteration: atha vyavasthitān dṛiṣhṭvā dhārtarāṣhṭrān kapi-dhwajaḥpravṛitte śhastra-sampāte dhanurudyamya pāṇḍavaḥhṛiṣhīkeśhaṁ tadā vākyam idam āha mahī-pate
verses.translation: BG 1.20:At that time, the son of Pandu, Arjun, who had the insignia of Hanuman on the flag of his chariot, took up his bow. Seeing your sons arrayed against him, O King, Arjun then spoke the following words to Shree Krishna.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत || 21||यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान् |कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन् रणसमुद्यमे || 22||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchasenayor ubhayor madhye rathaṁ sthāpaya me ’chyutayāvadetān nirīkṣhe ’haṁ yoddhu-kāmān avasthitānkairmayā saha yoddhavyam asmin raṇa-samudyame
verses.translation: BG 1.21-22:Arjun said: O Infallible One, please take my chariot to the middle of both armies, so that I may look at the warriors arrayed for battle, whom I must fight in this great combat.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत || 21||यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान् |कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन् रणसमुद्यमे || 22||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchasenayor ubhayor madhye rathaṁ sthāpaya me ’chyutayāvadetān nirīkṣhe ’haṁ yoddhu-kāmān avasthitānkairmayā saha yoddhavyam asmin raṇa-samudyame
verses.translation: BG 1.21-22:Arjun said: O Infallible One, please take my chariot to the middle of both armies, so that I may look at the warriors arrayed for battle, whom I must fight in this great combat.

verses.slok: योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागता: |धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षव: || 23||
verses.transliteration: yotsyamānān avekṣhe ’haṁ ya ete ’tra samāgatāḥdhārtarāṣhṭrasya durbuddher yuddhe priya-chikīrṣhavaḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.23:I desire to see those who have come here to fight on the side of the evil-minded son of Dhritarasthra, wishing to please him.

verses.slok: सञ्जय उवाच |एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत |सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम् || 24||
verses.transliteration: sañjaya uvāchaevam ukto hṛiṣhīkeśho guḍākeśhena bhāratasenayor ubhayor madhye sthāpayitvā rathottamam
verses.translation: BG 1.24:Sanjay said: O Dhritarasthra, having thus been addressed by Arjun, the conqueror of sleep, Shree Krishna then drew the magnificent chariot between the two armies.

verses.slok: भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखत: सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम् |उवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति || 25||
verses.transliteration: bhīṣhma-droṇa-pramukhataḥ sarveṣhāṁ cha mahī-kṣhitāmuvācha pārtha paśhyaitān samavetān kurūn iti
verses.translation: BG 1.25:In the presence of Bheeshma, Dronacharya, and all the other kings, Shree Krishna said: O Parth, behold these Kurus gathered here.

verses.slok: तत्रापश्यत्स्थितान् पार्थ: पितृ नथ पितामहान् |आचार्यान्मातुलान्भ्रातृ न्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्सखींस्तथा || 26||श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि |
verses.transliteration: tatrāpaśhyat sthitān pārthaḥ pitṝīn atha pitāmahānāchāryān mātulān bhrātṝīn putrān pautrān sakhīṁs tathāśhvaśhurān suhṛidaśh chaiva senayor ubhayor api
verses.translation: BG 1.26:There, Arjun could see stationed in both armies, his fathers, grandfathers, teachers, maternal uncles, brothers, cousins, sons, nephews, grand-nephews, friends, fathers-in-law, and well-wishers.

verses.slok: तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेय: सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान् || 27||कृपया परयाविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत् |
verses.transliteration: tān samīkṣhya sa kaunteyaḥ sarvān bandhūn avasthitānkṛipayā parayāviṣhṭo viṣhīdann idam abravīt
verses.translation: BG 1.27:Seeing all his relatives present there, Arjun, the son of Kunti, was overwhelmed with compassion, and with deep sorrow, spoke the following words.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् || 28||सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति |
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchadṛiṣhṭvemaṁ sva-janaṁ kṛiṣhṇa yuyutsuṁ samupasthitamsīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhaṁ cha pariśhuṣhyati
verses.translation: BG 1.28:Arjun said: O Krishna, seeing my own kinsmen arrayed for battle here and intent on killing each other, my limbs are giving way and my mouth is drying up.

verses.slok: वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते || 29||गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चै व परिदह्यते |न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मन: || 30||निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव |न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे || 31||
verses.transliteration: vepathuśh cha śharīre me roma-harṣhaśh cha jāyategāṇḍīvaṁ sraṁsate hastāt tvak chaiva paridahyatena cha śhaknomy avasthātuṁ bhramatīva cha me manaḥnimittāni cha paśhyāmi viparītāni keśhavana cha śhreyo ’nupaśhyāmi hatvā sva-janam āhave
verses.translation: BG 1.29-31:My whole body shudders; my hair is standing on end. My bow, the Gāṇḍīv, is slipping from my hand, and my skin is burning all over. My mind is in quandary and whirling in confusion; I am unable to hold myself steady any longer. O Krishna, killer of the Keshi demon, I only see omens of misfortune. I do not foresee how any good can come from killing my own kinsmen in this battle.

verses.slok: वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते || 29||गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चै व परिदह्यते |न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मन: || 30||निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव |न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे || 31||
verses.transliteration: vepathuśh cha śharīre me roma-harṣhaśh cha jāyategāṇḍīvaṁ sraṁsate hastāt tvak chaiva paridahyatena cha śhaknomy avasthātuṁ bhramatīva cha me manaḥnimittāni cha paśhyāmi viparītāni keśhavana cha śhreyo ’nupaśhyāmi hatvā sva-janam āhave
verses.translation: BG 1.29-31:My whole body shudders; my hair is standing on end. My bow, the Gāṇḍīv, is slipping from my hand, and my skin is burning all over. My mind is in quandary and whirling in confusion; I am unable to hold myself steady any longer. O Krishna, killer of the Keshi demon, I only see omens of misfortune. I do not foresee how any good can come from killing my own kinsmen in this battle.

verses.slok: वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते || 29||गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चै व परिदह्यते |न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मन: || 30||निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव |न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे || 31||
verses.transliteration: vepathuśh cha śharīre me roma-harṣhaśh cha jāyategāṇḍīvaṁ sraṁsate hastāt tvak chaiva paridahyatena cha śhaknomy avasthātuṁ bhramatīva cha me manaḥnimittāni cha paśhyāmi viparītāni keśhavana cha śhreyo ’nupaśhyāmi hatvā sva-janam āhave
verses.translation: BG 1.29-31:My whole body shudders; my hair is standing on end. My bow, the Gāṇḍīv, is slipping from my hand, and my skin is burning all over. My mind is in quandary and whirling in confusion; I am unable to hold myself steady any longer. O Krishna, killer of the Keshi demon, I only see omens of misfortune. I do not foresee how any good can come from killing my own kinsmen in this battle.

verses.slok: न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च |किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा || 32||येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगा: सुखानि च |त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च || 33||
verses.transliteration: na kāṅkṣhe vijayaṁ kṛiṣhṇa na cha rājyaṁ sukhāni chakiṁ no rājyena govinda kiṁ bhogair jīvitena vāyeṣhām arthe kāṅkṣhitaṁ no rājyaṁ bhogāḥ sukhāni chata ime ’vasthitā yuddhe prāṇāṁs tyaktvā dhanāni cha
verses.translation: BG 1.32-33:O Krishna, I do not desire victory, kingdom, or the happiness accruing to it. Of what avail will be a kingdom, pleasures, or even life itself, when the very persons for whom we covet them, are standing before us for battle?

verses.slok: न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च |किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा || 32||येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगा: सुखानि च |त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च || 33||
verses.transliteration: na kāṅkṣhe vijayaṁ kṛiṣhṇa na cha rājyaṁ sukhāni chakiṁ no rājyena govinda kiṁ bhogair jīvitena vāyeṣhām arthe kāṅkṣhitaṁ no rājyaṁ bhogāḥ sukhāni chata ime ’vasthitā yuddhe prāṇāṁs tyaktvā dhanāni cha
verses.translation: BG 1.32-33:O Krishna, I do not desire victory, kingdom, or the happiness accruing to it. Of what avail will be a kingdom, pleasures, or even life itself, when the very persons for whom we covet them, are standing before us for battle?

verses.slok: आचार्या: पितर: पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहा: |मातुला: श्वशुरा: पौत्रा: श्याला: सम्बन्धिनस्तथा || 34||एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन |अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतो: किं नु महीकृते || 35||
verses.transliteration: āchāryāḥ pitaraḥ putrās tathaiva cha pitāmahāḥmātulāḥ śhvaśhurāḥ pautrāḥ śhyālāḥ sambandhinas tathā
verses.translation: BG 1.34-35:Teachers, fathers, sons, grandfathers, maternal uncles, grandsons, fathers-in-law, grand-nephews, brothers-in-law, and other kinsmen are present here, staking their lives and riches. O Madhusudan, I do not wish to slay them, even if they attack me. If we kill the sons of Dhritarashtra, what satisfaction will we derive from the dominion over the three worlds, what to speak of this Earth?

verses.slok: आचार्या: पितर: पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहा: |मातुला: श्वशुरा: पौत्रा: श्याला: सम्बन्धिनस्तथा || 34||एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन |अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतो: किं नु महीकृते || 35||
verses.transliteration: āchāryāḥ pitaraḥ putrās tathaiva cha pitāmahāḥmātulāḥ śhvaśhurāḥ pautrāḥ śhyālāḥ sambandhinas tathā
verses.translation: BG 1.34-35:Teachers, fathers, sons, grandfathers, maternal uncles, grandsons, fathers-in-law, grand-nephews, brothers-in-law, and other kinsmen are present here, staking their lives and riches. O Madhusudan, I do not wish to slay them, even if they attack me. If we kill the sons of Dhritarashtra, what satisfaction will we derive from the dominion over the three worlds, what to speak of this Earth?

verses.slok: निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्न: का प्रीति: स्याज्जनार्दन |पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिन: || 36 ||तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान् |स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिन: स्याम माधव || 37||
verses.transliteration: nihatya dhārtarāṣhṭrān naḥ kā prītiḥ syāj janārdanapāpam evāśhrayed asmān hatvaitān ātatāyinaḥtasmān nārhā vayaṁ hantuṁ dhārtarāṣhṭrān sa-bāndhavānsva-janaṁ hi kathaṁ hatvā sukhinaḥ syāma mādhava
verses.translation: BG 1.36-37:O Maintainer of all living entities, what pleasure will we derive from killing the sons of Dhritarasthra? Even though they may be aggressors, sin will certainly come upon us if we slay them. Hence, it does not behoove us to kill our own cousins, the sons of Dhritarashtra, and friends. O Madhav (Krishna), how can we hope to be happy by killing our own kinsmen?

verses.slok: निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्न: का प्रीति: स्याज्जनार्दन |पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिन: || 36 ||तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान् |स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिन: स्याम माधव || 37||
verses.transliteration: nihatya dhārtarāṣhṭrān naḥ kā prītiḥ syāj janārdanapāpam evāśhrayed asmān hatvaitān ātatāyinaḥtasmān nārhā vayaṁ hantuṁ dhārtarāṣhṭrān sa-bāndhavānsva-janaṁ hi kathaṁ hatvā sukhinaḥ syāma mādhava
verses.translation: BG 1.36-37:O Maintainer of all living entities, what pleasure will we derive from killing the sons of Dhritarasthra? Even though they may be aggressors, sin will certainly come upon us if we slay them. Hence, it does not behoove us to kill our own cousins, the sons of Dhritarashtra, and friends. O Madhav (Krishna), how can we hope to be happy by killing our own kinsmen?

verses.slok: यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतस: |कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् || 38||कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभि: पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम् |कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन || 39||
verses.transliteration: yady apy ete na paśhyanti lobhopahata-chetasaḥkula-kṣhaya-kṛitaṁ doṣhaṁ mitra-drohe cha pātakamkathaṁ na jñeyam asmābhiḥ pāpād asmān nivartitumkula-kṣhaya-kṛitaṁ doṣhaṁ prapaśhyadbhir janārdana
verses.translation: BG 1.38-39:Their thoughts are overpowered by greed and they see no wrong in annihilating their relatives or wreaking treachery upon friends. Yet, O Janardan (Krishna), why should we, who can clearly see the crime in killing our kindred, not turn away from this sin?

verses.slok: यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतस: |कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् || 38||कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभि: पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम् |कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन || 39||
verses.transliteration: yady apy ete na paśhyanti lobhopahata-chetasaḥkula-kṣhaya-kṛitaṁ doṣhaṁ mitra-drohe cha pātakamkathaṁ na jñeyam asmābhiḥ pāpād asmān nivartitumkula-kṣhaya-kṛitaṁ doṣhaṁ prapaśhyadbhir janārdana
verses.translation: BG 1.38-39:Their thoughts are overpowered by greed and they see no wrong in annihilating their relatives or wreaking treachery upon friends. Yet, O Janardan (Krishna), why should we, who can clearly see the crime in killing our kindred, not turn away from this sin?

verses.slok: कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्मा: सनातना: |धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत || 40||
verses.transliteration: kula-kṣhaye praṇaśhyanti kula-dharmāḥ sanātanāḥdharme naṣhṭe kulaṁ kṛitsnam adharmo ’bhibhavaty uta
verses.translation: BG 1.40:When a dynasty is destroyed, its traditions get vanquished, and the rest of the family becomes involved in irreligion.

verses.slok: अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रिय: |स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्कर: || 41||
verses.transliteration: adharmābhibhavāt kṛiṣhṇa praduṣhyanti kula-striyaḥstrīṣhu duṣhṭāsu vārṣhṇeya jāyate varṇa-saṅkaraḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.41:With the preponderance of vice, O Krishna, the women of the family become immoral; and from the immorality of women, O descendent of Vrishni, unwanted progeny are born.

verses.slok: सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च |पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रिया: || 42||
verses.transliteration: saṅkaro narakāyaiva kula-ghnānāṁ kulasya chapatanti pitaro hy eṣhāṁ lupta-piṇḍodaka-kriyāḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.42:An increase in unwanted children results in hellish life both for the family and for those who destroy the family. Deprived of sacrificial offerings, the ancestors of such corrupt families also fall.

verses.slok: दोषैरेतै: कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकै: |उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्मा: कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वता: || 43||
verses.transliteration: doṣhair etaiḥ kula-ghnānāṁ varṇa-saṅkara-kārakaiḥutsādyante jāti-dharmāḥ kula-dharmāśh cha śhāśhvatāḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.43:Through the evil deeds of those who destroy the family tradition and thus give rise to unwanted progeny, a variety of social and family welfare activities are ruined.

verses.slok: उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन |नरकेऽनियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम || 44||
verses.transliteration: utsanna-kula-dharmāṇāṁ manuṣhyāṇāṁ janārdananarake ‘niyataṁ vāso bhavatītyanuśhuśhruma
verses.translation: BG 1.44:O Janardan (Krishna), I have heard from the learned that those who destroy family traditions dwell in hell for an indefinite period of time.

verses.slok: अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम् |यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यता: || 45||यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणय: |धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् || 46||
verses.transliteration: aho bata mahat pāpaṁ kartuṁ vyavasitā vayamyad rājya-sukha-lobhena hantuṁ sva-janam udyatāḥyadi mām apratīkāram aśhastraṁ śhastra-pāṇayaḥdhārtarāṣhṭrā raṇe hanyus tan me kṣhemataraṁ bhavet
verses.translation: BG 1.45-46:Alas! How strange it is that we have set our mind to perform this great sin with horrifying consequences. Driven by the desire for kingly pleasures, we are intent on killing our own kinsmen. It would be better if, with weapons in hand, the sons of Dhritarashtra kill me unarmed and unresisting on the battlefield.

verses.slok: अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम् |यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यता: || 45||यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणय: |धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् || 46||
verses.transliteration: aho bata mahat pāpaṁ kartuṁ vyavasitā vayamyad rājya-sukha-lobhena hantuṁ sva-janam udyatāḥyadi mām apratīkāram aśhastraṁ śhastra-pāṇayaḥdhārtarāṣhṭrā raṇe hanyus tan me kṣhemataraṁ bhavet
verses.translation: BG 1.45-46:Alas! How strange it is that we have set our mind to perform this great sin with horrifying consequences. Driven by the desire for kingly pleasures, we are intent on killing our own kinsmen. It would be better if, with weapons in hand, the sons of Dhritarashtra kill me unarmed and unresisting on the battlefield.

verses.slok: सञ्जय उवाच |एवमुक्त्वार्जुन: सङ्ख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् |विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानस: || 47||
verses.transliteration: sañjaya uvāchaevam uktvārjunaḥ saṅkhye rathopastha upāviśhatvisṛijya sa-śharaṁ chāpaṁ śhoka-saṁvigna-mānasaḥ
verses.translation: BG 1.47:Sanjay said: Speaking thus, Arjun cast aside his bow and arrows, and sank into the seat of his chariot, his mind in distress and overwhelmed with grief.

verses.slok: सञ्जय उवाच |तं तथा कृपयाविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम् |विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदन: || 1||
verses.transliteration: sañjaya uvāchataṁ tathā kṛipayāviṣhṭamaśhru pūrṇākulekṣhaṇamviṣhīdantamidaṁ vākyam uvācha madhusūdanaḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.1:Sanjay said: Seeing Arjun overwhelmed with pity, his mind grief-stricken, and his eyes full of tears, Shree Krishna spoke the following words.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् |अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन || 2||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchakutastvā kaśhmalamidaṁ viṣhame samupasthitamanārya-juṣhṭamaswargyam akīrti-karam arjuna
verses.translation: BG 2.2:The Supreme Lord said: My dear Arjun, how has this delusion overcome you in this hour of peril?  It is not befitting an honorable person.  It leads not to the higher abodes, but to disgrace.

verses.slok: क्लैब्यं मा स्म गम: पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते |क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप || 3||
verses.transliteration: klaibyaṁ mā sma gamaḥ pārtha naitat tvayyupapadyatekṣhudraṁ hṛidaya-daurbalyaṁ tyaktvottiṣhṭha parantapa
verses.translation: BG 2.3:O Parth, it does not befit you to yield to this unmanliness.  Give up such petty weakness of heart and arise, O vanquisher of enemies.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |कथं भीष्ममहं सङ्ख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन |इषुभि: प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन || 4||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchakathaṁ bhīṣhmam ahaṁ sankhye droṇaṁ cha madhusūdanaiṣhubhiḥ pratiyotsyāmi pūjārhāvari-sūdana
verses.translation: BG 2.4:Arjun said: O Madhusudan, how can I shoot arrows in battle on men like Bheeshma and Dronacharya, who are worthy of my worship, O destroyer of enemies?

verses.slok: गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान्श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके |हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैवभुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् || 5||
verses.transliteration: gurūnahatvā hi mahānubhāvānśhreyo bhoktuṁ bhaikṣhyamapīha lokehatvārtha-kāmāṁstu gurūnihaivabhuñjīya bhogān rudhira-pradigdhān
verses.translation: BG 2.5:It would be better to live in this world by begging, than to enjoy life by killing these noble elders, who are my teachers. If we kill them, the wealth and pleasures we enjoy will be tainted with blood.

verses.slok: न चैतद्विद्म: कतरन्नो गरीयोयद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयु: |यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषामस्तेऽवस्थिता: प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्रा: || 6||
verses.transliteration: na chaitadvidmaḥ kataranno garīyoyadvā jayema yadi vā no jayeyuḥyāneva hatvā na jijīviṣhāmaste ’vasthitāḥ pramukhe dhārtarāṣhṭrāḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.6:We do not even know which result of this war is preferable for us—conquering them or being conquered by them. Even after killing them we will not desire to live. Yet they have taken the side of the sons of Dhritarasthra, and now stand before us on the battlefield.

verses.slok: कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभाव:पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसम्मूढचेता: |यच्छ्रेय: स्यान्निश्चितं ब्रूहि तन्मेशिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम् || 7||
verses.transliteration: kārpaṇya-doṣhopahata-svabhāvaḥpṛichchhāmi tvāṁ dharma-sammūḍha-chetāḥyach-chhreyaḥ syānniśhchitaṁ brūhi tanmeśhiṣhyaste ’haṁ śhādhi māṁ tvāṁ prapannam
verses.translation: BG 2.7:I am confused about my duty, and am besieged with anxiety and faintheartedness. I am Your disciple, and am surrendered to You. Please instruct me for certain what is best for me.

verses.slok: न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्याद्यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम् |अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धंराज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम् || 8||
verses.transliteration: na hi prapaśhyāmi mamāpanudyādyach-chhokam uchchhoṣhaṇam-indriyāṇāmavāpya bhūmāv-asapatnamṛiddhaṁrājyaṁ surāṇāmapi chādhipatyam
verses.translation: BG 2.8:I can find no means of driving away this anguish that is drying up my senses. Even if I win a prosperous and unrivalled kingdom on the earth, or gain sovereignty like the celestial gods, I will be unable to dispel this grief.

verses.slok: सञ्जय उवाच |एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेश: परन्तप |न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह || 9||
verses.transliteration: sañjaya uvāchaevam-uktvā hṛiṣhīkeśhaṁ guḍākeśhaḥ parantapana yotsya iti govindam uktvā tūṣhṇīṁ babhūva ha
verses.translation: BG 2.9:Sanjay said: Having thus spoken, Gudakesh, that chastiser of enemies, addressed Hrishikesh: “Govind, I shall not fight,” and became silent.

verses.slok: तमुवाच हृषीकेश: प्रहसन्निव भारत |सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वच: || 10||
verses.transliteration: tam-uvācha hṛiṣhīkeśhaḥ prahasanniva bhāratasenayorubhayor-madhye viṣhīdantam-idaṁ vachaḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.10:O Dhritarashtra, thereafter, in the midst of both the armies, Shree Krishna smilingly spoke the following words to the grief-stricken Arjun.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे |गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिता: || 11||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchaaśhochyān-anvaśhochas-tvaṁ prajñā-vādānśh cha bhāṣhasegatāsūn-agatāsūnśh-cha nānuśhochanti paṇḍitāḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.11:The Supreme Lord said: While you speak words of wisdom, you are mourning for that which is not worthy of grief. The wise lament neither for the living nor for the dead.

verses.slok: न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः |न चैव न भविष्याम: सर्वे वयमत: परम् || 12||
verses.transliteration: na tvevāhaṁ jātu nāsaṁ na tvaṁ neme janādhipāḥna chaiva na bhaviṣhyāmaḥ sarve vayamataḥ param
verses.translation: BG 2.12:Never was there a time when I did not exist, nor you, nor all these kings; nor in the future shall any of us cease to be.

verses.slok: देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा |तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति || 13||
verses.transliteration: dehino ’smin yathā dehe kaumāraṁ yauvanaṁ jarātathā dehāntara-prāptir dhīras tatra na muhyati
verses.translation: BG 2.13:Just as the embodied soul continuously passes from childhood to youth to old age, similarly, at the time of death, the soul passes into another body. The wise are not deluded by this.

verses.slok: मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदु:खदा: |आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत || 14||
verses.transliteration: mātrā-sparśhās tu kaunteya śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥāgamāpāyino ’nityās tāns-titikṣhasva bhārata
verses.translation: BG 2.14:O son of Kunti, the contact between the senses and the sense objects gives rise to fleeting perceptions of happiness and distress. These are non-permanent, and come and go like the winter and summer seasons. O descendent of Bharat, one must learn to tolerate them without being disturbed.

verses.slok: यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ |समदु:खसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते || 15||
verses.transliteration: yaṁ hi na vyathayantyete puruṣhaṁ puruṣharṣhabhasama-duḥkha-sukhaṁ dhīraṁ so ’mṛitatvāya kalpate
verses.translation: BG 2.15:O Arjun, noblest amongst men, that person who is not affected by happiness and distress, and remains steady in both, becomes eligible for liberation.

verses.slok: नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सत: |उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभि: || 16||
verses.transliteration: nāsato vidyate bhāvo nābhāvo vidyate sataḥubhayorapi dṛiṣhṭo ’nta stvanayos tattva-darśhibhiḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.16:Of the transient there is no endurance, and of the eternal there is no cessation. This has verily been observed and concluded by the seers of the Truth, after studying the nature of both.

verses.slok: अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् |विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित्कर्तुमर्हति || 17||
verses.transliteration: avināśhi tu tadviddhi yena sarvam idaṁ tatamvināśham avyayasyāsya na kaśhchit kartum arhati
verses.translation: BG 2.17:That which pervades the entire body, know it to be indestructible. No one can cause the destruction of the imperishable soul.

verses.slok: अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ता: शरीरिण: |अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत || 18||
verses.transliteration: antavanta ime dehā nityasyoktāḥ śharīriṇaḥanāśhino ’prameyasya tasmād yudhyasva bhārata
verses.translation: BG 2.18:Only the material body is perishable; the embodied soul within is indestructible, immeasurable, and eternal. Therefore, fight, O descendent of Bharat.

verses.slok: य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम् |उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते || 19||
verses.transliteration: ya enaṁ vetti hantāraṁ yaśh chainaṁ manyate hatamubhau tau na vijānīto nāyaṁ hanti na hanyate
verses.translation: BG 2.19:Neither of them is in knowledge—the one who thinks the soul can slay and the one who thinks the soul can be slain. For truly, the soul neither kills nor can it be killed.

verses.slok: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिनायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूय: |अजो नित्य: शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणोन हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे || 20||
verses.transliteration: na jāyate mriyate vā kadāchinnāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥajo nityaḥ śhāśhvato ’yaṁ purāṇona hanyate hanyamāne śharīre
verses.translation: BG 2.20:The soul is neither born, nor does it ever die; nor having once existed, does it ever cease to be. The soul is without birth, eternal, immortal, and ageless. It is not destroyed when the body is destroyed.

verses.slok: वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम् |कथं स पुरुष: पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् || 21||
verses.transliteration: vedāvināśhinaṁ nityaṁ ya enam ajam avyayamkathaṁ sa puruṣhaḥ pārtha kaṁ ghātayati hanti kam
verses.translation: BG 2.21:O Parth, how can one who knows the soul to be imperishable, eternal, unborn, and immutable kill anyone or cause anyone to kill?

verses.slok: वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहायनवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि |तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णान्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही || 22||
verses.transliteration: vāsānsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāyanavāni gṛihṇāti naro ’parāṇitathā śharīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇānyanyāni sanyāti navāni dehī
verses.translation: BG 2.22:As a person sheds worn-out garments and wears new ones, likewise, at the time of death, the soul casts off its worn-out body and enters a new one.

verses.slok: नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावक: |न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुत: || 23||
verses.transliteration: nainaṁ chhindanti śhastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥna chainaṁ kledayantyāpo na śhoṣhayati mārutaḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.23:Weapons cannot shred the soul, nor can fire burn it. Water cannot wet it, nor can the wind dry it.

verses.slok: अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च |नित्य: सर्वगत: स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातन: || 24||
verses.transliteration: achchhedyo ’yam adāhyo ’yam akledyo ’śhoṣhya eva chanityaḥ sarva-gataḥ sthāṇur achalo ’yaṁ sanātanaḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.24:The soul is unbreakable and incombustible; it can neither be dampened nor dried. It is everlasting, in all places, unalterable, immutable, and primordial.

verses.slok: अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते |तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि || 25||
verses.transliteration: avyakto ’yam achintyo ’yam avikāryo ’yam uchyatetasmādevaṁ viditvainaṁ nānuśhochitum arhasi
verses.translation: BG 2.25:The soul is spoken of as invisible, inconceivable, and unchangeable. Knowing this, you should not grieve for the body.

verses.slok: अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम् |तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि || 26||
verses.transliteration: atha chainaṁ nitya-jātaṁ nityaṁ vā manyase mṛitamtathāpi tvaṁ mahā-bāho naivaṁ śhochitum arhasi
verses.translation: BG 2.26:If, however, you think that the self is subject to constant birth and death, O mighty-armed Arjun, even then you should not grieve like this.

verses.slok: जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च |तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि || 27||
verses.transliteration: jātasya hi dhruvo mṛityur dhruvaṁ janma mṛitasya chatasmād aparihārye ’rthe na tvaṁ śhochitum arhasi
verses.translation: BG 2.27:Death is certain for one who has been born, and rebirth is inevitable for one who has died. Therefore, you should not lament over the inevitable.

verses.slok: अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत |अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना || 28||
verses.transliteration: avyaktādīni bhūtāni vyakta-madhyāni bhārataavyakta-nidhanānyeva tatra kā paridevanā
verses.translation: BG 2.28:O scion of Bharat, all created beings are unmanifest before birth, manifest in life, and again unmanifest on death. So why grieve?

verses.slok: आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेनमाश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्य: |आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्य: शृ्णोतिश्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित् || 29||
verses.transliteration: āśhcharya-vat paśhyati kaśhchid enanāśhcharya-vad vadati tathaiva chānyaḥāśhcharya-vach chainam anyaḥ śhṛiṇotiśhrutvāpyenaṁ veda na chaiva kaśhchit
verses.translation: BG 2.29:Some see the soul as amazing, some describe it as amazing, and some hear of the soul as amazing, while others, even on hearing, cannot understand it at all.

verses.slok: देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत |तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि || 30||
verses.transliteration: dehī nityam avadhyo ’yaṁ dehe sarvasya bhāratatasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni na tvaṁ śhochitum arhasi
verses.translation: BG 2.30:O Arjun, the soul that dwells within the body is immortal; therefore, you should not mourn for anyone.

verses.slok: स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि |धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते || 31||
verses.transliteration: swa-dharmam api chāvekṣhya na vikampitum arhasidharmyāddhi yuddhāch chhreyo ’nyat kṣhatriyasya na vidyate
verses.translation: BG 2.31:Besides, considering your duty as a warrior, you should not waver. Indeed, for a warrior, there is no better engagement than fighting for upholding of righteousness.

verses.slok: यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम् |सुखिन: क्षत्रिया: पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम् || 32||
verses.transliteration: yadṛichchhayā chopapannaṁ swarga-dvāram apāvṛitamsukhinaḥ kṣhatriyāḥ pārtha labhante yuddham īdṛiśham
verses.translation: BG 2.32:O Parth, happy are the warriors to whom such opportunities to defend righteousness come unsought, opening for them the stairway to the celestial abodes.

verses.slok: अथ चेतत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं संग्रामं न करिष्यसि |तत: स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि || 33||
verses.transliteration: atha chet tvam imaṁ dharmyaṁ saṅgrāmaṁ na kariṣhyasitataḥ sva-dharmaṁ kīrtiṁ cha hitvā pāpam avāpsyasi
verses.translation: BG 2.33:If, however, you refuse to fight this righteous war, abandoning your social duty and reputation, you will certainly incur sin.

verses.slok: अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम् |सम्भावितस्य चाकीर्ति र्मरणादतिरिच्यते || 34||
verses.transliteration: akīrtiṁ chāpi bhūtāni kathayiṣhyanti te ’vyayāmsambhāvitasya chākīrtir maraṇād atirichyate
verses.translation: BG 2.34:People will speak of you as a coward and a deserter. For a respectable person, infamy is worse than death.

verses.slok: भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथा: |येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम् || 35||
verses.transliteration: bhayād raṇād uparataṁ mansyante tvāṁ mahā-rathāḥyeṣhāṁ cha tvaṁ bahu-mato bhūtvā yāsyasi lāghavam
verses.translation: BG 2.35:The great generals who hold you in high esteem will think that you fled from the battlefield out of fear, and thus will lose their respect for you.

verses.slok: अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून्वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिता: |निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दु:खतरं नु किम् || 36||
verses.transliteration: avāchya-vādānśh cha bahūn vadiṣhyanti tavāhitāḥnindantastava sāmarthyaṁ tato duḥkhataraṁ nu kim
verses.translation: BG 2.36:Your enemies will defame and humiliate you with unkind words, disparaging your might. Alas, what could be more painful than that?

verses.slok: हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम् |तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चय: || 37||
verses.transliteration: hato vā prāpsyasi swargaṁ jitvā vā bhokṣhyase mahīmtasmād uttiṣhṭha kaunteya yuddhāya kṛita-niśhchayaḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.37:If you fight, you will either be slain on the battlefield and go to the celestial abodes, or you will gain victory and enjoy the kingdom on earth. Therefore arise with determination, O son of Kunti, and be prepared to fight.

verses.slok: सुखदु:खे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ |ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि || 38||
verses.transliteration: sukha-duḥkhe same kṛitvā lābhālābhau jayājayautato yuddhāya yujyasva naivaṁ pāpam avāpsyasi
verses.translation: BG 2.38:Fight for the sake of duty, treating alike happiness and distress, loss and gain, victory and defeat. Fulfilling your responsibility in this way, you will never incur sin.

verses.slok: एषा तेऽभिहिता साङ्ख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां शृणु |बुद्ध्या युक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि || 39||
verses.transliteration: eṣhā te ’bhihitā sānkhye buddhir yoge tvimāṁ śhṛiṇubuddhyā yukto yayā pārtha karma-bandhaṁ prahāsyasi
verses.translation: BG 2.39:Hitherto, I have explained to you Sānkhya Yog, or analytic knowledge regarding the nature of the soul. Now listen, O Parth, as I reveal Buddhi Yog, or the Yog of Intellect. When you work with such understanding, you will be freed from the bondage of karma.

verses.slok: नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते |स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात् || 40||
verses.transliteration: nehābhikrama-nāśho ’sti pratyavāyo na vidyatesvalpam apyasya dharmasya trāyate mahato bhayāt
verses.translation: BG 2.40:Working in this state of consciousness, there is no loss or adverse result, and even a little effort saves one from great danger.

verses.slok: व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन |बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम् || 41||
verses.transliteration: vyavasāyātmikā buddhir ekeha kuru-nandanabahu-śhākhā hyanantāśh cha buddhayo ’vyavasāyinām
verses.translation: BG 2.41:O descendent of the Kurus, the intellect of those who are on this path is resolute, and their aim is one-pointed. But the intellect of those who are irresolute is many-branched.

verses.slok: यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चित: |वेदवादरता: पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिन: || 42||कामात्मान: स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम् |क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति || 43||
verses.transliteration: yāmimāṁ puṣhpitāṁ vāchaṁ pravadanty-avipaśhchitaḥveda-vāda-ratāḥ pārtha nānyad astīti vādinaḥkāmātmānaḥ swarga-parā janma-karma-phala-pradāmkriyā-viśheṣha-bahulāṁ bhogaiśhwarya-gatiṁ prati
verses.translation: BG 2.42-43:Those with limited understanding, get attracted to the flowery words of the Vedas, which advocate ostentatious rituals for elevation to the celestial abodes, and presume no higher principle is described in them. They glorify only those portions of the Vedas that please their senses, and perform pompous ritualistic ceremonies for attaining high birth, opulence, sensual enjoyment, and elevation to the heavenly planets.

verses.slok: यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चित: |वेदवादरता: पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिन: || 42||कामात्मान: स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम् |क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति || 43||
verses.transliteration: yāmimāṁ puṣhpitāṁ vāchaṁ pravadanty-avipaśhchitaḥveda-vāda-ratāḥ pārtha nānyad astīti vādinaḥkāmātmānaḥ swarga-parā janma-karma-phala-pradāmkriyā-viśheṣha-bahulāṁ bhogaiśhwarya-gatiṁ prati
verses.translation: BG 2.42-43:Those with limited understanding, get attracted to the flowery words of the Vedas, which advocate ostentatious rituals for elevation to the celestial abodes, and presume no higher principle is described in them. They glorify only those portions of the Vedas that please their senses, and perform pompous ritualistic ceremonies for attaining high birth, opulence, sensual enjoyment, and elevation to the heavenly planets.

verses.slok: भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम् |व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धि: समाधौ न विधीयते || 44||
verses.transliteration: bhogaiśwvarya-prasaktānāṁ tayāpahṛita-chetasāmvyavasāyātmikā buddhiḥ samādhau na vidhīyate
verses.translation: BG 2.44:With their minds deeply attached to worldly pleasures and their intellects bewildered by such things, they are unable to possess the resolute determination for success on the path to God.

verses.slok: त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन |निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् || 45||
verses.transliteration: trai-guṇya-viṣhayā vedā nistrai-guṇyo bhavārjunanirdvandvo nitya-sattva-stho niryoga-kṣhema ātmavān
verses.translation: BG 2.45:The Vedas deal with the three modes of material nature, O Arjun. Rise above the three modes to a state of pure spiritual consciousness. Freeing yourself from dualities, eternally fixed in Truth, and without concern for material gain and safety, be situated in the self.

verses.slok: यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वत: सम्प्लुतोदके |तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानत: || 46||
verses.transliteration: yāvān artha udapāne sarvataḥ samplutodaketāvānsarveṣhu vedeṣhu brāhmaṇasya vijānataḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.46:Whatever purpose is served by a small well of water is naturally served in all respects by a large lake. Similarly, one who realizes the Absolute Truth also fulfills the purpose of all the Vedas.

verses.slok: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन |मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि || 47 ||
verses.transliteration: karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣhu kadāchanamā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stvakarmaṇi
verses.translation: BG 2.47:You have a right to perform your prescribed duties, but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions. Never consider yourself to be the cause of the results of your activities, nor be attached to inaction.

verses.slok: योगस्थ: कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय |सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्यो: समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते || 48||
verses.transliteration: yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjayasiddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga uchyate
verses.translation: BG 2.48:Be steadfast in the performance of your duty, O Arjun, abandoning attachment to success and failure. Such equanimity is called Yog.

verses.slok: दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय |बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणा: फलहेतव: || 49||
verses.transliteration: dūreṇa hy-avaraṁ karma buddhi-yogād dhanañjayabuddhau śharaṇam anvichchha kṛipaṇāḥ phala-hetavaḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.49:Seek refuge in divine knowledge and insight, O Arjun, and discard reward-seeking actions that are certainly inferior to works performed with the intellect established in divine knowledge. Miserly are those who seek to enjoy the fruits of their works.

verses.slok: बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते |तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योग: कर्मसु कौशलम् || 50||
verses.transliteration: buddhi-yukto jahātīha ubhe sukṛita-duṣhkṛitetasmād yogāya yujyasva yogaḥ karmasu kauśhalam
verses.translation: BG 2.50:One who prudently practices the science of work without attachment can get rid of both good and bad reactions in this life itself. Therefore, strive for Yog, which is the art of working skillfully (in proper consciousness).

verses.slok: कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिण: |जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ता: पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् || 51||
verses.transliteration: karma-jaṁ buddhi-yuktā hi phalaṁ tyaktvā manīṣhiṇaḥjanma-bandha-vinirmuktāḥ padaṁ gachchhanty-anāmayam
verses.translation: BG 2.51:The wise endowed with equanimity of intellect, abandon attachment to the fruits of actions, which bind one to the cycle of life and death. By working in such consciousness, they attain the state beyond all suffering.

verses.slok: यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति |तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च || 52||
verses.transliteration: yadā te moha-kalilaṁ buddhir vyatitariṣhyatitadā gantāsi nirvedaṁ śhrotavyasya śhrutasya cha
verses.translation: BG 2.52:When your intellect crosses the quagmire of delusion, you will then acquire indifference to what has been heard and what is yet to be heard (about enjoyments in this world and the next).

verses.slok: श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला |समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि || 53||
verses.transliteration: śhruti-vipratipannā te yadā sthāsyati niśhchalāsamādhāv-achalā buddhis tadā yogam avāpsyasi
verses.translation: BG 2.53:When your intellect ceases to be allured by the fruitive sections of the Vedas and remains steadfast in divine consciousness, you will then attain the state of perfect Yog.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव |स्थितधी: किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् || 54||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchasthita-prajñasya kā bhāṣhā samādhi-sthasya keśhavasthita-dhīḥ kiṁ prabhāṣheta kim āsīta vrajeta kim
verses.translation: BG 2.54:Arjun said : O Keshav, what is the disposition of one who is situated in divine consciousness? How does an enlightened person talk? How does he sit? How does he walk?

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |प्रजहाति यदा कामान्सर्वान्पार्थ मनोगतान् |आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्ट: स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते || 55||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchaprajahāti yadā kāmān sarvān pārtha mano-gatānātmany-evātmanā tuṣhṭaḥ sthita-prajñas tadochyate
verses.translation: BG 2.55:The Supreme Lord said: O Parth, when one discards all selfish desires and cravings of the senses that torment the mind, and becomes satisfied in the realization of the self, such a person is said to be transcendentally situated.

verses.slok: दु:खेष्वनुद्विग्नमना: सुखेषु विगतस्पृह: |वीतरागभयक्रोध: स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते || 56||
verses.transliteration: duḥkheṣhv-anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣhu vigata-spṛihaḥvīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthita-dhīr munir uchyate
verses.translation: BG 2.56:One whose mind remains undisturbed amidst misery, who does not crave for pleasure, and who is free from attachment, fear, and anger, is called a sage of steady wisdom.

verses.slok: य: सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम् |नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता || 57||
verses.transliteration: yaḥ sarvatrānabhisnehas tat tat prāpya śhubhāśhubhamnābhinandati na dveṣhṭi tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā
verses.translation: BG 2.57:One who remains unattached under all conditions, and is neither delighted by good fortune nor dejected by tribulation, he is a sage with perfect knowledge.

verses.slok: यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वश: |इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता || 58||
verses.transliteration: yadā sanharate chāyaṁ kūrmo ’ṅgānīva sarvaśhaḥindriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā
verses.translation: BG 2.58:One who is able to withdraw the senses from their objects, just as a tortoise withdraws its limbs into its shell, is established in divine wisdom.

verses.slok: विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिन: |रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते || 59||
verses.transliteration: viṣhayā vinivartante nirāhārasya dehinaḥrasa-varjaṁ raso ’pyasya paraṁ dṛiṣhṭvā nivartate
verses.translation: BG 2.59:Aspirants may restrain the senses from their objects of enjoyment, but the taste for the sense objects remains. However, even this taste ceases for those who realize the Supreme.

verses.slok: यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चित: |इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मन: || 60||
verses.transliteration: yatato hyapi kaunteya puruṣhasya vipaśhchitaḥindriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.60:The senses are so strong and turbulent, O son of Kunti, that they can forcibly carry away the mind even of a person endowed with discrimination who practices self-control.

verses.slok: तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्पर: |वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता || 61||
verses.transliteration: tāni sarvāṇi sanyamya yukta āsīta mat-paraḥvaśhe hi yasyendriyāṇi tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā
verses.translation: BG 2.61:They are established in perfect knowledge, who subdue their senses and keep their minds ever absorbed in Me.

verses.slok: ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंस: सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते |सङ्गात्सञ्जायते काम: कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते || 62||
verses.transliteration: dhyāyato viṣhayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣhūpajāyatesaṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate
verses.translation: BG 2.62:While contemplating on the objects of the senses, one develops attachment to them. Attachment leads to desire, and from desire arises anger.

verses.slok: क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोह: सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रम: |स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति || 63||
verses.transliteration: krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ sammohāt smṛiti-vibhramaḥsmṛiti-bhranśhād buddhi-nāśho buddhi-nāśhāt praṇaśhyati
verses.translation: BG 2.63:Anger leads to clouding of judgment, which results in bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, the intellect gets destroyed; and when the intellect is destroyed, one is ruined.

verses.slok: रागद्वेषवियुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन् |आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति || 64||
verses.transliteration: rāga-dveṣha-viyuktais tu viṣhayān indriyaiśh charanātma-vaśhyair-vidheyātmā prasādam adhigachchhati
verses.translation: BG 2.64:But one who controls the mind, and is free from attachment and aversion, even while using the objects of the senses, attains the Grace of God.

verses.slok: प्रसादे सर्वदु:खानां हानिरस्योपजायते |प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धि: पर्यवतिष्ठते || 65||
verses.transliteration: prasāde sarva-duḥkhānāṁ hānir asyopajāyateprasanna-chetaso hyāśhu buddhiḥ paryavatiṣhṭhate
verses.translation: BG 2.65:By divine grace comes the peace in which all sorrows end, and the intellect of such a person of tranquil mind soon becomes firmly established in God.

verses.slok: नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना |न चाभावयत: शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुत: सुखम् || 66||
verses.transliteration: nāsti buddhir-ayuktasya na chāyuktasya bhāvanāna chābhāvayataḥ śhāntir aśhāntasya kutaḥ sukham
verses.translation: BG 2.66:But an undisciplined person, who has not controlled the mind and senses, can neither have a resolute intellect nor steady contemplation on God. For one who never unites the mind with God there is no peace; and how can one who lacks peace be happy?

verses.slok: इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते |तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि || 67||
verses.transliteration: indriyāṇāṁ hi charatāṁ yan mano ’nuvidhīyatetadasya harati prajñāṁ vāyur nāvam ivāmbhasi
verses.translation: BG 2.67:Just as a strong wind sweeps a boat off its chartered course on the water, even one of the senses on which the mind focuses can lead the intellect astray.

verses.slok: तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वश: |इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता || 68||
verses.transliteration: tasmād yasya mahā-bāho nigṛihītāni sarvaśhaḥindriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā
verses.translation: BG 2.68:Therefore, one who has restrained the senses from their objects, O mighty armed Arjun, is firmly established in transcendental knowledge.

verses.slok: या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी |यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुने: || 69||
verses.transliteration: yā niśhā sarva-bhūtānāṁ tasyāṁ jāgarti sanyamīyasyāṁ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśhā paśhyato muneḥ
verses.translation: BG 2.69:What all beings consider as day is the night of ignorance for the wise, and what all creatures see as night is the day for the introspective sage.

verses.slok: आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठंसमुद्रमाप: प्रविशन्ति यद्वत् |तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वेस शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी || 70||
verses.transliteration: āpūryamāṇam achala-pratiṣhṭhaṁsamudram āpaḥ praviśhanti yadvattadvat kāmā yaṁ praviśhanti sarvesa śhāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī
verses.translation: BG 2.70:Just as the ocean remains undisturbed by the incessant flow of waters from rivers merging into it, likewise the sage who is unmoved despite the flow of desirable objects all around him attains peace, and not the person who strives to satisfy desires.

verses.slok: विहाय कामान्य: सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति नि:स्पृह: |निर्ममो निरहङ्कार: स शान्तिमधिगच्छति || 71||
verses.transliteration: vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumānśh charati niḥspṛihaḥnirmamo nirahankāraḥ sa śhāntim adhigachchhati
verses.translation: BG 2.71:That person, who gives up all material desires and lives free from a sense of greed, proprietorship, and egoism, attains perfect peace.

verses.slok: एषा ब्राह्मी स्थिति: पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति |स्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति || 72||
verses.transliteration: eṣhā brāhmī sthitiḥ pārtha naināṁ prāpya vimuhyatisthitvāsyām anta-kāle ’pi brahma-nirvāṇam ṛichchhati
verses.translation: BG 2.72:O Parth, such is the state of an enlightened soul that having attained it, one is never again deluded. Being established in this consciousness even at the hour of death, one is liberated from the cycle of life and death and reaches the Supreme Abode of God.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन |तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव || 1||व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे |तदेकं वद निश्चित्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम् || 2||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchajyāyasī chet karmaṇas te matā buddhir janārdanatat kiṁ karmaṇi ghore māṁ niyojayasi keśhavavyāmiśhreṇeva vākyena buddhiṁ mohayasīva metad ekaṁ vada niśhchitya yena śhreyo ’ham āpnuyām
verses.translation: BG 3.1-2:Arjun said: O Janardan, if You consider knowledge superior to action, then why do You ask me to wage this terrible war? My intellect is bewildered by Your ambiguous advice. Please tell me decisively the one path by which I may attain the highest good.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन |तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव || 1||व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे |तदेकं वद निश्चित्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम् || 2||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchajyāyasī chet karmaṇas te matā buddhir janārdanatat kiṁ karmaṇi ghore māṁ niyojayasi keśhavavyāmiśhreṇeva vākyena buddhiṁ mohayasīva metad ekaṁ vada niśhchitya yena śhreyo ’ham āpnuyām
verses.translation: BG 3.1-2:Arjun said: O Janardan, if You consider knowledge superior to action, then why do You ask me to wage this terrible war? My intellect is bewildered by Your ambiguous advice. Please tell me decisively the one path by which I may attain the highest good.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |लोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ |ज्ञानयोगेन साङ्ख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम् || 3||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchaloke ’smin dvi-vidhā niṣhṭhā purā proktā mayānaghajñāna-yogena sāṅkhyānāṁ karma-yogena yoginām
verses.translation: BG 3.3:The Lord said: O sinless one, the two paths leading to enlightenment were previously explained by Me: the path of knowledge, for those inclined toward contemplation, and the path of work for those inclined toward action.

verses.slok: न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते |न च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति || 4||
verses.transliteration: na karmaṇām anārambhān naiṣhkarmyaṁ puruṣho ’śhnutena cha sannyasanād eva siddhiṁ samadhigachchhati
verses.translation: BG 3.4:One cannot achieve freedom from karmic reactions by merely abstaining from work, nor can one attain perfection of knowledge by mere physical renunciation.

verses.slok: न हि कश्चित्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत् |कार्यते ह्यवश: कर्म सर्व: प्रकृतिजैर्गुणै: || 5||
verses.transliteration: na hi kaśhchit kṣhaṇam api jātu tiṣhṭhatyakarma-kṛitkāryate hyavaśhaḥ karma sarvaḥ prakṛiti-jair guṇaiḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.5:There is no one who can remain without action even for a moment. Indeed, all beings are compelled to act by their qualities born of material nature (the threeguṇas).

verses.slok: कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन् |इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचार: स उच्यते || 6||
verses.transliteration: karmendriyāṇi sanyamya ya āste manasā smaranindriyārthān vimūḍhātmā mithyāchāraḥ sa uchyate
verses.translation: BG 3.6:Those who restrain the external organs of action, while continuing to dwell on sense objects in the mind, certainly delude themselves and are to be called hypocrites.

verses.slok: यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन |कर्मेन्द्रियै: कर्मयोगमसक्त: स विशिष्यते || 7||
verses.transliteration: yas tvindriyāṇi manasā niyamyārabhate ’rjunakarmendriyaiḥ karma-yogam asaktaḥ sa viśhiṣhyate
verses.translation: BG 3.7:But those karm yogis who control their knowledge senses with the mind, O Arjun, and engage the working senses in working without attachment, are certainly superior.

verses.slok: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मण: |शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मण: || 8||
verses.transliteration: niyataṁ kuru karma tvaṁ karma jyāyo hyakarmaṇaḥśharīra-yātrāpi cha te na prasiddhyed akarmaṇaḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.8:You should thus perform your prescribed Vedic duties, since action is superior to inaction. By ceasing activity, even your bodily maintenance will not be possible.

verses.slok: यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धन: |तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसङ्ग: समाचर || 9||
verses.transliteration: yajñārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra loko ’yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥtad-arthaṁ karma kaunteya mukta-saṅgaḥ samāchara
verses.translation: BG 3.9:Work must be done as a yajna to the Supreme Lord; otherwise, work causes bondage in this material world. Therefore, O son of Kunti, for the satisfaction of God, perform your prescribed duties, without being attached to the results.

verses.slok: सहयज्ञा: प्रजा: सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापति: |अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक् || 10||
verses.transliteration: saha-yajñāḥ prajāḥ sṛiṣhṭvā purovācha prajāpatiḥanena prasaviṣhyadhvam eṣha vo ’stviṣhṭa-kāma-dhuk
verses.translation: BG 3.10:In the beginning of creation, Brahma created humankind along with duties, and said, “Prosper in the performance of theseyajñas(sacrifices), for they shall bestow upon you all you wish to achieve.”

verses.slok: देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु व: |परस्परं भावयन्त: श्रेय: परमवाप्स्यथ || 11||
verses.transliteration: devān bhāvayatānena te devā bhāvayantu vaḥparasparaṁ bhāvayantaḥ śhreyaḥ param avāpsyatha
verses.translation: BG 3.11:By your sacrifices, the celestial gods will be pleased, and by cooperation between humans and the celestial gods, great prosperity will reign for all.

verses.slok: इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविता: |तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव स: || 12||
verses.transliteration: iṣhṭān bhogān hi vo devā dāsyante yajña-bhāvitāḥtair dattān apradāyaibhyo yo bhuṅkte stena eva saḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.12:The celestial gods, being satisfied by the performance of sacrifice, will grant you all the desired necessities of life. But those who enjoy what is given to them, without making offerings in return, are verily thieves.

verses.slok: यज्ञशिष्टाशिन: सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषै: |भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात् || 13||
verses.transliteration: yajña-śhiṣhṭāśhinaḥ santo muchyante sarva-kilbiṣhaiḥbhuñjate te tvaghaṁ pāpā ye pachantyātma-kāraṇāt
verses.translation: BG 3.13:The spiritually-minded, who eat food that is first offered in sacrifice, are released from all kinds of sin. Others, who cook food for their own enjoyment, verily eat only sin.

verses.slok: अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भव: |यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञ: कर्मसमुद्भव: || 14||
verses.transliteration: annād bhavanti bhūtāni parjanyād anna-sambhavaḥyajñād bhavati parjanyo yajñaḥ karma-samudbhavaḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.14:All living beings subsist on food, and food is produced by rains. Rains come from the performance of sacrifice, and sacrifice is produced by the performance of prescribed duties.

verses.slok: कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम् |तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम् || 15||
verses.transliteration: karma brahmodbhavaṁ viddhi brahmākṣhara-samudbhavamtasmāt sarva-gataṁ brahma nityaṁ yajñe pratiṣhṭhitam
verses.translation: BG 3.15:The duties for human beings are described in the Vedas, and the Vedas are manifested by God Himself. Therefore, the all-pervading Lord is eternally present in acts of sacrifice.

verses.slok: एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह य: |अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति || 16||
verses.transliteration: evaṁ pravartitaṁ chakraṁ nānuvartayatīha yaḥaghāyur indriyārāmo moghaṁ pārtha sa jīvati
verses.translation: BG 3.16:O Parth, those who do not accept their responsibility in the cycle of sacrifice established by the Vedas are sinful. They live only for the delight of their senses; indeed their lives are in vain.

verses.slok: यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानव: |आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते || 17||
verses.transliteration: yas tvātma-ratir eva syād ātma-tṛiptaśh cha mānavaḥātmanyeva cha santuṣhṭas tasya kāryaṁ na vidyate
verses.translation: BG 3.17:But those who rejoice in the self, who are illumined and fully satisfied in the self, for them, there is no duty.

verses.slok: नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन |न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्चिदर्थव्यपाश्रय: || 18||
verses.transliteration: naiva tasya kṛitenārtho nākṛiteneha kaśhchanana chāsya sarva-bhūteṣhu kaśhchid artha-vyapāśhrayaḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.18:Such self-realized souls have nothing to gain or lose either in discharging or renouncing their duties. Nor do they need to depend on other living beings to fulfill their self-interest.

verses.slok: तस्मादसक्त: सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर |असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुष: || 19||
verses.transliteration: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samācharaasakto hyācharan karma param āpnoti pūruṣhaḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.19:Therefore, giving up attachment, perform actions as a matter of duty because by working without being attached to the fruits, one attains the Supreme.

verses.slok: कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादय: |लोकसंग्रहमेवापि सम्पश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि || 20||यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जन: |स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते || 21||
verses.transliteration: karmaṇaiva hi sansiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥloka-saṅgraham evāpi sampaśhyan kartum arhasiyad yad ācharati śhreṣhṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥsa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate
verses.translation: BG 3.20-21:By performing their prescribed duties, King Janak and others attained perfection. You should also perform your duties to set an example for the good of the world. Whatever actions great persons perform, common people follow. Whatever standards they set, all the world pursues.

verses.slok: कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादय: |लोकसंग्रहमेवापि सम्पश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि || 20||यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जन: |स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते || 21||
verses.transliteration: karmaṇaiva hi sansiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥloka-saṅgraham evāpi sampaśhyan kartum arhasiyad yad ācharati śhreṣhṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥsa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate
verses.translation: BG 3.20-21:By performing their prescribed duties, King Janak and others attained perfection. You should also perform your duties to set an example for the good of the world. Whatever actions great persons perform, common people follow. Whatever standards they set, all the world pursues.

verses.slok: न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन |नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि || 22||
verses.transliteration: na me pārthāsti kartavyaṁ triṣhu lokeṣhu kiñchananānavāptam avāptavyaṁ varta eva cha karmaṇi
verses.translation: BG 3.22:There is no duty for Me to do in all the three worlds, O Parth, nor do I have anything to gain or attain. Yet, I am engaged in prescribed duties.

verses.slok: यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रित: |मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्या: पार्थ सर्वश: || 23||
verses.transliteration: yadi hyahaṁ na varteyaṁ jātu karmaṇyatandritaḥmama vartmānuvartante manuṣhyāḥ pārtha sarvaśhaḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.23:For if I did not carefully perform the prescribed duties, O Parth, all men would follow My path in all respects.

verses.slok: उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम् |सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमा: प्रजा: || 24||
verses.transliteration: utsīdeyur ime lokā na kuryāṁ karma ched ahamsankarasya cha kartā syām upahanyām imāḥ prajāḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.24:If I ceased to perform prescribed actions, all these worlds would perish. I would be responsible for the pandemonium that would prevail, and would thereby destroy the peace of the human race.

verses.slok: सक्ता: कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत |कुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्चिकीर्षुर्लोकसंग्रहम् || 25||
verses.transliteration: saktāḥ karmaṇyavidvānso yathā kurvanti bhāratakuryād vidvāns tathāsaktaśh chikīrṣhur loka-saṅgraham
verses.translation: BG 3.25:As ignorant people perform their duties with attachment to the results, O scion of Bharat, so should the wise act without attachment, for the sake of leading people on the right path.

verses.slok: न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम् |जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान्युक्त: समाचरन् || 26||
verses.transliteration: na buddhi-bhedaṁ janayed ajñānāṁ karma-saṅgināmjoṣhayet sarva-karmāṇi vidvān yuktaḥ samācharan
verses.translation: BG 3.26:The wise should not create discord in the intellects of ignorant people, who are attached to fruitive actions, by inducing them to stop work. Rather, by performing their duties in an enlightened manner, they should inspire the ignorant also to do their prescribed duties.

verses.slok: प्रकृते: क्रियमाणानि गुणै: कर्माणि सर्वश: |अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते || 27||
verses.transliteration: prakṛiteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśhaḥahankāra-vimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate
verses.translation: BG 3.27:All activities are carried out by the three modes of material nature. But in ignorance, the soul, deluded by false identification with the body, thinks of itself as the doer.

verses.slok: तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयो: |गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते || 28||
verses.transliteration: tattva-vit tu mahā-bāho guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥguṇā guṇeṣhu vartanta iti matvā na sajjate
verses.translation: BG 3.28:O mighty-armed Arjun, illumined persons distinguish the soul as distinct fromguṇasand karmas. They perceive that it is only theguṇas(in the shape of the senses, mind, and others) that move among theguṇas(in the shape of the objects of perception), and thus they do not get entangled in them.

verses.slok: प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढा: सज्जन्ते गुणकर्मसु |तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत् || 29||
verses.transliteration: prakṛiter guṇa-sammūḍhāḥ sajjante guṇa-karmasutān akṛitsna-vido mandān kṛitsna-vin na vichālayet
verses.translation: BG 3.29:Those who are deluded by the operation of theguṇasbecome attached to the results of their actions. But the wise who understand these truths should not unsettle such ignorant people who know very little.

verses.slok: मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा |निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वर: || 30||
verses.transliteration: mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-chetasānirāśhīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.30:Performing all works as an offering unto Me, constantly meditate on Me as the Supreme. Become free from desire and selfishness, and with your mental grief departed, fight!

verses.slok: ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवा: |श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभि: || 31||
verses.transliteration: ye me matam idaṁ nityam anutiṣhṭhanti mānavāḥśhraddhāvanto ’nasūyanto muchyante te ’pi karmabhiḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.31:Those who abide by these teachings of Mine, with profound faith and free from envy, are released from the bondage of karma.

verses.slok: ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम् |सर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतस: || 32||
verses.transliteration: ye tvetad abhyasūyanto nānutiṣhṭhanti me matamsarva-jñāna-vimūḍhāns tān viddhi naṣhṭān achetasaḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.32:But those who find faults with My teachings, being bereft of knowledge and devoid of discrimination, they disregard these principles and bring about their own ruin.

verses.slok: सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्या: प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि |प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रह: किं करिष्यति || 33||
verses.transliteration: sadṛiśhaṁ cheṣhṭate svasyāḥ prakṛiter jñānavān apiprakṛitiṁ yānti bhūtāni nigrahaḥ kiṁ kariṣhyati
verses.translation: BG 3.33:Even wise people act according to their natures, for all living beings are propelled by their natural tendencies. What will one gain by repression?

verses.slok: इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ |तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ || 34||
verses.transliteration: indriyasyendriyasyārthe rāga-dveṣhau vyavasthitautayor na vaśham āgachchhet tau hyasya paripanthinau
verses.translation: BG 3.34:The senses naturally experience attachment and aversion to the sense objects, but do not be controlled by them, for they are way-layers and foes.

verses.slok: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुण: परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् |स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेय: परधर्मो भयावह: || 35||
verses.transliteration: śhreyān swa-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣhṭhitātswa-dharme nidhanaṁ śhreyaḥ para-dharmo bhayāvahaḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.35:It is far better to perform one’s natural prescribed duty, though tinged with faults, than to perform another’s prescribed duty, though perfectly. In fact, it is preferable to die in the discharge of one’s duty, than to follow the path of another, which is fraught with danger.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुष: |अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजित: || 36||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchaatha kena prayukto ’yaṁ pāpaṁ charati pūruṣhaḥanichchhann api vārṣhṇeya balād iva niyojitaḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.36:Arjun asked: Why is a person impelled to commit sinful acts, even unwillingly, as if by force, O descendent of Vrishni (Krishna)?

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भव: ||महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् || 37||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchakāma eṣha krodha eṣha rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥmahāśhano mahā-pāpmā viddhyenam iha vairiṇam
verses.translation: BG 3.37:The Supreme Lord said: It is lust alone, which is born of contact with the mode of passion, and later transformed into anger. Know this as the sinful, all-devouring enemy in the world.

verses.slok: धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथादर्शो मलेन च |यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम् || 38||
verses.transliteration: dhūmenāvriyate vahnir yathādarśho malena chayatholbenāvṛito garbhas tathā tenedam āvṛitam
verses.translation: BG 3.38:Just as a fire is covered by smoke, a mirror is masked by dust, and an embryo is concealed by the womb, similarly one’s knowledge gets shrouded by desire.

verses.slok: आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा |कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च || 39||
verses.transliteration: āvṛitaṁ jñānam etena jñānino nitya-vairiṇākāma-rūpeṇa kaunteya duṣhpūreṇānalena cha
verses.translation: BG 3.39:The knowledge of even the most discerning gets covered by this perpetual enemy in the form of insatiable desire, which is never satisfied and burns like fire, O son of Kunti.

verses.slok: इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते |एतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम् || 40||
verses.transliteration: indriyāṇi mano buddhir asyādhiṣhṭhānam uchyateetair vimohayatyeṣha jñānam āvṛitya dehinam
verses.translation: BG 3.40:The senses, mind, and intellect are said to be breeding grounds of desire. Through them, it clouds one’s knowledge and deludes the embodied soul.

verses.slok: तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ |पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम् || 41||
verses.transliteration: tasmāt tvam indriyāṇyādau niyamya bharatarṣhabhapāpmānaṁ prajahi hyenaṁ jñāna-vijñāna-nāśhanam
verses.translation: BG 3.41:Therefore, O best of the Bharatas, in the very beginning bring the senses under control and slay this enemy called desire, which is the embodiment of sin and destroys knowledge and realization.

verses.slok: इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्य: परं मन: |मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धे: परतस्तु स: || 42||
verses.transliteration: indriyāṇi parāṇyāhur indriyebhyaḥ paraṁ manaḥmanasas tu parā buddhir yo buddheḥ paratas tu saḥ
verses.translation: BG 3.42:The senses are superior to the gross body, and superior to the senses is the mind. Beyond the mind is the intellect, and even beyond the intellect is the soul.

verses.slok: एवं बुद्धे: परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना |जहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम् || 43||
verses.transliteration: evaṁ buddheḥ paraṁ buddhvā sanstabhyātmānam ātmanājahi śhatruṁ mahā-bāho kāma-rūpaṁ durāsadam
verses.translation: BG 3.43:Thus knowing the soul to be superior to the material intellect, O mighty armed Arjun, subdue the lower self (senses, mind, and intellect) by the higher self (strength of the soul), and kill this formidable enemy called lust.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |इमं विवस्वते योगं प्रोक्तवानहमव्ययम् |विवस्वान्मनवे प्राह मनुरिक्ष्वाकवेऽब्रवीत् || 1||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchaimaṁ vivasvate yogaṁ proktavān aham avyayamvivasvān manave prāha manur ikṣhvākave ’bravīt
verses.translation: BG 4.1:The Supreme Lord Shree Krishna said: I taught this eternal science of Yog to the Sun God, Vivasvan, who passed it on to Manu; and Manu, in turn, instructed it to Ikshvaku.

verses.slok: एवं परम्पराप्राप्तमिमं राजर्षयो विदु: |स कालेनेह महता योगो नष्ट: परन्तप || 2||
