verses.transliteration: evaṁ paramparā-prāptam imaṁ rājarṣhayo viduḥsa kāleneha mahatā yogo naṣhṭaḥ parantapa
verses.translation: BG 4.2:O subduer of enemies, the saintly kings thus received this science of Yog in a continuous tradition. But with the long passage of time, it was lost to the world.

verses.slok: स एवायं मया तेऽद्य योग: प्रोक्त: पुरातन: |भक्तोऽसि मे सखा चेति रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम् || 3||
verses.transliteration: sa evāyaṁ mayā te ’dya yogaḥ proktaḥ purātanaḥbhakto ’si me sakhā cheti rahasyaṁ hyetad uttamam
verses.translation: BG 4.3:The same ancient knowledge of Yog, which is the supreme secret, I am today revealing unto you, because you are My friend as well as My devotee, who can understand this transcendental wisdom.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |अपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वत: |कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति || 4||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchaaparaṁ bhavato janma paraṁ janma vivasvataḥkatham etad vijānīyāṁ tvam ādau proktavān iti
verses.translation: BG 4.4:Arjun said: You were born much after Vivasvan. How am I to understand that in the beginning You instructed this science to him?

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |बहूनि मे व्यतीतानि जन्मानि तव चार्जुन |तान्यहं वेद सर्वाणि न त्वं वेत्थ परन्तप || 5||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchabahūni me vyatītāni janmāni tava chārjunatānyahaṁ veda sarvāṇi na tvaṁ vettha parantapa
verses.translation: BG 4.5:The Supreme Lord said: Both you and I have had many births, O Arjun. You have forgotten them, while I remember them all, O Parantapa.

verses.slok: अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन् |प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय सम्भवाम्यात्ममायया || 6||
verses.transliteration: ajo ’pi sannavyayātmā bhūtānām īśhvaro ’pi sanprakṛitiṁ svām adhiṣhṭhāya sambhavāmyātma-māyayā
verses.translation: BG 4.6:Although I am unborn, the Lord of all living entities, and have an imperishable nature, yet I appear in this world by virtue ofYogmaya, My divine power.

verses.slok: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत |अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम् || 7||
verses.transliteration: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārataabhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṁ sṛijāmyaham
verses.translation: BG 4.7:Whenever there is a decline in righteousness and an increase in unrighteousness, O Arjun, at that time I manifest Myself on earth.

verses.slok: परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम् |धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे || 8||
verses.transliteration: paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ vināśhāya cha duṣhkṛitāmdharma-sansthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge
verses.translation: BG 4.8:To protect the righteous, to annihilate the wicked, and to reestablish the principles of dharma I appear on this earth, age after age.

verses.slok: जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वत: |त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन || 9||
verses.transliteration: janma karma cha me divyam evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥtyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma naiti mām eti so ’rjuna
verses.translation: BG 4.9:Those who understand the divine nature of My birth and activities, O Arjun, upon leaving the body, do not have to take birth again, but come to My eternal abode.

verses.slok: वीतरागभयक्रोधा मन्मया मामुपाश्रिता: |बहवो ज्ञानतपसा पूता मद्भावमागता: || 10||
verses.transliteration: vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhā man-mayā mām upāśhritāḥbahavo jñāna-tapasā pūtā mad-bhāvam āgatāḥ
verses.translation: BG 4.10:Being free from attachment, fear, and anger, becoming fully absorbed in Me, and taking refuge in Me, many persons in the past became purified by knowledge of Me, and thus attained My divine love.

verses.slok: ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम् |मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्या: पार्थ सर्वश: || 11||
verses.transliteration: ye yathā māṁ prapadyante tāns tathaiva bhajāmyahammama vartmānuvartante manuṣhyāḥ pārtha sarvaśhaḥ
verses.translation: BG 4.11:In whatever way people surrender unto Me, I reciprocate accordingly. Everyone follows My path, knowingly or unknowingly, O son of Pritha.

verses.slok: काङ् क्षन्त: कर्मणां सिद्धिं यजन्त इह देवता: |क्षिप्रं हि मानुषे लोके सिद्धिर्भवति कर्मजा || 12||
verses.transliteration: kāṅkṣhantaḥ karmaṇāṁ siddhiṁ yajanta iha devatāḥkṣhipraṁ hi mānuṣhe loke siddhir bhavati karmajā
verses.translation: BG 4.12:In this world, those desiring success in material activities worship the celestial gods, since material rewards manifest quickly.

verses.slok: चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागश: |तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम् || 13||
verses.transliteration: chātur-varṇyaṁ mayā sṛiṣhṭaṁ guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśhaḥtasya kartāram api māṁ viddhyakartāram avyayam
verses.translation: BG 4.13:The four categories of occupations were created by Me according to people’s qualities and activities. Although I am the Creator of this system, know Me to be the Non-doer and Eternal.

verses.slok: न मां कर्माणि लिम्पन्ति न मे कर्मफले स्पृहा |इति मां योऽभिजानाति कर्मभिर्न स बध्यते || 14||
verses.transliteration: na māṁ karmāṇi limpanti na me karma-phale spṛihāiti māṁ yo ’bhijānāti karmabhir na sa badhyate
verses.translation: BG 4.14:Activities do not taint Me, nor do I desire the fruits of action. One who knows Me in this way is never bound by the karmic reactions of work.

verses.slok: एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभि: |कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वै: पूर्वतरं कृतम् || 15||
verses.transliteration: evaṁ jñātvā kṛitaṁ karma pūrvair api mumukṣhubhiḥkuru karmaiva tasmāttvaṁ pūrvaiḥ pūrvataraṁ kṛitam
verses.translation: BG 4.15:Knowing this truth, even seekers of liberation in ancient times performed actions. Therefore, following the footsteps of those ancient sages, you too should perform your duty.

verses.slok: किं कर्म किमकर्मेति कवयोऽप्यत्र मोहिता: |तत्ते कर्म प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् || 16||
verses.transliteration: kiṁ karma kim akarmeti kavayo ’pyatra mohitāḥtat te karma pravakṣhyāmi yaj jñātvā mokṣhyase ’śhubhāt
verses.translation: BG 4.16:What is action and what is inaction? Even the wise are confused in determining this. Now I shall explain to you the secret of action, by knowing which, you may free yourself from material bondage.

verses.slok: कर्मणो ह्यपि बोद्धव्यं बोद्धव्यं च विकर्मण: |अकर्मणश्च बोद्धव्यं गहना कर्मणो गति: || 17||
verses.transliteration: karmaṇo hyapi boddhavyaṁ boddhavyaṁ cha vikarmaṇaḥakarmaṇaśh cha boddhavyaṁ gahanā karmaṇo gatiḥ
verses.translation: BG 4.17:You must understand the nature of all three—recommended action, wrong action, and inaction. The truth about these is profound and difficult to understand.

verses.slok: कर्मण्यकर्म य: पश्येदकर्मणि च कर्म य: |स बुद्धिमान्मनुष्येषु स युक्त: कृत्स्नकर्मकृत् || 18||
verses.transliteration: karmaṇyakarma yaḥ paśhyed akarmaṇi cha karma yaḥsa buddhimān manuṣhyeṣhu sa yuktaḥ kṛitsna-karma-kṛit
verses.translation: BG 4.18:Those who see action in inaction and inaction in action are truly wise amongst humans. Although performing all kinds of actions, they are yogis and masters of all their actions.

verses.slok: यस्य सर्वे समारम्भा: कामसङ्कल्पवर्जिता: |ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणं तमाहु: पण्डितं बुधा: || 19||
verses.transliteration: yasya sarve samārambhāḥ kāma-saṅkalpa-varjitāḥjñānāgni-dagdha-karmāṇaṁ tam āhuḥ paṇḍitaṁ budhāḥ
verses.translation: BG 4.19:The enlightened sages call those persons wise, whose every action is free from the desire for material pleasures and who have burnt the reactions of work in the fire of divine knowledge.

verses.slok: त्यक्त्वा कर्मफलासङ्गं नित्यतृप्तो निराश्रय: |कर्मण्यभिप्रवृत्तोऽपि नैव किञ्चित्करोति स: || 20||
verses.transliteration: tyaktvā karma-phalāsaṅgaṁ nitya-tṛipto nirāśhrayaḥkarmaṇyabhipravṛitto ’pi naiva kiñchit karoti saḥ
verses.translation: BG 4.20:Such people, having given up attachment to the fruits of their actions, are always satisfied and not dependent on external things. Despite engaging in activities, they do not do anything at all.

verses.slok: निराशीर्यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रह: |शारीरं केवलं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् || 21||
verses.transliteration: nirāśhīr yata-chittātmā tyakta-sarva-parigrahaḥśhārīraṁ kevalaṁ karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣham
verses.translation: BG 4.21:Free from expectations and the sense of ownership, with the mind and intellect fully controlled, they incur no sin even though performing actions by their body.

verses.slok: यदृच्छालाभसन्तुष्टो द्वन्द्वातीतो विमत्सर: |सम: सिद्धावसिद्धौ च कृत्वापि न निबध्यते || 22||
verses.transliteration: yadṛichchhā-lābha-santuṣhṭo dvandvātīto vimatsaraḥsamaḥ siddhāvasiddhau cha kṛitvāpi na nibadhyate
verses.translation: BG 4.22:Content with whatever gain comes of its own accord, and free from envy, they are beyond the dualities of life. Being equipoised in success and failure, they are not bound by their actions, even while performing all kinds of activities.

verses.slok: गतसङ्गस्य मुक्तस्य ज्ञानावस्थितचेतस: |यज्ञायाचरत: कर्म समग्रं प्रविलीयते || 23||
verses.transliteration: gata-saṅgasya muktasya jñānāvasthita-chetasaḥyajñāyācharataḥ karma samagraṁ pravilīyate
verses.translation: BG 4.23:They are released from the bondage of material attachments and their intellect is established in divine knowledge. Since they perform all actions as a sacrifice (to God), they are freed from all karmic reactions.

verses.slok: ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्म हविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम् |ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना || 24||
verses.transliteration: brahmārpaṇaṁ brahma havir brahmāgnau brahmaṇā hutambrahmaiva tena gantavyaṁ brahma-karma-samādhinā
verses.translation: BG 4.24:For those who are completely absorbed in God-consciousness, the oblation is Brahman, the ladle with which it is offered is Brahman, the act of offering is Brahman, and the sacrificial fire is also Brahman. Such persons, who view everything as God, easily attain Him.

verses.slok: दैवमेवापरे यज्ञं योगिन: पर्युपासते |ब्रह्माग्नावपरे यज्ञं यज्ञेनैवोपजुह्वति || 25||
verses.transliteration: daivam evāpare yajñaṁ yoginaḥ paryupāsatebrahmāgnāvapare yajñaṁ yajñenaivopajuhvati
verses.translation: BG 4.25:Some yogis worship the celestial gods with material offerings unto them. Others worship perfectly who offer the self as sacrifice in the fire of the Supreme Truth.

verses.slok: श्रोत्रादीनीन्द्रियाण्यन्ये संयमाग्निषु जुह्वति |शब्दादीन्विषयानन्य इन्द्रियाग्निषु जुह्वति || 26||
verses.transliteration: śhrotrādīnīndriyāṇyanye sanyamāgniṣhu juhvatiśhabdādīn viṣhayānanya indriyāgniṣhu juhvati
verses.translation: BG 4.26:Others offer hearing and other senses in the sacrificial fire of restraint. Still others offer sound and other objects of the senses as sacrifice in the fire of the senses.

verses.slok: सर्वाणीन्द्रियकर्माणि प्राणकर्माणि चापरे |आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते || 27||
verses.transliteration: sarvāṇīndriya-karmāṇi prāṇa-karmāṇi chāpareātma-sanyama-yogāgnau juhvati jñāna-dīpite
verses.translation: BG 4.27:Some, inspired by knowledge, offer the functions of all their senses and their life energy in the fire of the controlled mind.

verses.slok: द्रव्ययज्ञास्तपोयज्ञा योगयज्ञास्तथापरे |स्वाध्यायज्ञानयज्ञाश्च यतय: संशितव्रता: || 28||
verses.transliteration: dravya-yajñās tapo-yajñā yoga-yajñās tathāpareswādhyāya-jñāna-yajñāśh cha yatayaḥ sanśhita-vratāḥ
verses.translation: BG 4.28:Some offer their wealth as sacrifice, while others offer severe austerities as sacrifice. Some practice the eight-fold path of yogic practices, and yet others study the scriptures and cultivate knowledge as sacrifice, while observing strict vows.

verses.slok: अपाने जुह्वति प्राणं प्राणेऽपानं तथापरे |प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणा: || 29||अपरे नियताहारा: प्राणान्प्राणेषु जुह्वति |सर्वेऽप्येते यज्ञविदो यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषा: || 30||
verses.transliteration: apāne juhvati prāṇaṁ prāṇe ’pānaṁ tathāpareprāṇāpāna-gatī ruddhvā prāṇāyāma-parāyaṇāḥapare niyatāhārāḥ prāṇān prāṇeṣhu juhvatisarve ’pyete yajña-vido yajña-kṣhapita-kalmaṣhāḥ
verses.translation: BG 4.29-30:Still others offer as sacrifice the outgoing breath in the incoming breath, while some offer the incoming breath into the outgoing breath. Some arduously practiceprāṇāyāmand restrain the incoming and outgoing breaths, purely absorbed in the regulation of the life-energy. Yet others curtail their food intake and offer the breath into the life-energy as sacrifice. All these knowers of sacrifice are cleansed of their impurities as a result of such performances.

verses.slok: अपाने जुह्वति प्राणं प्राणेऽपानं तथापरे |प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणा: || 29||अपरे नियताहारा: प्राणान्प्राणेषु जुह्वति |सर्वेऽप्येते यज्ञविदो यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषा: || 30||
verses.transliteration: apāne juhvati prāṇaṁ prāṇe ’pānaṁ tathāpareprāṇāpāna-gatī ruddhvā prāṇāyāma-parāyaṇāḥapare niyatāhārāḥ prāṇān prāṇeṣhu juhvatisarve ’pyete yajña-vido yajña-kṣhapita-kalmaṣhāḥ
verses.translation: BG 4.29-30:Still others offer as sacrifice the outgoing breath in the incoming breath, while some offer the incoming breath into the outgoing breath. Some arduously practiceprāṇāyāmand restrain the incoming and outgoing breaths, purely absorbed in the regulation of the life-energy. Yet others curtail their food intake and offer the breath into the life-energy as sacrifice. All these knowers of sacrifice are cleansed of their impurities as a result of such performances.

verses.slok: यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजो यान्ति ब्रह्म सनातनम् |नायं लोकोऽस्त्ययज्ञस्य कुतोऽन्य: कुरुसत्तम || 31||
verses.transliteration: yajña-śhiṣhṭāmṛita-bhujo yānti brahma sanātanamnāyaṁ loko ’styayajñasya kuto ’nyaḥ kuru-sattama
verses.translation: BG 4.31:Those who know the secret of sacrifice, and engaging in it, partake of its remnants that are like nectar, advance toward the Absolute Truth. O best of the Kurus, those who perform no sacrifice find no happiness either in this world or the next.

verses.slok: एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे |कर्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे || 32||
verses.transliteration: evaṁ bahu-vidhā yajñā vitatā brahmaṇo mukhekarma-jān viddhi tān sarvān evaṁ jñātvā vimokṣhyase
verses.translation: BG 4.32:All these different kinds of sacrifice have been described in the Vedas. Know them as originating from different types of work; this understanding cuts the knots of material bondage.

verses.slok: श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञ: परन्तप |सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते || 33||
verses.transliteration: śhreyān dravya-mayād yajñāj jñāna-yajñaḥ parantapasarvaṁ karmākhilaṁ pārtha jñāne parisamāpyate
verses.translation: BG 4.33:O subduer of enemies, sacrifice performed in knowledge is superior to any mechanical material sacrifice. After all, O Parth, all sacrifices of work culminate in knowledge.

verses.slok: तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया |उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिन: || 34||
verses.transliteration: tad viddhi praṇipātena paripraśhnena sevayāupadekṣhyanti te jñānaṁ jñāninas tattva-darśhinaḥ
verses.translation: BG 4.34:Learn the Truth by approaching a spiritual master. Inquire from him with reverence and render service unto him. Such an enlightened Saint can impart knowledge unto you because he has seen the Truth.

verses.slok: यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव |येन भूतान्यशेषेण द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि || 35||
verses.transliteration: yaj jñātvā na punar moham evaṁ yāsyasi pāṇḍavayena bhūtānyaśheṣheṇa drakṣhyasyātmanyatho mayi
verses.translation: BG 4.35:Following this path and having achieved enlightenment from a Guru, O Arjun, you will no longer fall into delusion. In the light of that knowledge, you will see that all living beings are but parts of the Supreme, and are within Me.

verses.slok: अपि चेदसि पापेभ्य: सर्वेभ्य: पापकृत्तम: |सर्वं ज्ञानप्लवेनैव वृजिनं सन्तरिष्यसि || 36||
verses.transliteration: api ched asi pāpebhyaḥ sarvebhyaḥ pāpa-kṛit-tamaḥsarvaṁ jñāna-plavenaiva vṛijinaṁ santariṣhyasi
verses.translation: BG 4.36:Even those who are considered the most immoral of all sinners can cross over this ocean of material existence by seating themselves in the boat of divine knowledge.

verses.slok: यथैधांसि समिद्धोऽग्निर्भस्मसात्कुरुतेऽर्जुन |ज्ञानाग्नि: सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुते तथा || 37||
verses.transliteration: yathaidhānsi samiddho ’gnir bhasma-sāt kurute ’rjunajñānāgniḥ sarva-karmāṇi bhasma-sāt kurute tathā
verses.translation: BG 4.37:As a kindled fire reduces wood to ashes, O Arjun, so does the fire of knowledge burn to ashes all reactions from material activities.

verses.slok: न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते |तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्ध: कालेनात्मनि विन्दति || 38||
verses.transliteration: na hi jñānena sadṛiśhaṁ pavitramiha vidyatetatsvayaṁ yogasansiddhaḥ kālenātmani vindati
verses.translation: BG 4.38:In this world, there is nothing as purifying as divine knowledge. One who has attained purity of mind through prolonged practice of Yog, receives such knowledge within the heart, in due course of time.

verses.slok: श्रद्धावान् लभते ज्ञानं तत्पर: संयतेन्द्रिय: |ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति || 39||
verses.transliteration: śhraddhāvānllabhate jñānaṁ tat-paraḥ sanyatendriyaḥjñānaṁ labdhvā parāṁ śhāntim achireṇādhigachchhati
verses.translation: BG 4.39:Those whose faith is deep and who have practiced controlling their mind and senses attain divine knowledge. Through such transcendental knowledge, they quickly attain everlasting supreme peace.

verses.slok: अज्ञश्चाश्रद्दधानश्च संशयात्मा विनश्यति |नायं लोकोऽस्ति न परो न सुखं संशयात्मन: || 40||
verses.transliteration: ajñaśh chāśhraddadhānaśh cha sanśhayātmā vinaśhyatināyaṁ loko ’sti na paro na sukhaṁ sanśhayātmanaḥ
verses.translation: BG 4.40:But persons who possess neither faith nor knowledge, and who are of a doubting nature, suffer a downfall. For the skeptical souls, there is no happiness either in this world or the next.

verses.slok: योगसंन्यस्तकर्माणं ज्ञानसञ्छिन्नसंशयम् |आत्मवन्तं न कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय || 41||
verses.transliteration: yoga-sannyasta-karmāṇaṁ jñāna-sañchhinna-sanśhayamātmavantaṁ na karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanañjaya
verses.translation: BG 4.41:O Arjun, actions do not bind those who have renouncedkarmin the fire of Yog, whose doubts have been dispelled by knowledge, and who are situated in knowledge of the self.

verses.slok: तस्मादज्ञानसम्भूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनात्मन: |छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत || 42||
verses.transliteration: tasmād ajñāna-sambhūtaṁ hṛit-sthaṁ jñānāsinātmanaḥchhittvainaṁ sanśhayaṁ yogam ātiṣhṭhottiṣhṭha bhārata
verses.translation: BG 4.42:Therefore, with the sword of knowledge, cut asunder the doubts that have arisen in your heart. O scion of Bharat, establish yourself inkarm yog. Arise, stand up, and take action!

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |संन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि |यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्चितम् || 1||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchasannyāsaṁ karmaṇāṁ kṛiṣhṇa punar yogaṁ cha śhansasiyach chhreya etayor ekaṁ tan me brūhi su-niśhchitam
verses.translation: BG 5.1:Arjun said: O Shree Krishna, You praisedkarm sanyās(the path of renunciation of actions), and You also advised to dokarm yog(work with devotion).  Please tell me decisively which of the two is more beneficial?

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |संन्यास: कर्मयोगश्च नि:श्रेयसकरावुभौ |तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते || 2||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchasannyāsaḥ karma-yogaśh cha niḥśhreyasa-karāvubhautayos tu karma-sannyāsāt karma-yogo viśhiṣhyate
verses.translation: BG 5.2:The Supreme Lord said: Both the path ofkarm sanyās(renunciation of actions) andkarm yog(working in devotion) lead to the supreme goal. Butkarm yogis superior tokarm sanyās.

verses.slok: ज्ञेय: स नित्यसंन्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ् क्षति |निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते || 3||
verses.transliteration: jñeyaḥ sa nitya-sannyāsī yo na dveṣhṭi na kāṅkṣhatinirdvandvo hi mahā-bāho sukhaṁ bandhāt pramuchyate
verses.translation: BG 5.3:Thekarm yogis, who neither desire nor hate anything, should be considered always renounced.  Free from all dualities, they are easily liberated from the bonds of material energy.

verses.slok: साङ्ख्ययोगौ पृथग्बाला: प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिता: |एकमप्यास्थित: सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम् || 4||
verses.transliteration: sānkhya-yogau pṛithag bālāḥ pravadanti na paṇḍitāḥekamapyāsthitaḥ samyag ubhayor vindate phalam
verses.translation: BG 5.4:Only the ignorant speak ofsānkhya(renunciation of actions, orkarm sanyās) andkarm yog(work in devotion) as different.  Those who are truly learned say that by applying ourselves to any one of these paths, we can achieve the results of both.

verses.slok: यत्साङ्ख्यै: प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते |एकं साङ्ख्यं च योगं च य: पश्यति स पश्यति || 5||
verses.transliteration: yat sānkhyaiḥ prāpyate sthānaṁ tad yogair api gamyateekaṁ sānkhyaṁ cha yogaṁ cha yaḥ paśhyati sa paśhyati
verses.translation: BG 5.5:The supreme state that is attained by means ofkarm sanyāsis also attained by working in devotion. Hence, those who seekarm sanyāsandkarm yogto be identical, truly see things as they are.

verses.slok: संन्यासस्तु महाबाहो दु:खमाप्तुमयोगत: |योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म नचिरेणाधिगच्छति || 6||
verses.transliteration: sannyāsas tu mahā-bāho duḥkham āptum ayogataḥyoga-yukto munir brahma na chireṇādhigachchhati
verses.translation: BG 5.6:Perfect renunciation(karm sanyās)is difficult to attain without performing work in devotion(karm yog), O mighty-armed Arjun, but the sage who is adept inkarm yogquickly attains the Supreme.

verses.slok: योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रिय: |सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते || 7||
verses.transliteration: yoga-yukto viśhuddhātmā vijitātmā jitendriyaḥsarva-bhūtātma-bhūtātmā kurvann api na lipyate
verses.translation: BG 5.7:Thekarm yogis, who are of purified intellect, and who control the mind and senses, see the Soul of all souls in every living being.  Though performing all kinds of actions, they are never entangled.

verses.slok: नैव किञ्चित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् |पश्यञ्शृण्वन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्गच्छन्स्वपञ्श्वसन् || 8||प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्ण्न्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि |इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन् || 9||
verses.transliteration: naiva kiñchit karomīti yukto manyeta tattva-vitpaśhyañ śhṛiṇvan spṛiśhañjighrann aśhnangachchhan svapañśhvasanpralapan visṛijan gṛihṇann unmiṣhan nimiṣhann apiindriyāṇīndriyārtheṣhu vartanta iti dhārayan
verses.translation: BG 5.8-9:Those steadfast in karm yog, always think, “I am not the doer,” even while engaged in seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, moving, sleeping, breathing, speaking, excreting, grasping, and opening or closing the eyes. With the light of divine knowledge, they see that it is only the material senses that are moving amongst their objects.

verses.slok: नैव किञ्चित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् |पश्यञ्शृण्वन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्गच्छन्स्वपञ्श्वसन् || 8||प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्ण्न्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि |इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन् || 9||
verses.transliteration: naiva kiñchit karomīti yukto manyeta tattva-vitpaśhyañ śhṛiṇvan spṛiśhañjighrann aśhnangachchhan svapañśhvasanpralapan visṛijan gṛihṇann unmiṣhan nimiṣhann apiindriyāṇīndriyārtheṣhu vartanta iti dhārayan
verses.translation: BG 5.8-9:Those steadfast in karm yog, always think, “I am not the doer,” even while engaged in seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, moving, sleeping, breathing, speaking, excreting, grasping, and opening or closing the eyes. With the light of divine knowledge, they see that it is only the material senses that are moving amongst their objects.

verses.slok: ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति य: |लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा || 10||
verses.transliteration: brahmaṇyādhāya karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā karoti yaḥlipyate na sa pāpena padma-patram ivāmbhasā
verses.translation: BG 5.10:Those who dedicate their actions to God, abandoning all attachment, remain untouched by sin, just as a lotus leaf is untouched by water.

verses.slok: कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि |योगिन: कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वात्मशुद्धये || 11||
verses.transliteration: kāyena manasā buddhyā kevalair indriyair apiyoginaḥ karma kurvanti saṅgaṁ tyaktvātma-śhuddhaye
verses.translation: BG 5.11:The yogis, while giving up attachment, perform actions with their body, senses, mind, and intellect, only for the purpose of self-purification.

verses.slok: युक्त: कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम् |अयुक्त: कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते || 12||
verses.transliteration: yuktaḥ karma-phalaṁ tyaktvā śhāntim āpnoti naiṣhṭhikīmayuktaḥ kāma-kāreṇa phale sakto nibadhyate
verses.translation: BG 5.12:Offering the results of all activities to God, thekarm yogisattain everlasting peace.  Whereas those who, being impelled by their desires, work with a selfish motive become entangled because they are attached to the fruits of their actions.

verses.slok: सर्वकर्माणि मनसा संन्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी |नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन् || 13||
verses.transliteration: sarva-karmāṇi manasā sannyasyāste sukhaṁ vaśhīnava-dvāre pure dehī naiva kurvan na kārayan
verses.translation: BG 5.13:The embodied beings who are self-controlled and detached reside happily in the city of nine gates free from thoughts that they are the doers or the cause of anything.

verses.slok: न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभु: |न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते || 14||
verses.transliteration: na kartṛitvaṁ na karmāṇi lokasya sṛijati prabhuḥna karma-phala-saṅyogaṁ svabhāvas tu pravartate
verses.translation: BG 5.14:Neither the sense of doership nor the nature of actions comes from God; nor does He create the fruits of actions.  All this is enacted by the modes of material nature (guṇas).

verses.slok: नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभु: |अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तव: || 15||
verses.transliteration: nādatte kasyachit pāpaṁ na chaiva sukṛitaṁ vibhuḥajñānenāvṛitaṁ jñānaṁ tena muhyanti jantavaḥ
verses.translation: BG 5.15:The omnipresent God does not involve Himself in the sinful or virtuous deeds of anyone.  The living entities are deluded because their inner knowledge is covered by ignorance.

verses.slok: ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मन: |तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् || 16||
verses.transliteration: jñānena tu tad ajñānaṁ yeṣhāṁ nāśhitam ātmanaḥteṣhām āditya-vaj jñānaṁ prakāśhayati tat param
verses.translation: BG 5.16:But for those whose ignorance is destroyed by divine knowledge, the Supreme Entity is revealed, just as the sun illumines everything when it rises.

verses.slok: तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणा: |गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषा: || 17||
verses.transliteration: tad-buddhayas tad-ātmānas tan-niṣhṭhās tat-parāyaṇāḥgachchhantyapunar-āvṛittiṁ jñāna-nirdhūta-kalmaṣhāḥ
verses.translation: BG 5.17:Those whose intellect is fixed in God, who are completely absorbed in God, with firm faith in Him as the supreme goal, such persons quickly reach the state from which there is no return, their sins having been dispelled by the light of knowledge.

verses.slok: विद्याविनयसम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि |शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिता: समदर्शिन: || 18||
verses.transliteration: vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastiniśhuni chaiva śhva-pāke cha paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśhinaḥ
verses.translation: BG 5.18:The truly learned, with the eyes of divine knowledge, see with equal vision a Brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and a dog-eater.

verses.slok: इहैव तैर्जित: सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मन: |निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद् ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिता: || 19||
verses.transliteration: ihaiva tair jitaḥ sargo yeṣhāṁ sāmye sthitaṁ manaḥnirdoṣhaṁ hi samaṁ brahma tasmād brahmaṇi te sthitāḥ
verses.translation: BG 5.19:Those whose minds are established in equality of vision conquer the cycle of birth and death in this very life.  They possess the flawless qualities of God, and are therefore seated in the Absolute Truth.

verses.slok: न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम् |स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद् ब्रह्मणि स्थित: || 20||
verses.transliteration: na prahṛiṣhyet priyaṁ prāpya nodvijet prāpya chāpriyamsthira-buddhir asammūḍho brahma-vid brahmaṇi sthitaḥ
verses.translation: BG 5.20:Established in God, having a firm understanding of divine knowledge and not hampered by delusion, they neither rejoice in getting something pleasant nor grieve on experiencing the unpleasant.

verses.slok: बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम् |स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते || 21||
verses.transliteration: bāhya-sparśheṣhvasaktātmā vindatyātmani yat sukhamsa brahma-yoga-yuktātmā sukham akṣhayam aśhnute
verses.translation: BG 5.21:Those who are not attached to external sense pleasures realize divine bliss in the self.  Being united with God through Yog, they experience unending happiness.

verses.slok: ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दु:खयोनय एव ते |आद्यन्तवन्त: कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुध: || 22||
verses.transliteration: ye hi sansparśha-jā bhogā duḥkha-yonaya eva teādyantavantaḥ kaunteya na teṣhu ramate budhaḥ
verses.translation: BG 5.22:The pleasures that arise from contact with the sense objects, though appearing as enjoyable to worldly-minded people, are verily a source of misery.  O son of Kunti, such pleasures have a beginning and an end, so the wise do not delight in them.

verses.slok: शक्नोतीहैव य: सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात् |कामक्रोधोद्भवं वेगं स युक्त: स सुखी नर: || 23||
verses.transliteration: śhaknotīhaiva yaḥ soḍhuṁ prāk śharīra-vimokṣhaṇātkāma-krodhodbhavaṁ vegaṁ sa yuktaḥ sa sukhī naraḥ
verses.translation: BG 5.23:Those persons are yogis, who before giving up the body are able to check the forces of desire and anger; and they alone are happy.

verses.slok: योऽन्त:सुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तज्र्योतिरेव य: ।स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति ।। 24।।
verses.transliteration: yo ''ntaḥ-sukho ''ntar-ārāmas tathāntar-jyotir eva yaḥsa yogī brahma-nirvāṇaṁ brahma-bhūto ''dhigachchhati
verses.translation: BG 5.24:Those who are happy within themselves, enjoying the delight of God within, and are illumined by the inner light, such yogis are united with the Lord and are liberated from material existence.

verses.slok: लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषय: क्षीणकल्मषा: |छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मान: सर्वभूतहिते रता: || 25||
verses.transliteration: labhante brahma-nirvāṇam ṛiṣhayaḥ kṣhīṇa-kalmaṣhāḥchhinna-dvaidhā yatātmānaḥ sarva-bhūta-hite ratāḥ
verses.translation: BG 5.25:Those holy persons, whose sins have been purged, whose doubts are annihilated, whose minds are disciplined, and who are devoted to the welfare of all beings, attain God and are liberated from material existence.

verses.slok: कामक्रोधवियुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् |अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम् || 26||
verses.transliteration: kāma-krodha-viyuktānāṁ yatīnāṁ yata-chetasāmabhito brahma-nirvāṇaṁ vartate viditātmanām
verses.translation: BG 5.26:For thosesanyāsīs, who have broken out of anger and lust through constant effort, who have subdued their mind, and are self-realized, liberation from material existence is both here and hereafter.

verses.slok: स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवो: |प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ || 27||यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायण: |विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो य: सदा मुक्त एव स: || 28||
verses.transliteration: sparśhān kṛitvā bahir bāhyānśh chakṣhuśh chaivāntare bhruvoḥprāṇāpānau samau kṛitvā nāsābhyantara-chāriṇauyatendriya-mano-buddhir munir mokṣha-parāyaṇaḥvigatechchhā-bhaya-krodho yaḥ sadā mukta eva saḥ
verses.translation: BG 5.27-28:Shutting out all thoughts of external enjoyment, with the gaze fixed on the space between the eye-brows, equalizing the flow of the incoming and outgoing breath in the nostrils, and thus controlling the senses, mind, and intellect, the sage who becomes free from desire, fear, and anger, always lives in freedom.

verses.slok: स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवो: |प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ || 27||यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायण: |विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो य: सदा मुक्त एव स: || 28||
verses.transliteration: sparśhān kṛitvā bahir bāhyānśh chakṣhuśh chaivāntare bhruvoḥprāṇāpānau samau kṛitvā nāsābhyantara-chāriṇauyatendriya-mano-buddhir munir mokṣha-parāyaṇaḥvigatechchhā-bhaya-krodho yaḥ sadā mukta eva saḥ
verses.translation: BG 5.27-28:Shutting out all thoughts of external enjoyment, with the gaze fixed on the space between the eye-brows, equalizing the flow of the incoming and outgoing breath in the nostrils, and thus controlling the senses, mind, and intellect, the sage who becomes free from desire, fear, and anger, always lives in freedom.

verses.slok: भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् |सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति || 29||
verses.transliteration: bhoktāraṁ yajña-tapasāṁ sarva-loka-maheśhvaramsuhṛidaṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ jñātvā māṁ śhāntim ṛichchhati
verses.translation: BG 5.29:Having realized Me as the enjoyer of all sacrifices and austerities, the Supreme Lord of all the worlds and the selfless friend of all living beings, My devotee attains peace.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |अनाश्रित: कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति य: |स संन्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रिय: || 1||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchaanāśhritaḥ karma-phalaṁ kāryaṁ karma karoti yaḥsa sannyāsī cha yogī cha na niragnir na chākriyaḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.1:The Supreme Lord said: Those who perform prescribed duties without desiring the results of their actions are actualsanyāsīs(renunciates) and yogis, not those who have merely ceased performing sacrifices such as Agnihotra yajna or abandoned bodily activities.

verses.slok: यं संन्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव |न ह्यसंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन || 2||
verses.transliteration: yaṁ sannyāsam iti prāhur yogaṁ taṁ viddhi pāṇḍavana hyasannyasta-saṅkalpo yogī bhavati kaśhchana
verses.translation: BG 6.2:What is known assanyāsis non-different from Yog, for none become yogis without renouncing worldly desires.

verses.slok: आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते |योगारूढस्य तस्यैव शम: कारणमुच्यते || 3||
verses.transliteration: ārurukṣhor muner yogaṁ karma kāraṇam uchyateyogārūḍhasya tasyaiva śhamaḥ kāraṇam uchyate
verses.translation: BG 6.3:To the soul who is aspiring for perfection in Yog, work without attachment is said to be the means; to the sage who is already elevated in Yog, tranquility in meditation is said to be the means.

verses.slok: यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते |सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते || 4||
verses.transliteration: yadā hi nendriyārtheṣhu na karmasv-anuṣhajjatesarva-saṅkalpa-sannyāsī yogārūḍhas tadochyate
verses.translation: BG 6.4:When one is neither attached to sense objects nor to actions, such a person is said to be elevated in the science of Yog, having renounced all desires for the fruits of actions.

verses.slok: उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत् |आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मन: || 5||
verses.transliteration: uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmānam avasādayetātmaiva hyātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.5:Elevate yourself through the power of your mind, and not degrade yourself, for the mind can be the friend and also the enemy of the self.

verses.slok: बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जित: |अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्ते तात्मैव शत्रुवत् || 6||
verses.transliteration: bandhur ātmātmanas tasya yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥanātmanas tu śhatrutve vartetātmaiva śhatru-vat
verses.translation: BG 6.6:For those who have conquered the mind, it is their friend. For those who have failed to do so, the mind works like an enemy.

verses.slok: जितात्मन: प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहित: |शीतोष्णसुखदु:खेषु तथा मानापमानयो: || 7||
verses.transliteration: jitātmanaḥ praśhāntasya paramātmā samāhitaḥśhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣhu tathā mānāpamānayoḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.7:The yogis who have conquered the mind rise above the dualities of cold and heat, joy and sorrow, and honor and dishonor. Such yogis remain peaceful and steadfast in their devotion to God.

verses.slok: ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रिय: |युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चन: || 8||
verses.transliteration: jñāna-vijñāna-tṛiptātmā kūṭa-stho vijitendriyaḥyukta ityuchyate yogī sama-loṣhṭāśhma-kāñchanaḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.8:The yogi who are satisfied by knowledge and discrimination, and have conquered their senses, remain undisturbed in all circumstances. They see everything—dirt, stones, and gold—as the same.

verses.slok: सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु |साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते || 9||
verses.transliteration: suhṛin-mitrāryudāsīna-madhyastha-dveṣhya-bandhuṣhusādhuṣhvapi cha pāpeṣhu sama-buddhir viśhiṣhyate
verses.translation: BG 6.9:The yogis look upon all—well-wishers, friends, foes, the pious, and the sinners—with an impartial intellect. The yogi who is of equal intellect toward friend, companion, and foe, neutral among enemies and relatives, and unbiased between the righteous and sinful, is considered to be distinguished among humans.

verses.slok: योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थित: |एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रह: || 10||
verses.transliteration: yogī yuñjīta satatam ātmānaṁ rahasi sthitaḥekākī yata-chittātmā nirāśhīr aparigrahaḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.10:Those who seek the state of Yog should reside in seclusion, constantly engaged in meditation with a controlled mind and body, getting rid of desires and possessions for enjoyment.

verses.slok: शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मन: |नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् || 11||
verses.transliteration: śhuchau deśhe pratiṣhṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥnātyuchchhritaṁ nāti-nīchaṁ chailājina-kuśhottaram
verses.translation: BG 6.11:To practice Yog, one should make anāsan(seat) in a sanctified place, by placing Kuśh grass, deer skin, and a cloth, one over the other. Theāsanshould be neither too high nor too low.

verses.slok: तत्रैकाग्रं मन: कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रिय: |उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये || 12||समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिर: |सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् || 13||
verses.transliteration: tatraikāgraṁ manaḥ kṛitvā yata-chittendriya-kriyaḥupaviśhyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātma-viśhuddhayesamaṁ kāya-śhiro-grīvaṁ dhārayann achalaṁ sthiraḥsamprekṣhya nāsikāgraṁ svaṁ diśhaśh chānavalokayan
verses.translation: BG 6.12-13:Seated firmly on it, the yogi should strive to purify the mind by focusing it in meditation with one pointed concentration, controlling all thoughts and activities. He must hold the body, neck, and head firmly in a straight line, and gaze at the tip of the nose, without allowing the eyes to wander.

verses.slok: तत्रैकाग्रं मन: कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रिय: |उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये || 12||समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिर: |सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् || 13||
verses.transliteration: tatraikāgraṁ manaḥ kṛitvā yata-chittendriya-kriyaḥupaviśhyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātma-viśhuddhayesamaṁ kāya-śhiro-grīvaṁ dhārayann achalaṁ sthiraḥsamprekṣhya nāsikāgraṁ svaṁ diśhaśh chānavalokayan
verses.translation: BG 6.12-13:Seated firmly on it, the yogi should strive to purify the mind by focusing it in meditation with one pointed concentration, controlling all thoughts and activities. He must hold the body, neck, and head firmly in a straight line, and gaze at the tip of the nose, without allowing the eyes to wander.

verses.slok: प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थित: |मन: संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्पर: || 14||
verses.transliteration: praśhāntātmā vigata-bhīr brahmachāri-vrate sthitaḥmanaḥ sanyamya mach-chitto yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.14:Thus, with a serene, fearless, and unwavering mind, and staunch in the vow of celibacy, the vigilant yogi should meditate on Me, having Me alone as the supreme goal.

verses.slok: युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी नियतमानस: |शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति || 15||
verses.transliteration: yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁ yogī niyata-mānasaḥśhāntiṁ nirvāṇa-paramāṁ mat-sansthām adhigachchhati
verses.translation: BG 6.15:Thus, constantly keeping the mind absorbed in Me, the yogi of disciplined mind attainsnirvāṇ, and abides in Me in supreme peace.

verses.slok: नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नत: |न चाति स्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन || 16||
verses.transliteration: nātyaśhnatastu yogo ’sti na chaikāntam anaśhnataḥna chāti-svapna-śhīlasya jāgrato naiva chārjuna
verses.translation: BG 6.16:O Arjun, those who eat too much or too little, sleep too much or too little, cannot attain success in Yog.

verses.slok: युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु |युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दु:खहा || 17||
verses.transliteration: yuktāhāra-vihārasya yukta-cheṣhṭasya karmasuyukta-svapnāvabodhasya yogo bhavati duḥkha-hā
verses.translation: BG 6.17:But those who are temperate in eating and recreation, balanced in work, and regulated in sleep, can mitigate all sorrows by practicing Yog.

verses.slok: यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते |नि:स्पृह: सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा || 18||
verses.transliteration: yadā viniyataṁ chittam ātmanyevāvatiṣhṭhateniḥspṛihaḥ sarva-kāmebhyo yukta ityuchyate tadā
verses.translation: BG 6.18:With thorough discipline, they learn to withdraw the mind from selfish cravings and rivet it on the unsurpassable good of the self. Such persons are said to be in Yog, and are free from all yearning of the senses.

verses.slok: यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता |योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मन: || 19||
verses.transliteration: yathā dīpo nivāta-stho neṅgate sopamā smṛitāyogino yata-chittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.19:Just as a lamp in a windless place does not flicker, so the disciplined mind of a yogi remains steady in meditation on the Supreme.

verses.slok: यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया |यत्र चैवात्मनात्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति || 20||
verses.transliteration: yatroparamate chittaṁ niruddhaṁ yoga-sevayāyatra chaivātmanātmānaṁ paśhyann ātmani tuṣhyati
verses.translation: BG 6.20:When the mind, restrained from material activities, becomes still by the practice of Yog, then the yogi is able to behold the soul through the purified mind, and he rejoices in the inner joy.

verses.slok: सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद्बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम् |वेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वत: || 21||
verses.transliteration: sukham ātyantikaṁ yat tad buddhi-grāhyam atīndriyamvetti yatra na chaivāyaṁ sthitaśh chalati tattvataḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.21:In that joyous state of Yog, called samadhi, one experiences supreme boundless divine bliss, and thus situated, one never deviates from the Eternal Truth.

verses.slok: यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं तत: |यस्मिन्स्थितो न दु:खेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते || 22||
verses.transliteration: yaṁ labdhvā chāparaṁ lābhaṁ manyate nādhikaṁ tataḥyasmin sthito na duḥkhena guruṇāpi vichālyate
verses.translation: BG 6.22:Having gained that state, one does not consider any attainment to be greater. Being thus established, one is not shaken even in the midst of the greatest calamity.

verses.slok: तं विद्याद् दु:खसंयोगवियोगं योगसञ्ज्ञितम् |स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा || 23||
verses.transliteration: taṁ vidyād duḥkha-sanyoga-viyogaṁ yogasaṅjñitamsa niśhchayena yoktavyo yogo ’nirviṇṇa-chetasā
verses.translation: BG 6.23:That state of severance from union with misery is known as Yog. This Yog should be resolutely practiced with determination free from pessimism.

verses.slok: सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषत: |मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्तत: || 24||शनै: शनैरुपरमेद्बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया |आत्मसंस्थं मन: कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् || 25||
verses.transliteration: saṅkalpa-prabhavān kāmāns tyaktvā sarvān aśheṣhataḥmanasaivendriya-grāmaṁ viniyamya samantataḥśhanaiḥ śhanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛiti-gṛihītayāātma-sansthaṁ manaḥ kṛitvā na kiñchid api chintayet
verses.translation: BG 6.24-25:Completely renouncing all desires arising from thoughts of the world, one should restrain the senses from all sides with the mind. Slowly and steadily, with conviction in the intellect, the mind will become fixed in God alone, and will think of nothing else.

verses.slok: सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषत: |मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्तत: || 24||शनै: शनैरुपरमेद्बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया |आत्मसंस्थं मन: कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् || 25||
verses.transliteration: saṅkalpa-prabhavān kāmāns tyaktvā sarvān aśheṣhataḥmanasaivendriya-grāmaṁ viniyamya samantataḥśhanaiḥ śhanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛiti-gṛihītayāātma-sansthaṁ manaḥ kṛitvā na kiñchid api chintayet
verses.translation: BG 6.24-25:Completely renouncing all desires arising from thoughts of the world, one should restrain the senses from all sides with the mind. Slowly and steadily, with conviction in the intellect, the mind will become fixed in God alone, and will think of nothing else.

verses.slok: यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम् |ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् || 26||
verses.transliteration: yato yato niśhcharati manaśh chañchalam asthiramtatas tato niyamyaitad ātmanyeva vaśhaṁ nayet
verses.translation: BG 6.26:Whenever and wherever the restless and unsteady mind wanders, one should bring it back and continually focus it on God.

verses.slok: प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम् |उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम् || 27||
verses.transliteration: praśhānta-manasaṁ hyenaṁ yoginaṁ sukham uttamamupaiti śhānta-rajasaṁ brahma-bhūtam akalmaṣham
verses.translation: BG 6.27:Great transcendental happiness comes to the yogi whose mind is calm, whose passions are subdued, who is without sin, and who sees everything in connection with God.

verses.slok: युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मष: |सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते || 28||
verses.transliteration: yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁ yogī vigata-kalmaṣhaḥsukhena brahma-sansparśham atyantaṁ sukham aśhnute
verses.translation: BG 6.28:The self-controlled yogi, thus uniting the self with God, becomes free from material contamination, and being in constant touch with the Supreme, achieves the highest state of perfect happiness.

verses.slok: सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि |ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शन: || 29||
verses.transliteration: sarva-bhūta-stham ātmānaṁ sarva-bhūtāni chātmaniīkṣhate yoga-yuktātmā sarvatra sama-darśhanaḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.29:The true yogis, uniting their consciousness with God, see with equal eye, all living beings in God and God in all living beings.

verses.slok: यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति |तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति || 30||
verses.transliteration: yo māṁ paśhyati sarvatra sarvaṁ cha mayi paśhyatitasyāhaṁ na praṇaśhyāmi sa cha me na praṇaśhyati
verses.translation: BG 6.30:For those who see Me everywhere and see all things in Me, I am never lost, nor are they ever lost to Me.

verses.slok: सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थित: |सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते || 31||
verses.transliteration: sarva-bhūta-sthitaṁ yo māṁ bhajatyekatvam āsthitaḥsarvathā vartamāno ’pi sa yogī mayi vartate
verses.translation: BG 6.31:The yogi who is established in union with Me, and worships Me as the Supreme Soul residing in all beings, dwells only in Me, though engaged in all kinds of activities.

verses.slok: आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन |सुखं वा यदि वा दु:खं स योगी परमो मत: || 32||
verses.transliteration: ātmaupamyena sarvatra samaṁ paśhyati yo ’rjunasukhaṁ vā yadi vā duḥkhaṁ sa yogī paramo mataḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.32:I regard them to be perfect yogis who see the true equality of all living beings and respond to the joys and sorrows of others as if they were their own.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्त: साम्येन मधुसूदन |एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम् || 33||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchayo ’yaṁ yogas tvayā proktaḥ sāmyena madhusūdanaetasyāhaṁ na paśhyāmi chañchalatvāt sthitiṁ sthirām
verses.translation: BG 6.33:Arjun said: The system of Yog that you have described, O Madhusudan, appears impractical and unattainable to me, due to the restless mind.

verses.slok: चञ्चलं हि मन: कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद्दृढम् |तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् || 34||
verses.transliteration: chañchalaṁ hi manaḥ kṛiṣhṇa pramāthi balavad dṛiḍhamtasyāhaṁ nigrahaṁ manye vāyor iva su-duṣhkaram
verses.translation: BG 6.34:The mind is very restless, turbulent, strong and obstinate, O Krishna. It appears to me that it is more difficult to control than the wind.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम् |अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते || 35||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchaasanśhayaṁ mahā-bāho mano durnigrahaṁ chalamabhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa cha gṛihyate
verses.translation: BG 6.35:Lord Krishna said: O mighty-armed son of Kunti, what you say is correct; the mind is indeed very difficult to restrain. But by practice and detachment, it can be controlled.

verses.slok: असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मति: |वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायत: || 36||
verses.transliteration: asaṅyatātmanā yogo duṣhprāpa iti me matiḥvaśhyātmanā tu yatatā śhakyo ’vāptum upāyataḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.36:Yog is difficult to attain for one whose mind is unbridled. However, those who have learnt to control the mind, and who strive earnestly by proper means, can attain perfection in Yog. This is My opinion.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |अयति: श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानस: |अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति || 37||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchaayatiḥ śhraddhayopeto yogāch chalita-mānasaḥaprāpya yoga-sansiddhiṁ kāṅ gatiṁ kṛiṣhṇa gachchhati
verses.translation: BG 6.37:Arjun said: What is the fate of the unsuccessful yogi who begins the path with faith, but who does not endeavor sufficiently due to an unsteady mind and is unable to reach the goal of Yog in this life?

verses.slok: कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति |अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मण: पथि || 38||
verses.transliteration: kachchin nobhaya-vibhraṣhṭaśh chhinnābhram iva naśhyatiapratiṣhṭho mahā-bāho vimūḍho brahmaṇaḥ pathi
verses.translation: BG 6.38:Does not such a person who deviates from Yog get deprived of both material and spiritual success, O mighty-armed Krishna, and perish like a broken cloud with no position in either sphere?

verses.slok: एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषत: |त्वदन्य: संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते || 39||
verses.transliteration: etan me sanśhayaṁ kṛiṣhṇa chhettum arhasyaśheṣhataḥtvad-anyaḥ sanśhayasyāsya chhettā na hyupapadyate
verses.translation: BG 6.39:O Krishna, please dispel this doubt of mine completely, for who other than You can do so?

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते |न हि कल्याणकृत्कश्चिद्दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति || 40||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchapārtha naiveha nāmutra vināśhas tasya vidyatena hi kalyāṇa-kṛit kaśhchid durgatiṁ tāta gachchhati
verses.translation: BG 6.40:The Supreme Lord said: O Parth, one who engages on the spiritual path does not meet with destruction either in this world or the world to come. My dear friend, one who strives for God-realization is never overcome by evil.

verses.slok: प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वती: समा: |शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते || 41||अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम् |एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम् || 42||
verses.transliteration: prāpya puṇya-kṛitāṁ lokān uṣhitvā śhāśhvatīḥ samāḥśhuchīnāṁ śhrīmatāṁ gehe yoga-bhraṣhṭo ’bhijāyateatha vā yoginām eva kule bhavati dhīmatāmetad dhi durlabhataraṁ loke janma yad īdṛiśham
verses.translation: BG 6.41-42:The unsuccessful yogis, upon death, go to the abodes of the virtuous. After dwelling there for many ages, they are again reborn in the earth plane, into a family of pious and prosperous people. Else, if they had developed dispassion due to long practice of Yog, they are born into a family endowed with divine wisdom. Such a birth is very difficult to attain in this world.

verses.slok: प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वती: समा: |शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते || 41||अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम् |एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम् || 42||
verses.transliteration: prāpya puṇya-kṛitāṁ lokān uṣhitvā śhāśhvatīḥ samāḥśhuchīnāṁ śhrīmatāṁ gehe yoga-bhraṣhṭo ’bhijāyateatha vā yoginām eva kule bhavati dhīmatāmetad dhi durlabhataraṁ loke janma yad īdṛiśham
verses.translation: BG 6.41-42:The unsuccessful yogis, upon death, go to the abodes of the virtuous. After dwelling there for many ages, they are again reborn in the earth plane, into a family of pious and prosperous people. Else, if they had developed dispassion due to long practice of Yog, they are born into a family endowed with divine wisdom. Such a birth is very difficult to attain in this world.

verses.slok: तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम् |यतते च ततो भूय: संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन || 43||
verses.transliteration: tatra taṁ buddhi-sanyogaṁ labhate paurva-dehikamyatate cha tato bhūyaḥ sansiddhau kuru-nandana
verses.translation: BG 6.43:On taking such a birth, O descendant of Kurus, they reawaken the wisdom of their previous lives, and strive even harder toward perfection in Yog.

verses.slok: पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि स: |जिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्तते || 44||
verses.transliteration: pūrvābhyāsena tenaiva hriyate hyavaśho ’pi saḥjijñāsur api yogasya śhabda-brahmātivartate
verses.translation: BG 6.44:Indeed, they feel drawn toward God, even against their will, on the strength of their past discipline. Such seekers naturally rise above the ritualistic principles of the scriptures.

verses.slok: प्रयत्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिष: |अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम् || 45||
verses.transliteration: prayatnād yatamānas tu yogī sanśhuddha-kilbiṣhaḥaneka-janma-sansiddhas tato yāti parāṁ gatim
verses.translation: BG 6.45:With the accumulated merits of many past births, when these yogis engage in sincere endeavors to make further progress, they become purified from material desires and attain perfection in this life itself.

verses.slok: तपस्विभ्योऽधिकोयोगीज्ञानिभ्योऽपिमतोऽधिक:|कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिकोयोगीतस्माद्योगीभवार्जुन|| 46||
verses.transliteration: tapasvibhyo ’dhiko yogījñānibhyo ’pi mato ’dhikaḥkarmibhyaśh chādhiko yogītasmād yogī bhavārjuna
verses.translation: BG 6.46:A yogi is superior to thetapasvī(ascetic), superior to thejñānī(a person of learning), and even superior to thekarmī(ritualistic performer). Therefore, O Arjun, strive to be a yogi.

verses.slok: योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना |श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मत: || 47||
verses.transliteration: yoginām api sarveṣhāṁ mad-gatenāntar-ātmanāśhraddhāvān bhajate yo māṁ sa me yuktatamo mataḥ
verses.translation: BG 6.47:Of all yogis, those whose minds are always absorbed in Me, and who engage in devotion to Me with great faith, them I consider to be the highest of all.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |मय्यासक्तमना: पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रय: |असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु || 1||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchamayyāsakta-manāḥ pārtha yogaṁ yuñjan mad-āśhrayaḥasanśhayaṁ samagraṁ māṁ yathā jñāsyasi tach chhṛiṇu
verses.translation: BG 7.1:The Supreme Lord said: Now listen, O Arjun, how, with the mind attached exclusively to Me, and surrendering to Me through the practice ofbhakti yog, you can know Me completely, free from doubt.

verses.slok: ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषत: |यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते || 2||
verses.transliteration: jñānaṁ te ’haṁ sa-vijñānam idaṁ vakṣhyāmyaśheṣhataḥyaj jñātvā neha bhūyo ’nyaj jñātavyam-avaśhiṣhyate
verses.translation: BG 7.2:I shall now reveal unto you fully this knowledge and wisdom, knowing which nothing else remains to be known in this world.

verses.slok: मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये |यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वत: || 3||
verses.transliteration: manuṣhyāṇāṁ sahasreṣhu kaśhchid yatati siddhayeyatatām api siddhānāṁ kaśhchin māṁ vetti tattvataḥ
verses.translation: BG 7.3:Amongst thousands of persons, hardly one strives for perfection; and amongst those who have achieved perfection, hardly one knows Me in truth.

verses.slok: भूमिरापोऽनलो वायु: खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च |अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा || 4||
verses.transliteration: bhūmir-āpo ’nalo vāyuḥ khaṁ mano buddhir eva chaahankāra itīyaṁ me bhinnā prakṛitir aṣhṭadhā
verses.translation: BG 7.4:Earth, water, fire, air, space, mind, intellect, and ego—these are eight components of My material energy.

verses.slok: अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम् |जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत् || 5||
verses.transliteration: apareyam itas tvanyāṁ prakṛitiṁ viddhi me parāmjīva-bhūtāṁ mahā-bāho yayedaṁ dhāryate jagat
verses.translation: BG 7.5:Such is My inferior energy. But beyond it, O mighty-armed Arjun, I have a superior energy. This is thejīva śhakti(the soul energy), which comprises the embodied souls who are the basis of life in this world.

verses.slok: एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय |अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगत: प्रभव: प्रलयस्तथा || 6||
verses.transliteration: etad-yonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇītyupadhārayaahaṁ kṛitsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā
verses.translation: BG 7.6:Know that all living beings are manifested by these two energies of Mine. I am the source of the entire creation, and into Me it again dissolves.

verses.slok: मत्त: परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय |मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव || 7||
verses.transliteration: mattaḥ parataraṁ nānyat kiñchid asti dhanañjayamayi sarvam idaṁ protaṁ sūtre maṇi-gaṇā iva
verses.translation: BG 7.7:There is nothing higher than Myself, O Arjun. Everything rests in Me, as beads strung on a thread.

verses.slok: रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययो: |प्रणव: सर्ववेदेषु शब्द: खे पौरुषं नृषु || 8||
verses.transliteration: raso ’ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śhaśhi-sūryayoḥpraṇavaḥ sarva-vedeṣhu śhabdaḥ khe pauruṣhaṁ nṛiṣhu
verses.translation: BG 7.8:I am the taste in water, O son of Kunti, and the radiance of the sun and the moon. I am the sacred syllable Om in the Vedic mantras; I am the sound in ether, and the ability in humans.

verses.slok: पुण्यो गन्ध: पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ |जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु || 9||
verses.transliteration: puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛithivyāṁ cha tejaśh chāsmi vibhāvasaujīvanaṁ sarva-bhūteṣhu tapaśh chāsmi tapasviṣhu
verses.translation: BG 7.9:I am the pure fragrance of the Earth, and the brilliance in fire. I am the life-force in all beings, and the penance of the ascetics.

verses.slok: बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् |बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् || 10||
verses.transliteration: bījaṁ māṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ viddhi pārtha sanātanambuddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham
verses.translation: BG 7.10:O Arjun, know that I am the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intellect of the intelligent, and the splendor of the glorious.

verses.slok: बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम् |धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ || 11||
verses.transliteration: balaṁ balavatāṁ chāhaṁ kāma-rāga-vivarjitamdharmāviruddho bhūteṣhu kāmo ’smi bharatarṣhabha
verses.translation: BG 7.11:O best of the Bharatas, in strong persons, I am their strength devoid of desire and passion. I am sexual activity not conflicting with virtue or scriptural injunctions.

verses.slok: ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये |मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि || 12||
verses.transliteration: ye chaiva sāttvikā bhāvā rājasās tāmasāśh cha yematta eveti tān viddhi na tvahaṁ teṣhu te mayi
verses.translation: BG 7.12:The three states of material existence—goodness, passion, and ignorance—are manifested by My energy. They are in Me, but I am beyond them.

verses.slok: त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभि: सर्वमिदं जगत् |मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्य: परमव्ययम् || 13||
verses.transliteration: tribhir guṇa-mayair bhāvair ebhiḥ sarvam idaṁ jagatmohitaṁ nābhijānāti māmebhyaḥ param avyayam
verses.translation: BG 7.13:Deluded by the three modes of Maya, people in this world are unable to know Me, the imperishable and eternal.

verses.slok: दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया |मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते || 14||
verses.transliteration: daivī hyeṣhā guṇa-mayī mama māyā duratyayāmām eva ye prapadyante māyām etāṁ taranti te
verses.translation: BG 7.14:My divine energy Maya, consisting of the three modes of nature, is very difficult to overcome. But those who surrender unto Me cross over it easily.

verses.slok: न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढा: प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमा: |माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिता: || 15||
verses.transliteration: na māṁ duṣhkṛitino mūḍhāḥ prapadyante narādhamāḥmāyayāpahṛita-jñānā āsuraṁ bhāvam āśhritāḥ
verses.translation: BG 7.15:Four kinds of people do not surrender unto Me—those ignorant of knowledge, those who lazily follow their lower nature though capable of knowing Me, those with deluded intellect, and those with a demoniac nature.

verses.slok: चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जना: सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन |आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ || 16||
verses.transliteration: chatur-vidhā bhajante māṁ janāḥ sukṛitino ’rjunaārto jijñāsur arthārthī jñānī cha bharatarṣhabha
verses.translation: BG 7.16:O best amongst the Bharatas, four kinds of pious people engage in My devotion—the distressed, the seekers of knowledge, the seekers of worldly possessions, and those who are situated in knowledge.

verses.slok: तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्तिर्विशिष्यते |प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रिय: || 17||
verses.transliteration: teṣhāṁ jñānī nitya-yukta eka-bhaktir viśhiṣhyatepriyo hi jñānino ’tyartham ahaṁ sa cha mama priyaḥ
verses.translation: BG 7.17:Amongst these, I consider them to be the highest, who worship Me with knowledge, and are steadfastly and exclusively devoted to Me. I am very dear to them and they are very dear to Me.

verses.slok: उदारा: सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम् |आस्थित: स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम् || 18||
verses.transliteration: udārāḥ sarva evaite jñānī tvātmaiva me matamāsthitaḥ sa hi yuktātmā mām evānuttamāṁ gatim
verses.translation: BG 7.18:All those who are devoted to Me are indeed noble. But those in knowledge, who are of steadfast mind, whose intellect is merged in Me, and who have made Me alone as their supreme goal, I consider as My very self.

verses.slok: बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते |वासुदेव: सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभ: || 19||
verses.transliteration: bahūnāṁ janmanām ante jñānavān māṁ prapadyatevāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā su-durlabhaḥ
verses.translation: BG 7.19:After many births of spiritual practice, one who is endowed with knowledge surrenders unto Me, knowing Me to be all that is. Such a great soul is indeed very rare.

verses.slok: कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञाना: प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवता: |तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियता: स्वया || 20||
verses.transliteration: kāmais tais tair hṛita-jñānāḥ prapadyante ’nya-devatāḥtaṁ taṁ niyamam āsthāya prakṛityā niyatāḥ svayā
verses.translation: BG 7.20:Those whose knowledge has been carried away by material desires surrender to the celestial gods. Following their own nature, they worship thedevatās, practicing rituals meant to propitiate these celestial personalities.

verses.slok: यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्त: श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति |तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम् || 21||
verses.transliteration: yo yo yāṁ yāṁ tanuṁ bhaktaḥ śhraddhayārchitum ichchhatitasya tasyāchalāṁ śhraddhāṁ tām eva vidadhāmyaham
verses.translation: BG 7.21:Whatever celestial form a devotee seeks to worship with faith, I steady the faith of such a devotee in that form.

verses.slok: स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते |लभते च तत: कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान् || 22||
verses.transliteration: sa tayā śhraddhayā yuktas tasyārādhanam īhatelabhate cha tataḥ kāmān mayaiva vihitān hi tān
verses.translation: BG 7.22:Endowed with faith, the devotee worships a particular celestial god and obtains the objects of desire. But in reality, I alone arrange these benefits.

verses.slok: अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम् |देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि || 23||
verses.transliteration: antavat tu phalaṁ teṣhāṁ tad bhavatyalpa-medhasāmdevān deva-yajo yānti mad-bhaktā yānti mām api
verses.translation: BG 7.23:But the fruit gained by these people of little understanding is perishable. Those who worship the celestial gods go to the celestial abodes, while My devotees come to Me.

verses.slok: अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धय: |परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम् || 24||
verses.transliteration: avyaktaṁ vyaktim āpannaṁ manyante mām abuddhayaḥparaṁ bhāvam ajānanto mamāvyayam anuttamam
verses.translation: BG 7.24:The less intelligent think that I, the Supreme Lord Shree Krishna, was formless earlier and have now assumed this personality. They do not understand the imperishable exalted nature of My personal form.

verses.slok: नाहं प्रकाश: सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृत: |मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम् || 25||
verses.transliteration: nāhaṁ prakāśhaḥ sarvasya yoga-māyā-samāvṛitaḥmūḍho ’yaṁ nābhijānāti loko mām ajam avyayam
verses.translation: BG 7.25:I am not manifest to everyone, being veiled by My divineYogmayaenergy. Hence, those without knowledge do not know that I am without birth and changeless.

verses.slok: वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन |भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन || 26||
verses.transliteration: vedāhaṁ samatītāni vartamānāni chārjunabhaviṣhyāṇi cha bhūtāni māṁ tu veda na kaśhchana
verses.translation: BG 7.26:O Arjun, I know of past, present, and future, and I also know all living beings; but Me no one knows.

verses.slok: इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत |सर्वभूतानि सम्मोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप || 27||
verses.transliteration: ichchhā-dveṣha-samutthena dvandva-mohena bhāratasarva-bhūtāni sammohaṁ sarge yānti parantapa
verses.translation: BG 7.27:O descendant of Bharat, the dualities of desire and aversion arise from illusion. O conqueror of enemies, all living beings in the material realm are deluded by these.

verses.slok: येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम् |ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रता: || 28||
verses.transliteration: yeṣhāṁ tvanta-gataṁ pāpaṁ janānāṁ puṇya-karmaṇāmte dvandva-moha-nirmuktā bhajante māṁ dṛiḍha-vratāḥ
verses.translation: BG 7.28:But persons, whose sins have been destroyed by engaging in pious activities, become free from the illusion of dualities. Such persons worship Me with determination.

verses.slok: जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये |ते ब्रह्म तद्विदु: कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम् || 29||
verses.transliteration: jarā-maraṇa-mokṣhāya mām āśhritya yatanti yete brahma tadviduḥ kṛitsnam adhyātmaṁ karma chākhilam
verses.translation: BG 7.29:Those who take shelter in Me, striving for liberation from old-age and death, come to know the Brahman, the individual self, and the entire field of karmic action.

verses.slok: साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदु: |प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतस: || 30||
verses.transliteration: sādhibhūtādhidaivaṁ māṁ sādhiyajñaṁ cha ye viduḥprayāṇa-kāle ’pi cha māṁ te vidur yukta-chetasaḥ
verses.translation: BG 7.30:Those who know Me as the governing principle of theadhibhūta(field of matter) and theadhidaiva(the celestial gods), and asadhiyajña(the Lord of all sacrificial performances), such enlightened souls are in full consciousness of Me even at the time of death.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |किं तद्ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं किं कर्म पुरुषोत्तम |अधिभूतं च किं प्रोक्तमधिदैवं किमुच्यते || 1||अधियज्ञ: कथं कोऽत्र देहेऽस्मिन्मधुसूदन |प्रयाणकाले च कथं ज्ञेयोऽसि नियतात्मभि: || 2||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchakiṁ tad brahma kim adhyātmaṁ kiṁ karma puruṣhottamaadhibhūtaṁ cha kiṁ proktam adhidaivaṁ kim uchyateadhiyajñaḥ kathaṁ ko ’tra dehe ’smin madhusūdanaprayāṇa-kāle cha kathaṁ jñeyo ’si niyatātmabhiḥ
verses.translation: BG 8.1-2:Arjun said: O Supreme Lord, what is Brahman (Absolute Reality), what isadhyatma(the individual soul), and what is karma? What is said to beadhibhuta, and who is said to beAdhidaiva? Who isAdhiyajnain the body and how is He theAdhiyajna? O Krishna, how are You to be known at the time of death by those of steadfast mind?

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |किं तद्ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं किं कर्म पुरुषोत्तम |अधिभूतं च किं प्रोक्तमधिदैवं किमुच्यते || 1||अधियज्ञ: कथं कोऽत्र देहेऽस्मिन्मधुसूदन |प्रयाणकाले च कथं ज्ञेयोऽसि नियतात्मभि: || 2||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchakiṁ tad brahma kim adhyātmaṁ kiṁ karma puruṣhottamaadhibhūtaṁ cha kiṁ proktam adhidaivaṁ kim uchyateadhiyajñaḥ kathaṁ ko ’tra dehe ’smin madhusūdanaprayāṇa-kāle cha kathaṁ jñeyo ’si niyatātmabhiḥ
verses.translation: BG 8.1-2:Arjun said: O Supreme Lord, what is Brahman (Absolute Reality), what isadhyatma(the individual soul), and what is karma? What is said to beadhibhuta, and who is said to beAdhidaiva? Who isAdhiyajnain the body and how is He theAdhiyajna? O Krishna, how are You to be known at the time of death by those of steadfast mind?

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |अक्षरं ब्रह्म परमं स्वभावोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते |भूतभावोद्भवकरो विसर्ग: कर्मसञ्ज्ञित: || 3||
verses.transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvāchaakṣharaṁ brahma paramaṁ svabhāvo ’dhyātmam uchyatebhūta-bhāvodbhava-karo visargaḥ karma-sanjñitaḥ
verses.translation: BG 8.3:The Lord said: The Supreme Indestructible Entity is called Brahman; one’s own self is calledadhyatma.  Actions pertaining to the material personality of living beings, and its development are called karma, or fruitive activities.

verses.slok: अधिभूतं क्षरो भाव: पुरुषश्चाधिदैवतम् |अधियज्ञोऽहमेवात्र देहे देहभृतां वर || 4||
verses.transliteration: adhibhūtaṁ kṣharo bhāvaḥ puruṣhaśh chādhidaivatamadhiyajño ’ham evātra dehe deha-bhṛitāṁ vara
verses.translation: BG 8.4:O best of the embodied souls, the physical manifestation that is constantly changing is calledadhibhūta; the universal form of God, which presides over the celestial gods in this creation, is calledAdhidaiva; I, who dwell in the heart of every living being, am calledAdhiyajna, or the Lord of all sacrifices.

verses.slok: अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम् |य: प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशय: || 5||
verses.transliteration: anta-kāle cha mām eva smaran muktvā kalevaramyaḥ prayāti sa mad-bhāvaṁ yāti nāstyatra sanśhayaḥ
verses.translation: BG 8.5:Those who relinquish the body while remembering Me at the moment of death will come to Me. There is certainly no doubt about this.

verses.slok: यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम् |तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावित: || 6||
verses.transliteration: yaṁ yaṁ vāpi smaran bhāvaṁ tyajatyante kalevaramtaṁ tam evaiti kaunteya sadā tad-bhāva-bhāvitaḥ
verses.translation: BG 8.6:Whatever one remembers upon giving up the body at the time of death, O son of Kunti, one attains that state, being always absorbed in such contemplation.

verses.slok: तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु मामनुस्मर युध्य च |मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्मामेवैष्यस्यसंशयम् || 7||
verses.transliteration: tasmāt sarveṣhu kāleṣhu mām anusmara yudhya chamayyarpita-mano-buddhir mām evaiṣhyasyasanśhayam
verses.translation: BG 8.7:Therefore, always remember Me and also do your duty of fighting the war.  With mind and intellect surrendered to Me, you will definitely attain Me; of this, there is no doubt.

verses.slok: अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन चेतसा नान्यगामिना |परमं पुरुषं दिव्यं याति पार्थानुचिन्तयन् || 8||
verses.transliteration: abhyāsa-yoga-yuktena chetasā nānya-gāmināparamaṁ puruṣhaṁ divyaṁ yāti pārthānuchintayan
verses.translation: BG 8.8:With practice, O Parth, when you constantly engage the mind in remembering Me, the Supreme Divine Personality, without deviating, you will certainly attain Me.

verses.slok: कविं पुराणमनुशासितारमणोरणीयांसमनुस्मरेद्य: |सर्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूपमादित्यवर्णं तमस: परस्तात् || 9||प्रयाणकाले मनसाचलेनभक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव |भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक्स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम् || 10||
verses.transliteration: kaviṁ purāṇam anuśhāsitāramaṇor aṇīyānsam anusmared yaḥsarvasya dhātāram achintya-rūpamāditya-varṇaṁ tamasaḥ parastātprayāṇa-kāle manasāchalenabhaktyā yukto yoga-balena chaivabhruvor madhye prāṇam āveśhya samyaksa taṁ paraṁ puruṣham upaiti divyam
verses.translation: BG 8.9-10:God is Omniscient, the most ancient One, the Controller, subtler than the subtlest, the Support of all, and the possessor of an inconceivable divine form; He is brighter than the sun, and beyond all darkness of ignorance.  One who at the time of death, with unmoving mind attained by the practice of Yog, fixes theprāṇ(life-airs) between the eyebrows, and steadily remembers the Divine Lord with great devotion, certainly attains Him.

verses.slok: कविं पुराणमनुशासितारमणोरणीयांसमनुस्मरेद्य: |सर्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूपमादित्यवर्णं तमस: परस्तात् || 9||प्रयाणकाले मनसाचलेनभक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव |भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक्स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम् || 10||
verses.transliteration: kaviṁ purāṇam anuśhāsitāramaṇor aṇīyānsam anusmared yaḥsarvasya dhātāram achintya-rūpamāditya-varṇaṁ tamasaḥ parastātprayāṇa-kāle manasāchalenabhaktyā yukto yoga-balena chaivabhruvor madhye prāṇam āveśhya samyaksa taṁ paraṁ puruṣham upaiti divyam
verses.translation: BG 8.9-10:God is Omniscient, the most ancient One, the Controller, subtler than the subtlest, the Support of all, and the possessor of an inconceivable divine form; He is brighter than the sun, and beyond all darkness of ignorance.  One who at the time of death, with unmoving mind attained by the practice of Yog, fixes theprāṇ(life-airs) between the eyebrows, and steadily remembers the Divine Lord with great devotion, certainly attains Him.

verses.slok: यदक्षरं वेदविदो वदन्तिविशन्ति यद्यतयो वीतरागा: |यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्तितत्ते पदं संग्रहेण प्रवक्ष्ये || 11||
verses.transliteration: yad akṣharaṁ veda-vido vadantiviśhanti yad yatayo vīta-rāgāḥyad ichchhanto brahmacharyaṁ charantitat te padaṁ saṅgraheṇa pravakṣhye
verses.translation: BG 8.11:Scholars of the Vedas describe Him as Imperishable; great ascetics practice the vow of celibacy and renounce worldly pleasures to enter into Him.  I shall now explain to you briefly the path to that goal.

verses.slok: सर्वद्वाराणि संयम्य मनो हृदि निरुध्य च |मूर्ध्न्याधायात्मन: प्राणमास्थितो योगधारणाम् || 12||
verses.transliteration: sarva-dvārāṇi sanyamya mano hṛidi nirudhya chamūrdhnyādhāyātmanaḥ prāṇam āsthito yoga-dhāraṇām
verses.translation: BG 8.12:Restraining all the gates of the body and fixing the mind in the heart region, and then drawing the life-breath to the head, one should get established in steadfast yogic concentration.

verses.slok: ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म व्याहरन्मामनुस्मरन् |य: प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं स याति परमां गतिम् || 13||
verses.transliteration: oṁ ityekākṣharaṁ brahma vyāharan mām anusmaranyaḥ prayāti tyajan dehaṁ sa yāti paramāṁ gatim
verses.translation: BG 8.13:One who departs from the body while remembering Me, the Supreme Personality, and chanting the syllable Om, will attain the supreme goal.

verses.slok: अनन्यचेता: सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यश: |तस्याहं सुलभ: पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिन: || 14||
verses.transliteration: ananya-chetāḥ satataṁ yo māṁ smarati nityaśhaḥtasyāhaṁ sulabhaḥ pārtha nitya-yuktasya yoginaḥ
verses.translation: BG 8.14:O Parth, for those yogis who always think of Me with exclusive devotion, I am easily attainable because of their constant absorption in Me.

verses.slok: मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म दु:खालयमशाश्वतम् |नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मान: संसिद्धिं परमां गता: || 15||
verses.transliteration: mām upetya punar janma duḥkhālayam aśhāśhvatamnāpnuvanti mahātmānaḥ sansiddhiṁ paramāṁ gatāḥ
verses.translation: BG 8.15:Having attained Me, the great souls are no more subject to rebirth in this world, which is transient and full of misery, because they have attained the highest perfection.

verses.slok: आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोका: पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन |मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते || 16||
verses.transliteration: ā-brahma-bhuvanāl lokāḥ punar āvartino ’rjunamām upetya tu kaunteya punar janma na vidyate
verses.translation: BG 8.16:In all the worlds of this material creation, up to the highest abode of Brahma, you will be subject to rebirth, O Arjun.  But on attaining My Abode, O son of Kunti, there is no further rebirth.

verses.slok: सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तमहर्यद्ब्रह्मणो विदु: |रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जना: || 17||
verses.transliteration: sahasra-yuga-paryantam ahar yad brahmaṇo viduḥrātriṁ yuga-sahasrāntāṁ te ’ho-rātra-vido janāḥ
verses.translation: BG 8.17:One day of Brahma (kalp) lasts a thousand cycles of the four ages (mahā yug) and his night also extends for the same span of time.  The wise who know this understand the reality about day and night.

verses.slok: अव्यक्ताद्व्यक्तय: सर्वा: प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे |रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते तत्रैवाव्यक्तसञ्ज्ञके || 18||
verses.transliteration: avyaktād vyaktayaḥ sarvāḥ prabhavantyahar-āgamerātryāgame pralīyante tatraivāvyakta-sanjñake
verses.translation: BG 8.18:At the advent of Brahma’s day, all living beings emanate from the unmanifest source.  And at the fall of his night, all embodied beings again merge into their unmanifest source.

verses.slok: भूतग्राम: स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते |रात्र्यागमेऽवश: पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे || 19||
verses.transliteration: bhūta-grāmaḥ sa evāyaṁ bhūtvā bhūtvā pralīyaterātryāgame ’vaśhaḥ pārtha prabhavatyahar-āgame
verses.translation: BG 8.19:Multitudes of beings repeatedly take birth with the advent of Brahma’s day, and are reabsorbed on the arrival of the cosmic night, to manifest again automatically on the advent of the next cosmic day.

verses.slok: परस्तस्मात्तु भावोऽन्योऽव्यक्तोऽव्यक्तात्सनातन: |य: स सर्वेषु भूतेषु नश्यत्सु न विनश्यति || 20||
verses.transliteration: paras tasmāt tu bhāvo ’nyo ’vyakto ’vyaktāt sanātanaḥyaḥ sa sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu naśhyatsu na vinaśhyati
verses.translation: BG 8.20:Transcendental to this manifest and unmanifest creation, there is yet another unmanifest eternal dimension.  That realm does not cease even when all others do.

verses.slok: अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तस्तमाहु: परमां गतिम् |यं प्राप्य न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम || 21||
verses.transliteration: avyakto ’kṣhara ityuktas tam āhuḥ paramāṁ gatimyaṁ prāpya na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṁ mama
verses.translation: BG 8.21:That unmanifest dimension is the supreme goal, and upon reaching it, one never returns to this mortal world.  That is My Supreme Abode.

verses.slok: पुरुष: स पर: पार्थ भक्त्या लभ्यस्त्वनन्यया |यस्यान्त:स्थानि भूतानि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् || 22||
verses.transliteration: puruṣhaḥ sa paraḥ pārtha bhaktyā labhyas tvananyayāyasyāntaḥ-sthāni bhūtāni yena sarvam idaṁ tatam
verses.translation: BG 8.22:The Supreme Divine Personality is greater than all that exists.  Although He is all-pervading and all living beings are situated in Him, yet He can be known only through devotion.

verses.slok: यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिमावृत्तिं चैव योगिन: |प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ || 23||अग्निर्ज्योतिरह: शुक्ल: षण्मासा उत्तरायणम् |तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जना: || 24||धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्ण: षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम् |तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते || 25||शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते जगत: शाश्वते मते |एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययावर्तते पुन: || 26||
verses.transliteration: yatra kāle tvanāvṛittim āvṛittiṁ chaiva yoginaḥprayātā yānti taṁ kālaṁ vakṣhyāmi bharatarṣhabhaagnir jyotir ahaḥ śhuklaḥ ṣhaṇ-māsā uttarāyaṇamtatra prayātā gachchhanti brahma brahma-vido janāḥdhūmo rātris tathā kṛiṣhṇaḥ ṣhaṇ-māsā dakṣhiṇāyanamtatra chāndramasaṁ jyotir yogī prāpya nivartateśhukla-kṛiṣhṇe gatī hyete jagataḥ śhāśhvate mateekayā yātyanāvṛittim anyayāvartate punaḥ
verses.translation: BG 8.23-26:I shall now describe to you the different paths of passing away from this world, O best of the Bharatas, one of which leads to liberation and the other leads to rebirth.  Those who know the Supreme Brahman and who depart from this world, during the six months of the sun’s northern course, the bright fortnight of the moon, and the bright part of the day, attain the supreme destination.  The practitioners of Vedic rituals, who pass away during the six months of the sun’s southern course, the dark fortnight of the moon, the time of smoke, the night, attain the celestial abodes.  After enjoying celestial pleasures, they again return to the earth.  These two, bright and dark paths, always exist in this world.  The way of light leads to liberation and the way of darkness leads to rebirth.
