verses.translation: BG 12.18-19:Those, who are alike to friend and foe, equipoised in honor and dishonor, cold and heat, joy and sorrow, and are free from all unfavorable association; those who take praise and reproach alike, who are given to silent contemplation, content with what comes their way, without attachment to the place of residence, whose intellect is firmly fixed in Me, and who are full of devotion to Me, such persons are very dear to Me.

verses.slok: सम: शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयो: |शीतोष्णसुखदु:खेषु सम: सङ्गविवर्जित: || 18||तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित् |अनिकेत: स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नर: || 19||
verses.transliteration: samaḥ śhatrau cha mitre cha tathā mānāpamānayoḥśhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣhu samaḥ saṅga-vivarjitaḥtulya-nindā-stutir maunī santuṣhṭo yena kenachitaniketaḥ sthira-matir bhaktimān me priyo naraḥ
verses.translation: BG 12.18-19:Those, who are alike to friend and foe, equipoised in honor and dishonor, cold and heat, joy and sorrow, and are free from all unfavorable association; those who take praise and reproach alike, who are given to silent contemplation, content with what comes their way, without attachment to the place of residence, whose intellect is firmly fixed in Me, and who are full of devotion to Me, such persons are very dear to Me.

verses.slok: ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते |श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रिया: || 20||
verses.transliteration: ye tu dharmyāmṛitam idaṁ yathoktaṁ paryupāsateśhraddadhānā mat-paramā bhaktās te ’tīva me priyāḥ
verses.translation: BG 12.20:Those who honor this nectar of wisdom declared here, have faith in Me, and are devoted and intent on Me as the supreme goal, they are exceedingly dear to Me.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च |एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव || 1||*1
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchaprakṛitiṁ puruṣhaṁ chaiva kṣhetraṁ kṣhetra-jñam eva chaetad veditum ichchhāmi jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ cha keśhava
verses.translation: BG 13.1:Arjun said, “O Keshav, I wish to understand what areprakṛitiandpuruṣh, and what arekṣhetraandkṣhetrajña? I also wish to know what is true knowledge, and what is the goal of this knowledge?

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते |एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहु: क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विद: || 2||
verses.transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvāchaidaṁ śharīraṁ kaunteya kṣhetram ity abhidhīyateetad yo vetti taṁ prāhuḥ kṣhetra-jña iti tad-vidaḥ
verses.translation: BG 13.2:The Supreme Divine Lord said: O Arjun, this body is termed askṣhetra(the field of activities), and the one who knows this body is calledkṣhetrajña(the knower of the field) by the sages who discern the truth about both.

verses.slok: क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत |क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम || 3||
verses.transliteration: kṣhetra-jñaṁ chāpi māṁ viddhi sarva-kṣhetreṣhu bhāratakṣhetra-kṣhetrajñayor jñānaṁ yat taj jñānaṁ mataṁ mama
verses.translation: BG 13.3:O scion of Bharat, I am also the knower of all the individual fields of activity.  The understanding of the body as the field of activities, and the soul and God as the knowers of the field, this I hold to be true knowledge.

verses.slok: तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक्च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत् |स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे शृणु || 4||
verses.transliteration: tat kṣhetraṁ yach cha yādṛik cha yad-vikāri yataśh cha yatsa cha yo yat-prabhāvaśh cha tat samāsena me śhṛiṇu
verses.translation: BG 13.4:Listen and I will explain to you what that field is and what its nature is.  I will also explain how change takes place within it, from what it was created, who the knower of the field of activities is, and what his powers are.

verses.slok: ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधै: पृथक् |ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्चितै: || 5 ||
verses.transliteration: ṛiṣhibhir bahudhā gītaṁ chhandobhir vividhaiḥ pṛithakbrahma-sūtra-padaiśh chaiva hetumadbhir viniśhchitaiḥ
verses.translation: BG 13.5:Great sages have sung the truth about the field and the knower of the field in manifold ways.  It has been stated in various Vedic hymns, and especially revealed in the Brahma Sūtra, with sound logic and conclusive evidence.

verses.slok: महाभूतान्यङ्ककारो बुद्धिरव्यक्त मेव च |इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचरा: || 6||
verses.transliteration: mahā-bhūtāny ahankāro buddhir avyaktam eva chaindriyāṇi daśhaikaṁ cha pañcha chendriya-gocharāḥ
verses.translation: BG 13.6:The field of activities is composed of the five great elements, the ego, the intellect, the unmanifest primordial matter, the eleven senses (five knowledge senses, five working senses, and mind), and the five objects of the senses.

verses.slok: इच्छा द्वेष: सुखं दु:खं सङ्घातश्चेतना धृति: |एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् || 7||
verses.transliteration: ichchhā dveṣhaḥ sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ saṅghātaśh chetanā dhṛitiḥetat kṣhetraṁ samāsena sa-vikāram udāhṛitam
verses.translation: BG 13.7:Desire and aversion, happiness and misery, the body, consciousness, and the will—all these comprise the field and its modifications.

verses.slok: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् |आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रह: || 8||इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च |जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदु:खदोषानुदर्शनम् || 9||असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्ग: पुत्रदारगृहादिषु |नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु || 10||मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी |विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि || 11||अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् |एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा || 12||
verses.transliteration: amānitvam adambhitvam ahinsā kṣhāntir ārjavamāchāryopāsanaṁ śhauchaṁ sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥindriyārtheṣhu vairāgyam anahankāra eva chajanma-mṛityu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣhānudarśhanamasaktir anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛihādiṣhunityaṁ cha sama-chittatvam iṣhṭāniṣhṭopapattiṣhumayi chānanya-yogena bhaktir avyabhichāriṇīvivikta-deśha-sevitvam aratir jana-sansadiadhyātma-jñāna-nityatvaṁ tattva-jñānārtha-darśhanametaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṁ yad ato ’nyathā
verses.translation: BG 13.8-12:Humbleness; freedom from hypocrisy; non-violence; forgiveness; simplicity; service of the Guru; cleanliness of body and mind; steadfastness; and self-control; dispassion toward the objects of the senses; absence of egotism; keeping in mind the evils of birth, disease, old age, and death; non-attachment; absence of clinging to spouse, children, home, and so on; even-mindedness amidst desired and undesired events in life; constant and exclusive devotion toward Me; an inclination for solitary places and an aversion for mundane society; constancy in spiritual knowledge; and philosophical pursuit of the Absolute Truth—all these I declare to be knowledge, and what is contrary to it, I call ignorance.

verses.slok: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् |आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रह: || 8||इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च |जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदु:खदोषानुदर्शनम् || 9||असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्ग: पुत्रदारगृहादिषु |नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु || 10||मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी |विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि || 11||अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् |एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा || 12||
verses.transliteration: amānitvam adambhitvam ahinsā kṣhāntir ārjavamāchāryopāsanaṁ śhauchaṁ sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥindriyārtheṣhu vairāgyam anahankāra eva chajanma-mṛityu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣhānudarśhanamasaktir anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛihādiṣhunityaṁ cha sama-chittatvam iṣhṭāniṣhṭopapattiṣhumayi chānanya-yogena bhaktir avyabhichāriṇīvivikta-deśha-sevitvam aratir jana-sansadiadhyātma-jñāna-nityatvaṁ tattva-jñānārtha-darśhanametaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṁ yad ato ’nyathā
verses.translation: BG 13.8-12:Humbleness; freedom from hypocrisy; non-violence; forgiveness; simplicity; service of the Guru; cleanliness of body and mind; steadfastness; and self-control; dispassion toward the objects of the senses; absence of egotism; keeping in mind the evils of birth, disease, old age, and death; non-attachment; absence of clinging to spouse, children, home, and so on; even-mindedness amidst desired and undesired events in life; constant and exclusive devotion toward Me; an inclination for solitary places and an aversion for mundane society; constancy in spiritual knowledge; and philosophical pursuit of the Absolute Truth—all these I declare to be knowledge, and what is contrary to it, I call ignorance.

verses.slok: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् |आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रह: || 8||इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च |जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदु:खदोषानुदर्शनम् || 9||असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्ग: पुत्रदारगृहादिषु |नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु || 10||मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी |विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि || 11||अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् |एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा || 12||
verses.transliteration: amānitvam adambhitvam ahinsā kṣhāntir ārjavamāchāryopāsanaṁ śhauchaṁ sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥindriyārtheṣhu vairāgyam anahankāra eva chajanma-mṛityu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣhānudarśhanamasaktir anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛihādiṣhunityaṁ cha sama-chittatvam iṣhṭāniṣhṭopapattiṣhumayi chānanya-yogena bhaktir avyabhichāriṇīvivikta-deśha-sevitvam aratir jana-sansadiadhyātma-jñāna-nityatvaṁ tattva-jñānārtha-darśhanametaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṁ yad ato ’nyathā
verses.translation: BG 13.8-12:Humbleness; freedom from hypocrisy; non-violence; forgiveness; simplicity; service of the Guru; cleanliness of body and mind; steadfastness; and self-control; dispassion toward the objects of the senses; absence of egotism; keeping in mind the evils of birth, disease, old age, and death; non-attachment; absence of clinging to spouse, children, home, and so on; even-mindedness amidst desired and undesired events in life; constant and exclusive devotion toward Me; an inclination for solitary places and an aversion for mundane society; constancy in spiritual knowledge; and philosophical pursuit of the Absolute Truth—all these I declare to be knowledge, and what is contrary to it, I call ignorance.

verses.slok: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् |आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रह: || 8||इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च |जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदु:खदोषानुदर्शनम् || 9||असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्ग: पुत्रदारगृहादिषु |नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु || 10||मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी |विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि || 11||अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् |एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा || 12||
verses.transliteration: amānitvam adambhitvam ahinsā kṣhāntir ārjavamāchāryopāsanaṁ śhauchaṁ sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥindriyārtheṣhu vairāgyam anahankāra eva chajanma-mṛityu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣhānudarśhanamasaktir anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛihādiṣhunityaṁ cha sama-chittatvam iṣhṭāniṣhṭopapattiṣhumayi chānanya-yogena bhaktir avyabhichāriṇīvivikta-deśha-sevitvam aratir jana-sansadiadhyātma-jñāna-nityatvaṁ tattva-jñānārtha-darśhanametaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṁ yad ato ’nyathā
verses.translation: BG 13.8-12:Humbleness; freedom from hypocrisy; non-violence; forgiveness; simplicity; service of the Guru; cleanliness of body and mind; steadfastness; and self-control; dispassion toward the objects of the senses; absence of egotism; keeping in mind the evils of birth, disease, old age, and death; non-attachment; absence of clinging to spouse, children, home, and so on; even-mindedness amidst desired and undesired events in life; constant and exclusive devotion toward Me; an inclination for solitary places and an aversion for mundane society; constancy in spiritual knowledge; and philosophical pursuit of the Absolute Truth—all these I declare to be knowledge, and what is contrary to it, I call ignorance.

verses.slok: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् |आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रह: || 8||इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च |जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदु:खदोषानुदर्शनम् || 9||असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्ग: पुत्रदारगृहादिषु |नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु || 10||मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी |विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि || 11||अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् |एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा || 12||
verses.transliteration: amānitvam adambhitvam ahinsā kṣhāntir ārjavamāchāryopāsanaṁ śhauchaṁ sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥindriyārtheṣhu vairāgyam anahankāra eva chajanma-mṛityu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣhānudarśhanamasaktir anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛihādiṣhunityaṁ cha sama-chittatvam iṣhṭāniṣhṭopapattiṣhumayi chānanya-yogena bhaktir avyabhichāriṇīvivikta-deśha-sevitvam aratir jana-sansadiadhyātma-jñāna-nityatvaṁ tattva-jñānārtha-darśhanametaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṁ yad ato ’nyathā
verses.translation: BG 13.8-12:Humbleness; freedom from hypocrisy; non-violence; forgiveness; simplicity; service of the Guru; cleanliness of body and mind; steadfastness; and self-control; dispassion toward the objects of the senses; absence of egotism; keeping in mind the evils of birth, disease, old age, and death; non-attachment; absence of clinging to spouse, children, home, and so on; even-mindedness amidst desired and undesired events in life; constant and exclusive devotion toward Me; an inclination for solitary places and an aversion for mundane society; constancy in spiritual knowledge; and philosophical pursuit of the Absolute Truth—all these I declare to be knowledge, and what is contrary to it, I call ignorance.

verses.slok: ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्रुते |अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते || 13||
verses.transliteration: jñeyaṁ yat tat pravakṣhyāmi yaj jñātvāmṛitam aśhnuteanādi mat-paraṁ brahma na sat tan nāsad uchyate
verses.translation: BG 13.13:I shall now reveal to you that which ought to be known, and by knowing which, one attains immortality.  It is the beginningless Brahman, which lies beyond existence and non-existence.

verses.slok: सर्वत: पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम् |सर्वत: श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति || 14||
verses.transliteration: sarvataḥ pāṇi-pādaṁ tat sarvato ’kṣhi-śhiro-mukhamsarvataḥ śhrutimal loke sarvam āvṛitya tiṣhṭhati
verses.translation: BG 13.14:Everywhere are His hands and feet, eyes, heads, and faces.  His ears too are in all places, for He pervades everything in the universe.

verses.slok: सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम् |असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च || 15||
verses.transliteration: sarvendriya-guṇābhāsaṁ sarvendriya-vivarjitamasaktaṁ sarva-bhṛich chaiva nirguṇaṁ guṇa-bhoktṛi cha
verses.translation: BG 13.15:Though He perceives all sense-objects, yet He is devoid of the senses.  He is unattached to everything, and yet He is the sustainer of all.  Although He is without attributes, yet He is the enjoyer of the three modes of material nature.

verses.slok: बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च |सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत् || 16||
verses.transliteration: bahir antaśh cha bhūtānām acharaṁ charam eva chasūkṣhmatvāt tad avijñeyaṁ dūra-sthaṁ chāntike cha tat
verses.translation: BG 13.16:He exists outside and inside all living beings, those that are moving and not moving.  He is subtle, and hence, He is incomprehensible.  He is very far, but He is also very near.

verses.slok: अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम् |भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च || 17||
verses.transliteration: avibhaktaṁ cha bhūteṣhu vibhaktam iva cha sthitambhūta-bhartṛi cha taj jñeyaṁ grasiṣhṇu prabhaviṣhṇu cha
verses.translation: BG 13.17:He is indivisible, yet He appears to be divided amongst living beings.  Know the Supreme Entity to be the Sustainer, Annihilator, and Creator of all beings.

verses.slok: ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमस: परमुच्यते |ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् || 18||
verses.transliteration: jyotiṣhām api taj jyotis tamasaḥ param uchyatejñānaṁ jñeyaṁ jñāna-gamyaṁ hṛidi sarvasya viṣhṭhitam
verses.translation: BG 13.18:He is the source of light in all luminaries, and is entirely beyond the darkness of ignorance.  He is knowledge, the object of knowledge, and the goal of knowledge.  He dwells within the hearts of all living beings.

verses.slok: इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासत: |मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते || 19||
verses.transliteration: iti kṣhetraṁ tathā jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ choktaṁ samāsataḥmad-bhakta etad vijñāya mad-bhāvāyopapadyate
verses.translation: BG 13.19:I have thus revealed to you the nature of the field, the meaning of knowledge, and the object of knowledge.  Only My devotees can understand this in reality, and by doing so, they attain My divine nature.

verses.slok: प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि |विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसम्भवान् || 20||
verses.transliteration: prakṛitiṁ puruṣhaṁ chaiva viddhy anādī ubhāv apivikārānśh cha guṇānśh chaiva viddhi prakṛiti-sambhavān
verses.translation: BG 13.20:Know thatprakṛiti(material nature) andpuruṣh(the individual souls) are both beginningless. Also know that all transformations of the body and the three modes of nature are produced by material energy.

verses.slok: कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतु: प्रकृतिरुच्यते |पुरुष: सुखदु:खानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते || 21||
verses.transliteration: kārya-kāraṇa-kartṛitve hetuḥ prakṛitir uchyatepuruṣhaḥ sukha-duḥkhānāṁ bhoktṛitve hetur uchyate
verses.translation: BG 13.21:In the matter of creation, the material energy is responsible for cause and effect; in the matter of experiencing happiness and distress, the individual soul is declared responsible.

verses.slok: पुरुष: प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङक्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् |कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु || 22||
verses.transliteration: puruṣhaḥ prakṛiti-stho hi bhuṅkte prakṛiti-jān guṇānkāraṇaṁ guṇa-saṅgo ’sya sad-asad-yoni-janmasu
verses.translation: BG 13.22:When thepuruṣh(individual soul) seated inprakṛiti(the material energy) desires to enjoy the threeguṇas, attachment to them becomes the cause of its birth in superior and inferior wombs.

verses.slok: उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वर: |परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुष: पर: || 23||
verses.transliteration: upadraṣhṭānumantā cha bhartā bhoktā maheśhvaraḥparamātmeti chāpy ukto dehe ’smin puruṣhaḥ paraḥ
verses.translation: BG 13.23:Within the body also resides the Supreme Lord.  He is said to be the Witness, the Permitter, the Supporter, Transcendental Enjoyer, the ultimate Controller, and theParamātmā(Supreme Soul).

verses.slok: य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणै: सह |सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते || 24||
verses.transliteration: ya evaṁ vetti puruṣhaṁ prakṛitiṁ cha guṇaiḥ sahasarvathā vartamāno ’pi na sa bhūyo ’bhijāyate
verses.translation: BG 13.24:Those who understand the truth about Supreme Soul, the individual soul, material nature, and the interaction of the three modes of nature will not take birth here again.  They will be liberated regardless of their present condition.

verses.slok: ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना |अन्ये साङ् ख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे || 25||
verses.transliteration: dhyānenātmani paśhyanti kechid ātmānam ātmanāanye sānkhyena yogena karma-yogena chāpare
verses.translation: BG 13.25:Some try to perceive the Supreme Soul within their hearts through meditation, and others try to do so through the cultivation of knowledge, while still others strive to attain that realization by the path of action.

verses.slok: अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्त: श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते |तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणा: || 26||
verses.transliteration: anye tv evam ajānantaḥ śhrutvānyebhya upāsatete ’pi chātitaranty eva mṛityuṁ śhruti-parāyaṇāḥ
verses.translation: BG 13.26:There are still others who are unaware of these spiritual paths, but they hear from others and begin worshipping the Supreme Lord.  By such devotion to hearing from saints, they too can gradually cross over the ocean of birth and death.

verses.slok: यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम् |क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ || 27||
verses.transliteration: yāvat sañjāyate kiñchit sattvaṁ sthāvara-jaṅgamamkṣhetra-kṣhetrajña-sanyogāt tad viddhi bharatarṣhabha
verses.translation: BG 13.27:O best of the Bharatas, whatever moving or unmoving being you see in existence, know it to be a combination of the field of activities and the knower of the field.

verses.slok: समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम् |विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं य: पश्यति स पश्यति || 28||
verses.transliteration: samaṁ sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu tiṣhṭhantaṁ parameśhvaramvinaśhyatsv avinaśhyantaṁ yaḥ paśhyati sa paśhyati
verses.translation: BG 13.28:They alone truly see, who perceive theParamātmā(Supreme Soul) accompanying the soul in all beings, and who understand both to be imperishable in this perishable body.

verses.slok: समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् |न हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् || 29||
verses.transliteration: samaṁ paśhyan hi sarvatra samavasthitam īśhvaramna hinasty ātmanātmānaṁ tato yāti parāṁ gatim
verses.translation: BG 13.29:Those, who see God as the Supreme Soul equally present everywhere and in all living beings, do not degrade themselves by their mind.  Thereby, they reach the supreme destination.

verses.slok: प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वश: |य: पश्यति तथात्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति || 30||
verses.transliteration: prakṛityaiva cha karmāṇi kriyamāṇāni sarvaśhaḥyaḥ paśhyati tathātmānam akartāraṁ sa paśhyati
verses.translation: BG 13.30:They alone truly see who understand that all actions (of the body) are performed by material nature, while the embodied soul actually does nothing.

verses.slok: यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति |तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा || 31||
verses.transliteration: yadā bhūta-pṛithag-bhāvam eka-stham anupaśhyatitata eva cha vistāraṁ brahma sampadyate tadā
verses.translation: BG 13.31:When they see the diverse variety of living beings situated in the same material nature, and understand all of them to be born from it, they attain the realization of the Brahman.

verses.slok: अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्यय: |शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते || 32||
verses.transliteration: anāditvān nirguṇatvāt paramātmāyam avyayaḥśharīra-stho ’pi kaunteya na karoti na lipyate
verses.translation: BG 13.32:The Supreme Soul is imperishable, without beginning, and devoid of any material qualities, O son of Kunti.  Although situated within the body, It neither acts, nor is It tainted by material energy.

verses.slok: यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते |सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते || 33||
verses.transliteration: yathā sarva-gataṁ saukṣhmyād ākāśhaṁ nopalipyatesarvatrāvasthito dehe tathātmā nopalipyate
verses.translation: BG 13.33:Space holds everything within it, but being subtle, does not get contaminated by what it holds.  Similarly, though its consciousness pervades the body, the soul is not affected by the attributes of the body.

verses.slok: यथा प्रकाशयत्येक: कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रवि: |क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत || 34||
verses.transliteration: yathā prakāśhayaty ekaḥ kṛitsnaṁ lokam imaṁ raviḥkṣhetraṁ kṣhetrī tathā kṛitsnaṁ prakāśhayati bhārata
verses.translation: BG 13.34:Just as one sun illumines the entire solar system, so does the individual soul illumine the entire body (with consciousness).

verses.slok: क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा |भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम् || 35||
verses.transliteration: kṣhetra-kṣhetrajñayor evam antaraṁ jñāna-chakṣhuṣhābhūta-prakṛiti-mokṣhaṁ cha ye vidur yānti te param
verses.translation: BG 13.35:Those who perceive with the eyes of knowledge the difference between the body and the knower of the body, and the process of release from material nature, attain the supreme destination.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |परं भूय: प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम् |यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनय: सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गता: || 1||
verses.transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvāchaparaṁ bhūyaḥ pravakṣhyāmi jñānānāṁ jñānam uttamamyaj jñātvā munayaḥ sarve parāṁ siddhim ito gatāḥ
verses.translation: BG 14.1:The Divine Lord said: I shall once again explain to you the supreme wisdom, the best of all knowledge; by knowing which, all the great saints attained the highest perfection.

verses.slok: इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागता: |सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च || 2||
verses.transliteration: idaṁ jñānam upāśhritya mama sādharmyam āgatāḥsarge ’pi nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti cha
verses.translation: BG 14.2:Those who take refuge in this wisdom will be united with Me. They will not be reborn at the time of creation nor destroyed at the time of dissolution.

verses.slok: मम योनिर्महद् ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम् |सम्भव: सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत || 3||सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तय: सम्भवन्ति या: |तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रद: पिता || 4||
verses.transliteration: mama yonir mahad brahma tasmin garbhaṁ dadhāmy ahamsambhavaḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ tato bhavati bhāratasarva-yoniṣhu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥtāsāṁ brahma mahad yonir ahaṁ bīja-pradaḥ pitā
verses.translation: BG 14.3-4:The total material substance,prakṛiti, is the womb. I impregnate it with the individual souls, and thus all living beings are born. O son of Kunti, for all species of life that are produced, the material nature is the womb, and I am the seed-giving Father.

verses.slok: मम योनिर्महद् ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम् |सम्भव: सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत || 3||सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तय: सम्भवन्ति या: |तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रद: पिता || 4||
verses.transliteration: mama yonir mahad brahma tasmin garbhaṁ dadhāmy ahamsambhavaḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ tato bhavati bhāratasarva-yoniṣhu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥtāsāṁ brahma mahad yonir ahaṁ bīja-pradaḥ pitā
verses.translation: BG 14.3-4:The total material substance,prakṛiti, is the womb. I impregnate it with the individual souls, and thus all living beings are born. O son of Kunti, for all species of life that are produced, the material nature is the womb, and I am the seed-giving Father.

verses.slok: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणा: प्रकृतिसम्भवा: |निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् || 5||
verses.transliteration: sattvaṁ rajas tama iti guṇāḥ prakṛiti-sambhavāḥnibadhnanti mahā-bāho dehe dehinam avyayam
verses.translation: BG 14.5:O mighty-armed Arjun, the material energy consists of threeguṇas(modes)—sattva(goodness),rajas(passion), andtamas(ignorance). These modes bind the eternal soul to the perishable body.

verses.slok: तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम् |सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ || 6||
verses.transliteration: tatra sattvaṁ nirmalatvāt prakāśhakam anāmayamsukha-saṅgena badhnāti jñāna-saṅgena chānagha
verses.translation: BG 14.6:Amongst these,sattva guṇa, the mode of goodness, being purer than the others, is illuminating and full of well-being. O sinless one, it binds the soul by creating attachment for a sense of happiness and knowledge.

verses.slok: रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम् |तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् || 7||
verses.transliteration: rajo rāgātmakaṁ viddhi tṛiṣhṇā-saṅga-samudbhavamtan nibadhnāti kaunteya karma-saṅgena dehinam
verses.translation: BG 14.7:O Arjun,rajo guṇais of the nature of passion. It arises from worldly desires and affections, and binds the soul through attachment to fruitive actions.

verses.slok: तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम् |प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत || 8||
verses.transliteration: tamas tv ajñāna-jaṁ viddhi mohanaṁ sarva-dehināmpramādālasya-nidrābhis tan nibadhnāti bhārata
verses.translation: BG 14.8:O Arjun,tamo guṇa, which is born of ignorance, is the cause of illusion for the embodied souls. It deludes all living beings through negligence, laziness, and sleep.

verses.slok: सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रज: कर्मणि भारत |ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तम: प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत || 9||
verses.transliteration: sattvaṁ sukhe sañjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhāratajñānam āvṛitya tu tamaḥ pramāde sañjayaty uta
verses.translation: BG 14.9:Sattvabinds one to material happiness;rajasconditions the soul toward actions; andtamasclouds wisdom and binds one to delusion.

verses.slok: रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत |रज: सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तम: सत्त्वं रजस्तथा || 10||
verses.transliteration: rajas tamaśh chābhibhūya sattvaṁ bhavati bhāratarajaḥ sattvaṁ tamaśh chaiva tamaḥ sattvaṁ rajas tathā
verses.translation: BG 14.10:Sometimes goodness (sattva) prevails over passion (rajas) and ignorance (tamas), O scion of Bharat. Sometimes passion (rajas) dominates goodness (sattva) and ignorance (tamas), and at other times ignorance (tamas) overcomes goodness (sattva) and passion (rajas).

verses.slok: सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते |ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत || 11||लोभ: प्रवृत्तिरारम्भ: कर्मणामशम: स्पृहा |रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ || 12||अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च |तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन || 13||
verses.transliteration: sarva-dvāreṣhu dehe ’smin prakāśha upajāyatejñānaṁ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛiddhaṁ sattvam ity utalobhaḥ pravṛittir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśhamaḥ spṛihārajasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe bharatarṣhabhaaprakāśho ’pravṛittiśh cha pramādo moha eva chatamasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe kuru-nandana
verses.translation: BG 14.11-13:When all the gates of the body are illumined by knowledge, know it to be a manifestation of the mode of goodness. When the mode of passion predominates, O Arjun, the symptoms of greed, exertion for worldly gain, restlessness, and craving develop. O Arjun, nescience, inertia, negligence, and delusion—these are the dominant signs of the mode of ignorance.

verses.slok: सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते |ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत || 11||लोभ: प्रवृत्तिरारम्भ: कर्मणामशम: स्पृहा |रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ || 12||अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च |तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन || 13||
verses.transliteration: sarva-dvāreṣhu dehe ’smin prakāśha upajāyatejñānaṁ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛiddhaṁ sattvam ity utalobhaḥ pravṛittir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśhamaḥ spṛihārajasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe bharatarṣhabhaaprakāśho ’pravṛittiśh cha pramādo moha eva chatamasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe kuru-nandana
verses.translation: BG 14.11-13:When all the gates of the body are illumined by knowledge, know it to be a manifestation of the mode of goodness. When the mode of passion predominates, O Arjun, the symptoms of greed, exertion for worldly gain, restlessness, and craving develop. O Arjun, nescience, inertia, negligence, and delusion—these are the dominant signs of the mode of ignorance.

verses.slok: सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते |ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत || 11||लोभ: प्रवृत्तिरारम्भ: कर्मणामशम: स्पृहा |रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ || 12||अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च |तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन || 13||
verses.transliteration: sarva-dvāreṣhu dehe ’smin prakāśha upajāyatejñānaṁ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛiddhaṁ sattvam ity utalobhaḥ pravṛittir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśhamaḥ spṛihārajasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe bharatarṣhabhaaprakāśho ’pravṛittiśh cha pramādo moha eva chatamasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe kuru-nandana
verses.translation: BG 14.11-13:When all the gates of the body are illumined by knowledge, know it to be a manifestation of the mode of goodness. When the mode of passion predominates, O Arjun, the symptoms of greed, exertion for worldly gain, restlessness, and craving develop. O Arjun, nescience, inertia, negligence, and delusion—these are the dominant signs of the mode of ignorance.

verses.slok: यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् |तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते || 14||रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते |तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते || 15||
verses.transliteration: yadā sattve pravṛiddhe tu pralayaṁ yāti deha-bhṛittadottama-vidāṁ lokān amalān pratipadyaterajasi pralayaṁ gatvā karma-saṅgiṣhu jāyatetathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍha-yoniṣhu jāyate
verses.translation: BG 14.14-15:Those who die with predominance ofsattvareach the pure abodes (which are free fromrajasandtamas) of the learned. Those who die with prevalence of the mode of passion are born among people driven by work, while those dying in the mode of ignorance take birth in the animal kingdom.

verses.slok: यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् |तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते || 14||रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते |तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते || 15||
verses.transliteration: yadā sattve pravṛiddhe tu pralayaṁ yāti deha-bhṛittadottama-vidāṁ lokān amalān pratipadyaterajasi pralayaṁ gatvā karma-saṅgiṣhu jāyatetathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍha-yoniṣhu jāyate
verses.translation: BG 14.14-15:Those who die with predominance ofsattvareach the pure abodes (which are free fromrajasandtamas) of the learned. Those who die with prevalence of the mode of passion are born among people driven by work, while those dying in the mode of ignorance take birth in the animal kingdom.

verses.slok: कर्मण: सुकृतस्याहु: सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम् |रजसस्तु फलं दु:खमज्ञानं तमस: फलम् || 16||
verses.transliteration: karmaṇaḥ sukṛitasyāhuḥ sāttvikaṁ nirmalaṁ phalamrajasas tu phalaṁ duḥkham ajñānaṁ tamasaḥ phalam
verses.translation: BG 14.16:It is said the fruit of actions performed in the mode of goodness bestow pure results. Actions done in the mode of passion result in pain, while those performed in the mode of ignorance result in darkness.

verses.slok: सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च |प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च || 17||
verses.transliteration: sattvāt sañjāyate jñānaṁ rajaso lobha eva chapramāda-mohau tamaso bhavato ’jñānam eva cha
verses.translation: BG 14.17:From the mode of goodness arises knowledge, from the mode of passion arises greed, and from the mode of ignorance arise negligence and delusion.

verses.slok: ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसा: |जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसा: || 18||
verses.transliteration: ūrdhvaṁ gachchhanti sattva-sthā madhye tiṣhṭhanti rājasāḥjaghanya-guṇa-vṛitti-sthā adho gachchhanti tāmasāḥ
verses.translation: BG 14.18:Those situated in the mode of goodness rise upward; those in the mode of passion stay in the middle; and those in the mode of ignorance go downward.

verses.slok: नान्यं गुणेभ्य: कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति |गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति || 19||
verses.transliteration: nānyaṁ guṇebhyaḥ kartāraṁ yadā draṣhṭānupaśhyatiguṇebhyaśh cha paraṁ vetti mad-bhāvaṁ so ’dhigachchhati
verses.translation: BG 14.19:When wise persons see that in all work there is no agent of action other than the threeguṇas, and they know Me to be transcendental to theseguṇas, they attain My divine nature.

verses.slok: गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भवान् |जन्ममृत्युजरादु:खैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्रुते || 20||
verses.transliteration: guṇān etān atītya trīn dehī deha-samudbhavānjanma-mṛityu-jarā-duḥkhair vimukto ’mṛitam aśhnute
verses.translation: BG 14.20:By transcending the three modes of material nature associated with the body, one becomes free from birth, death, old age, and misery, and attains immortality.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो |किमाचार: कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते || 21||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchakair liṅgais trīn guṇān etān atīto bhavati prabhokim āchāraḥ kathaṁ chaitāns trīn guṇān ativartate
verses.translation: BG 14.21:Arjun inquired: What are the characteristics of those who have gone beyond the threeguṇas, O Lord? How do they act? How do they go beyond the bondage of theguṇas?

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव |न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ् क्षति || 22||उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते |गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते || 23||
verses.transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvāchaprakāśhaṁ cha pravṛittiṁ cha moham eva cha pāṇḍavana dveṣhṭi sampravṛittāni na nivṛittāni kāṅkṣhatiudāsīna-vad āsīno guṇair yo na vichālyateguṇā vartanta ity evaṁ yo ’vatiṣhṭhati neṅgate
verses.translation: BG 14.22-23:The Supreme Divine Personality said: O Arjun, The persons who are transcendental to the threeguṇasneither hate illumination (which is born ofsattva), nor activity (which is born ofrajas), nor even delusion (which is born oftamas), when these are abundantly present, nor do they long for them when they are absent. They remain neutral to the modes of nature and are not disturbed by them. Knowing it is only theguṇasthat act, they stay established in the self, without wavering.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव |न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ् क्षति || 22||उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते |गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते || 23||
verses.transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvāchaprakāśhaṁ cha pravṛittiṁ cha moham eva cha pāṇḍavana dveṣhṭi sampravṛittāni na nivṛittāni kāṅkṣhatiudāsīna-vad āsīno guṇair yo na vichālyateguṇā vartanta ity evaṁ yo ’vatiṣhṭhati neṅgate
verses.translation: BG 14.22-23:The Supreme Divine Personality said: O Arjun, The persons who are transcendental to the threeguṇasneither hate illumination (which is born ofsattva), nor activity (which is born ofrajas), nor even delusion (which is born oftamas), when these are abundantly present, nor do they long for them when they are absent. They remain neutral to the modes of nature and are not disturbed by them. Knowing it is only theguṇasthat act, they stay established in the self, without wavering.

verses.slok: समदु:खसुख: स्वस्थ: समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चन: |तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुति: || 24||मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयो: |सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीत: स उच्यते || 25||
verses.transliteration: sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ sva-sthaḥ sama-loṣhṭāśhma-kāñchanaḥtulya-priyāpriyo dhīras tulya-nindātma-sanstutiḥ
verses.translation: BG 14.24-25:Those who are alike in happiness and distress; who are established in the self; who look upon a clod, a stone, and a piece of gold as of equal value; who remain the same amidst pleasant and unpleasant events; who are intelligent; who accept both blame and praise with equanimity; who remain the same in honor and dishonor; who treat both friend and foe alike; and who have abandoned all enterprises – they are said to have risen above the threeguṇas.

verses.slok: समदु:खसुख: स्वस्थ: समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चन: |तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुति: || 24||मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयो: |सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीत: स उच्यते || 25||
verses.transliteration: sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ sva-sthaḥ sama-loṣhṭāśhma-kāñchanaḥtulya-priyāpriyo dhīras tulya-nindātma-sanstutiḥ
verses.translation: BG 14.24-25:Those who are alike in happiness and distress; who are established in the self; who look upon a clod, a stone, and a piece of gold as of equal value; who remain the same amidst pleasant and unpleasant events; who are intelligent; who accept both blame and praise with equanimity; who remain the same in honor and dishonor; who treat both friend and foe alike; and who have abandoned all enterprises – they are said to have risen above the threeguṇas.

verses.slok: मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते |स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते || 26||
verses.transliteration: māṁ cha yo ’vyabhichāreṇa bhakti-yogena sevatesa guṇān samatītyaitān brahma-bhūyāya kalpate
verses.translation: BG 14.26:Those who serve Me with unalloyed devotion rise above the three modes of material nature and come to the level of the Brahman.

verses.slok: ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च |शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च || 27||
verses.transliteration: brahmaṇo hi pratiṣhṭhāham amṛitasyāvyayasya chaśhāśhvatasya cha dharmasya sukhasyaikāntikasya cha
verses.translation: BG 14.27:I am the basis of the formless Brahman, the immortal and imperishable, of eternal dharma, and of unending divine bliss.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |ऊर्ध्वमूलमध:शाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम् |छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् || 1||
verses.transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvāchaūrdhva-mūlam adhaḥ-śhākham aśhvatthaṁ prāhur avyayamchhandānsi yasya parṇāni yas taṁ veda sa veda-vit
verses.translation: BG 15.1:The Supreme Divine Personality said: They speak of an eternalaśhvatthtree with its roots above and branches below.  Its leaves are the Vedic hymns, and one who knows the secret of this tree is the knower of the Vedas.

verses.slok: अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखागुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवाला: |अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानिकर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके || 2||
verses.transliteration: adhaśh chordhvaṁ prasṛitās tasya śhākhāguṇa-pravṛiddhā viṣhaya-pravālāḥadhaśh cha mūlāny anusantatānikarmānubandhīni manuṣhya-loke
verses.translation: BG 15.2:The branches of the tree extend upward and downward, nourished by the threeguṇas, with the objects of the senses as tender buds.  The roots of the tree hang downward, causing the flow of karma in the human form.  Below, its roots branch out causing (karmic) actions in the world of humans.

verses.slok: न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यतेनान्तो न चादिर्न च सम्प्रतिष्ठा |अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूलमसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा || 3||तत: पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्यंयस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूय: |तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्येयत: प्रवृत्ति: प्रसृता पुराणी || 4||
verses.transliteration: na rūpam asyeha tathopalabhyatenānto na chādir na cha sampratiṣhṭhāaśhvattham enaṁ su-virūḍha-mūlamasaṅga-śhastreṇa dṛiḍhena chhittvātataḥ padaṁ tat parimārgitavyaṁyasmin gatā na nivartanti bhūyaḥtam eva chādyaṁ puruṣhaṁ prapadyeyataḥ pravṛittiḥ prasṛitā purāṇī
verses.translation: BG 15.3-4:The real form of this tree is not perceived in this world, neither its beginning nor end, nor its continued existence.  But this deep-rooted aśhvatth tree must be cut down with a strong axe of detachment.  Then one must search out the base of the tree, which is the Supreme Lord, from whom streamed forth the activity of the universe a long time ago.  Upon taking refuge in Him, one will not return to this world again.

verses.slok: न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यतेनान्तो न चादिर्न च सम्प्रतिष्ठा |अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूलमसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा || 3||तत: पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्यंयस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूय: |तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्येयत: प्रवृत्ति: प्रसृता पुराणी || 4||
verses.transliteration: na rūpam asyeha tathopalabhyatenānto na chādir na cha sampratiṣhṭhāaśhvattham enaṁ su-virūḍha-mūlamasaṅga-śhastreṇa dṛiḍhena chhittvātataḥ padaṁ tat parimārgitavyaṁyasmin gatā na nivartanti bhūyaḥtam eva chādyaṁ puruṣhaṁ prapadyeyataḥ pravṛittiḥ prasṛitā purāṇī
verses.translation: BG 15.3-4:The real form of this tree is not perceived in this world, neither its beginning nor end, nor its continued existence.  But this deep-rooted aśhvatth tree must be cut down with a strong axe of detachment.  Then one must search out the base of the tree, which is the Supreme Lord, from whom streamed forth the activity of the universe a long time ago.  Upon taking refuge in Him, one will not return to this world again.

verses.slok: निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषाअध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामा: |द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ता: सुखदु:खसंज्ञैर्गच्छन्त्यमूढा: पदमव्ययं तत् || 5||
verses.transliteration: nirmāna-mohā jita-saṅga-doṣhāadhyātma-nityā vinivṛitta-kāmāḥdvandvair vimuktāḥ sukha-duḥkha-sanjñairgachchhanty amūḍhāḥ padam avyayaṁ tat
verses.translation: BG 15.5:Those who are free from vanity and delusion, who have overcome the evil of attachment, who dwell constantly on the self and on God, who are free from the desire to enjoy the senses, and are beyond the dualities of pleasure and pain, such liberated personalities attain My eternal Abode.

verses.slok: न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावक: |यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम || 6||
verses.transliteration: na tad bhāsayate sūryo na śhaśhāṅko na pāvakaḥyad gatvā na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṁ mama
verses.translation: BG 15.6:Neither the sun nor the moon, nor fire can illumine that Supreme Abode of Mine.  Having gone There, one does not return to this material world again.

verses.slok: ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूत: सनातन: |मन:षष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति || 7||
verses.transliteration: mamaivānśho jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥmanaḥ-ṣhaṣhṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛiti-sthāni karṣhati
verses.translation: BG 15.7:The embodied souls in this material world are My eternal fragmental parts.  But bound by material nature, they are struggling with the six senses including the mind.

verses.slok: शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वर: |गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् || 8||
verses.transliteration: śharīraṁ yad avāpnoti yach chāpy utkrāmatīśhvaraḥgṛihītvaitāni sanyāti vāyur gandhān ivāśhayāt
verses.translation: BG 15.8:As the air carries fragrance from place to place, so does the embodied soul carry the mind and senses with it, when it leaves an old body and enters a new one.

verses.slok: श्रोत्रं चक्षु: स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च |अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते || 9||
verses.transliteration: śhrotraṁ chakṣhuḥ sparśhanaṁ cha rasanaṁ ghrāṇam eva chaadhiṣhṭhāya manaśh chāyaṁ viṣhayān upasevate
verses.translation: BG 15.9:Using the sense perceptions of the ears, eyes, skin, tongue, and nose, which are grouped around the mind, the embodied soul savors the objects of the senses.

verses.slok: उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम् |विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुष: || 10||
verses.transliteration: utkrāmantaṁ sthitaṁ vāpi bhuñjānaṁ vā guṇānvitamvimūḍhā nānupaśhyanti paśhyanti jñāna-chakṣhuṣhaḥ
verses.translation: BG 15.10:The ignorant do not perceive the soul as it resides in the body, and as it enjoys sense objects; nor do they perceive it when it departs.  But those who possess the eyes of knowledge can behold it.

verses.slok: यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम् |यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतस: || 11||
verses.transliteration: yatanto yoginaśh chainaṁ paśhyanty ātmany avasthitamyatanto ‘py akṛitātmāno nainaṁ paśhyanty achetasaḥ
verses.translation: BG 15.11:Striving yogis too are able to realize the soul enshrined in the body.  However, those whose minds are not purified cannot cognize it, even though they strive to do so.

verses.slok: यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम् |यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम् || 12||
verses.transliteration: yad āditya-gataṁ tejo jagad bhāsayate ’khilamyach chandramasi yach chāgnau tat tejo viddhi māmakam
verses.translation: BG 15.12:Know that I am like the brilliance of the sun that illuminates the entire solar system.  The radiance of the moon and the brightness of the fire also come from Me.

verses.slok: गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा |पुष्णामि चौषधी: सर्वा: सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मक: || 13||
verses.transliteration: gām āviśhya cha bhūtāni dhārayāmy aham ojasāpuṣhṇāmi chauṣhadhīḥ sarvāḥ somo bhūtvā rasātmakaḥ
verses.translation: BG 15.13:Permeating the earth, I nourish all living beings with My energy.  Becoming the moon, I nourish all plants with the juice of life.

verses.slok: अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रित: |प्राणापानसमायुक्त: पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम् || 14||
verses.transliteration: ahaṁ vaiśhvānaro bhūtvā prāṇināṁ deham āśhritaḥprāṇāpāna-samāyuktaḥ pachāmy annaṁ chatur-vidham
verses.translation: BG 15.14:It is I who take the form of the fire of digestion in the stomachs of all living beings, and combine with the incoming and outgoing breaths, to digest and assimilate the four kinds of foods.

verses.slok: सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टोमत्त: स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च |वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्योवेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम् || 15||
verses.transliteration: sarvasya chāhaṁ hṛidi sanniviṣhṭomattaḥ smṛitir jñānam apohanaṁ chavedaiśh cha sarvair aham eva vedyovedānta-kṛid veda-vid eva chāham
verses.translation: BG 15.15:I am seated in the hearts of all living beings, and from Me come memory, knowledge, as well as forgetfulness.  I alone am to be known by all the Vedas, am the author of the Vedant, and the knower of the meaning of the Vedas.

verses.slok: द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च |क्षर: सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते || 16||
verses.transliteration: dvāv imau puruṣhau loke kṣharaśh chākṣhara eva chakṣharaḥ sarvāṇi bhūtāni kūṭa-stho ’kṣhara uchyate
verses.translation: BG 15.16:There are two kinds of beings in creation, thekṣhar(perishable) and theakṣhar(imperishable).  The perishable are all beings in the material realm.  The imperishable are the the liberated beings.

verses.slok: उत्तम: पुरुषस्त्वन्य: परमात्मेत्युदाहृत: |यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वर: || 17||
verses.transliteration: uttamaḥ puruṣhas tv anyaḥ paramātmety udāhṛitaḥyo loka-trayam āviśhya bibharty avyaya īśhvaraḥ
verses.translation: BG 15.17:Besides these, is the Supreme Divine Personality, who is the indestructible Supreme Soul.  He enters the three worlds as the unchanging Controller and supports all living beings.

verses.slok: यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तम: |अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथित: पुरुषोत्तम: || 18||
verses.transliteration: yasmāt kṣharam atīto ’ham akṣharād api chottamaḥato ’smi loke vede cha prathitaḥ puruṣhottamaḥ
verses.translation: BG 15.18:I am transcendental to the perishable world of matter, and even to the imperishable soul; hence I am celebrated, both in the Vedas and theSmṛitis, as the Supreme Divine Personality.

verses.slok: यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम् |स सर्वविद्भजति मां सर्वभावेन भारत || 19||
verses.transliteration: yo mām evam asammūḍho jānāti puruṣhottamamsa sarva-vid bhajati māṁ sarva-bhāvena bhārata
verses.translation: BG 15.19:Those who know Me without doubt as the Supreme Divine Personality truly have complete knowledge.  O Arjun, they worship Me with their whole being.

verses.slok: इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयानघ |एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत || 20||
verses.transliteration: iti guhyatamaṁ śhāstram idam uktaṁ mayānaghaetad buddhvā buddhimān syāt kṛita-kṛityaśh cha bhārata
verses.translation: BG 15.20:I have shared this most secret principle of the Vedic scriptures with you, O sinless Arjun.  By understanding this, a person becomes enlightened, and fulfills all that is to be accomplished.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थिति: |दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् || 1||अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्याग: शान्तिरपैशुनम् |दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् || 2||तेज: क्षमा धृति: शौचमद्रोहोनातिमानिता |भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत || 3||
verses.transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvāchaabhayaṁ sattva-sanśhuddhir jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥdānaṁ damaśh cha yajñaśh cha svādhyāyas tapa ārjavamahinsā satyam akrodhas tyāgaḥ śhāntir apaiśhunamdayā bhūteṣhv aloluptvaṁ mārdavaṁ hrīr achāpalamtejaḥ kṣhamā dhṛitiḥ śhaucham adroho nāti-mānitābhavanti sampadaṁ daivīm abhijātasya bhārata
verses.translation: BG 16.1-3:The Supreme Divine Personality said: O scion of Bharat, these are the saintly virtues of those endowed with a divine nature—fearlessness, purity of mind, steadfastness in spiritual knowledge, charity, control of the senses, sacrifice, study of the sacred books, austerity, and straightforwardness; non-violence, truthfulness, absence of anger, renunciation, peacefulness, restraint from fault-finding, compassion toward all living beings, absence of covetousness, gentleness, modesty, and lack of fickleness; vigor, forgiveness, fortitude, cleanliness, bearing enmity toward none, and absence of vanity.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थिति: |दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् || 1||अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्याग: शान्तिरपैशुनम् |दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् || 2||तेज: क्षमा धृति: शौचमद्रोहोनातिमानिता |भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत || 3||
verses.transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvāchaabhayaṁ sattva-sanśhuddhir jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥdānaṁ damaśh cha yajñaśh cha svādhyāyas tapa ārjavamahinsā satyam akrodhas tyāgaḥ śhāntir apaiśhunamdayā bhūteṣhv aloluptvaṁ mārdavaṁ hrīr achāpalamtejaḥ kṣhamā dhṛitiḥ śhaucham adroho nāti-mānitābhavanti sampadaṁ daivīm abhijātasya bhārata
verses.translation: BG 16.1-3:The Supreme Divine Personality said: O scion of Bharat, these are the saintly virtues of those endowed with a divine nature—fearlessness, purity of mind, steadfastness in spiritual knowledge, charity, control of the senses, sacrifice, study of the sacred books, austerity, and straightforwardness; non-violence, truthfulness, absence of anger, renunciation, peacefulness, restraint from fault-finding, compassion toward all living beings, absence of covetousness, gentleness, modesty, and lack of fickleness; vigor, forgiveness, fortitude, cleanliness, bearing enmity toward none, and absence of vanity.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थिति: |दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् || 1||अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्याग: शान्तिरपैशुनम् |दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् || 2||तेज: क्षमा धृति: शौचमद्रोहोनातिमानिता |भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत || 3||
verses.transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvāchaabhayaṁ sattva-sanśhuddhir jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥdānaṁ damaśh cha yajñaśh cha svādhyāyas tapa ārjavamahinsā satyam akrodhas tyāgaḥ śhāntir apaiśhunamdayā bhūteṣhv aloluptvaṁ mārdavaṁ hrīr achāpalamtejaḥ kṣhamā dhṛitiḥ śhaucham adroho nāti-mānitābhavanti sampadaṁ daivīm abhijātasya bhārata
verses.translation: BG 16.1-3:The Supreme Divine Personality said: O scion of Bharat, these are the saintly virtues of those endowed with a divine nature—fearlessness, purity of mind, steadfastness in spiritual knowledge, charity, control of the senses, sacrifice, study of the sacred books, austerity, and straightforwardness; non-violence, truthfulness, absence of anger, renunciation, peacefulness, restraint from fault-finding, compassion toward all living beings, absence of covetousness, gentleness, modesty, and lack of fickleness; vigor, forgiveness, fortitude, cleanliness, bearing enmity toward none, and absence of vanity.

verses.slok: दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोध: पारुष्यमेव च |अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् || 4||
verses.transliteration: dambho darpo ’bhimānaśh cha krodhaḥ pāruṣhyam eva chaajñānaṁ chābhijātasya pārtha sampadam āsurīm
verses.translation: BG 16.4:O Parth, the qualities of those who possess a demoniac nature are hypocrisy, arrogance, conceit, anger, harshness, and ignorance.

verses.slok: दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता |मा शुच: सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव || 5||
verses.transliteration: daivī sampad vimokṣhāya nibandhāyāsurī matāmā śhuchaḥ sampadaṁ daivīm abhijāto ’si pāṇḍava
verses.translation: BG 16.5:The divine qualities lead to liberation, while the demoniac qualities are the cause for a continuing destiny of bondage. Grieve not, O Arjun, as you were born with saintly virtues.

verses.slok: द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन्दैव आसुर एव च |दैवो विस्तरश: प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे शृणु || 6||
verses.transliteration: dvau bhūta-sargau loke ’smin daiva āsura eva chadaivo vistaraśhaḥ prokta āsuraṁ pārtha me śhṛiṇu
verses.translation: BG 16.6:There are two kinds of beings in this world—those endowed with a divine nature and those possessing a demoniac nature. I have described the divine qualities in detail, O Arjun. Now hear from me about the demoniac nature.

verses.slok: प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुरा: |न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते || 7||
verses.transliteration: pravṛittiṁ cha nivṛittiṁ cha janā na vidur āsurāḥna śhauchaṁ nāpi chāchāro na satyaṁ teṣhu vidyate
verses.translation: BG 16.7:Those possessing a demoniac nature do not comprehend which actions are proper and which are improper. Hence, they possess neither purity, nor good conduct, nor even truthfulness.

verses.slok: असत्यमप्रतिष्ठं ते जगदाहुरनीश्वरम् |अपरस्परसम्भूतं किमन्यत्कामहैतुकम् || 8||
verses.transliteration: asatyam apratiṣhṭhaṁ te jagad āhur anīśhvaramaparaspara-sambhūtaṁ kim anyat kāma-haitukam
verses.translation: BG 16.8:They say, “The world is without Absolute Truth, without any basis (for moral order), and without a God (who has created or is controlling it). It is created from the combination of the two sexes, and has no purpose other than sexual gratification.”

verses.slok: एतां दृष्टिमवष्टभ्य नष्टात्मानोऽल्पबुद्धय: |प्रभवन्त्युग्रकर्माण: क्षयाय जगतोऽहिता: || 9||
verses.transliteration: etāṁ dṛiṣhṭim avaṣhṭabhya naṣhṭātmāno ’lpa-buddhayaḥprabhavanty ugra-karmāṇaḥ kṣhayāya jagato ’hitāḥ
verses.translation: BG 16.9:Holding fast to such views, these misdirected souls, with small intellect and cruel actions, arise as enemies of the world threatening its destruction.

verses.slok: काममाश्रित्य दुष्पूरं दम्भमानमदान्विता: |मोहाद्गृहीत्वासद्ग्राहान्प्रवर्तन्तेऽशुचिव्रता: || 10||
verses.transliteration: kāmam āśhritya duṣhpūraṁ dambha-māna-madānvitāḥmohād gṛihītvāsad-grāhān pravartante ’śhuchi-vratāḥ
verses.translation: BG 16.10:Harboring insatiable lust, full of hypocrisy, pride and arrogance, the demoniac cling to their false tenets. Thus illusioned, they are attracted to the impermanent and work with impure resolve.

verses.slok: चिन्तामपरिमेयां च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिता: |कामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्चिता: || 11||
verses.transliteration: chintām aparimeyāṁ cha pralayāntām upāśhritāḥkāmopabhoga-paramā etāvad iti niśhchitāḥ
verses.translation: BG 16.11:They are obsessed with endless anxieties that end only with death. Still, they maintain with complete assurance that gratification of desires and accumulation of wealth is the highest purpose of life.

verses.slok: आशापाशशतैर्बद्धा: कामक्रोधपरायणा: |ईहन्ते कामभोगार्थमन्यायेनार्थसञ्जयान् || 12||
verses.transliteration: āśhā-pāśha-śhatair baddhāḥ kāma-krodha-parāyaṇāḥīhante kāma-bhogārtham anyāyenārtha-sañchayān
verses.translation: BG 16.12:Held in bondage by hundreds of desires, and driven by lust and anger, they strive to accumulate wealth by unjust means, all for the gratification of their senses.

verses.slok: इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम् |इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् || 13||असौ मया हत: शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि |ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी || 14||आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया |यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिता: || 15||
verses.transliteration: idam adya mayā labdham imaṁ prāpsye manorathamidam astīdam api me bhaviṣhyati punar dhanamasau mayā hataḥ śhatrur haniṣhye chāparān apiīśhvaro ’ham ahaṁ bhogī siddho ’haṁ balavān sukhīāḍhyo ’bhijanavān asmi ko ’nyo ’sti sadṛiśho mayāyakṣhye dāsyāmi modiṣhya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ
verses.translation: BG 16.13-15:The demoniac persons think, “I have gained so much wealth today, and I shall now fulfill this desire of mine. This is mine, and tomorrow I shall have even more. That enemy has been destroyed by me, and I shall destroy the others too! I am like God Himself, I am the enjoyer, I am perfect, I am powerful, and I am happy. I am wealthy and I have highly placed relatives. Who else is equal to me? I shall perform sacrifices (to the celestial gods); I shall give alms; I shall rejoice.” In this way, they are deluded by ignorance.

verses.slok: इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम् |इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् || 13||असौ मया हत: शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि |ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी || 14||आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया |यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिता: || 15||
verses.transliteration: idam adya mayā labdham imaṁ prāpsye manorathamidam astīdam api me bhaviṣhyati punar dhanamasau mayā hataḥ śhatrur haniṣhye chāparān apiīśhvaro ’ham ahaṁ bhogī siddho ’haṁ balavān sukhīāḍhyo ’bhijanavān asmi ko ’nyo ’sti sadṛiśho mayāyakṣhye dāsyāmi modiṣhya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ
verses.translation: BG 16.13-15:The demoniac persons think, “I have gained so much wealth today, and I shall now fulfill this desire of mine. This is mine, and tomorrow I shall have even more. That enemy has been destroyed by me, and I shall destroy the others too! I am like God Himself, I am the enjoyer, I am perfect, I am powerful, and I am happy. I am wealthy and I have highly placed relatives. Who else is equal to me? I shall perform sacrifices (to the celestial gods); I shall give alms; I shall rejoice.” In this way, they are deluded by ignorance.

verses.slok: इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम् |इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् || 13||असौ मया हत: शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि |ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी || 14||आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया |यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिता: || 15||
verses.transliteration: idam adya mayā labdham imaṁ prāpsye manorathamidam astīdam api me bhaviṣhyati punar dhanamasau mayā hataḥ śhatrur haniṣhye chāparān apiīśhvaro ’ham ahaṁ bhogī siddho ’haṁ balavān sukhīāḍhyo ’bhijanavān asmi ko ’nyo ’sti sadṛiśho mayāyakṣhye dāsyāmi modiṣhya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ
verses.translation: BG 16.13-15:The demoniac persons think, “I have gained so much wealth today, and I shall now fulfill this desire of mine. This is mine, and tomorrow I shall have even more. That enemy has been destroyed by me, and I shall destroy the others too! I am like God Himself, I am the enjoyer, I am perfect, I am powerful, and I am happy. I am wealthy and I have highly placed relatives. Who else is equal to me? I shall perform sacrifices (to the celestial gods); I shall give alms; I shall rejoice.” In this way, they are deluded by ignorance.

verses.slok: अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृता: |प्रसक्ता: कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरकेऽशुचौ || 16||
verses.transliteration: aneka-chitta-vibhrāntā moha-jāla-samāvṛitāḥprasaktāḥ kāma-bhogeṣhu patanti narake ’śhuchau
verses.translation: BG 16.16:Possessed and led astray by such imaginings, enveloped in a mesh of delusion, and addicted to the gratification of sensuous pleasures, they descend to the murkiest hell.

verses.slok: आत्मसम्भाविता: स्तब्धा धनमानमदान्विता: |यजन्ते नामयज्ञैस्ते दम्भेनाविधिपूर्वकम् || 17||
verses.transliteration: ātma-sambhāvitāḥ stabdhā dhana-māna-madānvitāḥyajante nāma-yajñais te dambhenāvidhi-pūrvakam
verses.translation: BG 16.17:Such self-conceited and stubborn people, full of pride and arrogant in their wealth, perform ostentatious sacrifices in name only, with no regard to the rules of the scriptures.

verses.slok: अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिता: |मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयका: || 18||
verses.transliteration: ahankāraṁ balaṁ darpaṁ kāmaṁ krodhaṁ cha sanśhritāḥmām ātma-para-deheṣhu pradviṣhanto ’bhyasūyakāḥ
verses.translation: BG 16.18:Blinded by egotism, strength, arrogance, desire, and anger, the demonic abuse Me, who am present in their own body and in the bodies of others.

verses.slok: तानहं द्विषत: क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान् |क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु || 19||आसुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि |मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् || 20||
verses.transliteration: tān ahaṁ dviṣhataḥ krūrān sansāreṣhu narādhamānkṣhipāmy ajasram aśhubhān āsurīṣhv eva yoniṣhuāsurīṁ yonim āpannā mūḍhā janmani janmanimām aprāpyaiva kaunteya tato yānty adhamāṁ gatim
verses.translation: BG 16.19-20:These cruel and hateful persons, the vile and vicious of humankind, I constantly hurl into the wombs of those with similar demoniac natures in the cycle of rebirth in the material world. These ignorant souls take birth again and again in demoniac wombs. Failing to reach Me, O Arjun, they gradually sink to the most abominable type of existence.

verses.slok: तानहं द्विषत: क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान् |क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु || 19||आसुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि |मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् || 20||
verses.transliteration: tān ahaṁ dviṣhataḥ krūrān sansāreṣhu narādhamānkṣhipāmy ajasram aśhubhān āsurīṣhv eva yoniṣhuāsurīṁ yonim āpannā mūḍhā janmani janmanimām aprāpyaiva kaunteya tato yānty adhamāṁ gatim
verses.translation: BG 16.19-20:These cruel and hateful persons, the vile and vicious of humankind, I constantly hurl into the wombs of those with similar demoniac natures in the cycle of rebirth in the material world. These ignorant souls take birth again and again in demoniac wombs. Failing to reach Me, O Arjun, they gradually sink to the most abominable type of existence.

verses.slok: त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मन: |काम: क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत् || 21||
verses.transliteration: tri-vidhaṁ narakasyedaṁ dvāraṁ nāśhanam ātmanaḥkāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayaṁ tyajet
verses.translation: BG 16.21:There are three gates leading to the hell of self-destruction for the soul—lust, anger, and greed. Therefore, one should abandon all three.

verses.slok: एतैर्विमुक्त: कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नर: |आचरत्यात्मन: श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् || 22||
verses.transliteration: etair vimuktaḥ kaunteya tamo-dvārais tribhir naraḥācharaty ātmanaḥ śhreyas tato yāti parāṁ gatim
verses.translation: BG 16.22:Those who are free from the three gates to darkness endeavor for the welfare of their soul, and thereby attain the supreme goal.

verses.slok: य: शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारत: |न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम् || 23||
verses.transliteration: yaḥ śhāstra-vidhim utsṛijya vartate kāma-kārataḥna sa siddhim avāpnoti na sukhaṁ na parāṁ gatim
verses.translation: BG 16.23:Those who act under the impulse of desire, discarding the injunctions of the scriptures, attain neither perfection, nor happiness, nor the supreme goal in life.

verses.slok: तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्याकार्यव्यवस्थितौ |ज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म कर्तुमिहार्हसि || 24||
verses.transliteration: tasmāch chhāstraṁ pramāṇaṁ te kāryākārya-vyavasthitaujñātvā śhāstra-vidhānoktaṁ karma kartum ihārhasi
verses.translation: BG 16.24:Therefore, let the scriptures be your authority in determining what should be done and what should not be done. Understand the scriptural injunctions and teachings, and then perform your actions in this world accordingly.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |ये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विता: |तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तम: || 1||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchaye śhāstra-vidhim utsṛijya yajante śhraddhayānvitāḥteṣhāṁ niṣhṭhā tu kā kṛiṣhṇa sattvam āho rajas tamaḥ
verses.translation: BG 17.1:Arjun said: O Krishna, where do they stand who disregard the injunctions of the scriptures, but still worship with faith? Is their faith in the mode of goodness, passion, or ignorance?

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |त्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा |सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां शृणु || 2||
verses.transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvāchatri-vidhā bhavati śhraddhā dehināṁ sā svabhāva-jāsāttvikī rājasī chaiva tāmasī cheti tāṁ śhṛiṇu
verses.translation: BG 17.2:The Supreme Divine Personality said: Every human being is born with innate faith, which can be of three kinds—sāttvic, rājasic,ortāmasic. Now hear about this from Me.

verses.slok: सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत |श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्ध: स एव स: || 3||
verses.transliteration: sattvānurūpā sarvasya śhraddhā bhavati bhārataśhraddhā-mayo ‘yaṁ puruṣho yo yach-chhraddhaḥ sa eva saḥ
verses.translation: BG 17.3:The faith of all humans conforms to the nature of their mind. All people possess faith, and whatever the nature of their faith, that is verily what they are.

verses.slok: यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसा: |प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जना: || 4||
verses.transliteration: yajante sāttvikā devān yakṣha-rakṣhānsi rājasāḥpretān bhūta-gaṇānśh chānye yajante tāmasā janāḥ
verses.translation: BG 17.4:Those in the mode of goodness worship the celestial gods; those in the mode of passion worship theyakṣhasandrākṣhasas; those in the mode of ignorance worship ghosts and spirits.

verses.slok: अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जना: |दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ता: कामरागबलान्विता: || 5||कर्षयन्त: शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतस: |मां चैवान्त:शरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान् || 6||
verses.transliteration: aśhāstra-vihitaṁ ghoraṁ tapyante ye tapo janāḥdambhāhankāra-sanyuktāḥ kāma-rāga-balānvitāḥkarṣhayantaḥ śharīra-sthaṁ bhūta-grāmam achetasaḥmāṁ chaivāntaḥ śharīra-sthaṁ tān viddhy āsura-niśhchayān
verses.translation: BG 17.5-6:Some people perform stern austerities that are not enjoined by the scriptures, but rather motivated by hypocrisy and egotism. Impelled by desire and attachment, they torment not only the elements of their body, but also I who dwell within them as the Supreme Soul. Know these senseless people to be of demoniacal resolves.

verses.slok: अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जना: |दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ता: कामरागबलान्विता: || 5||कर्षयन्त: शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतस: |मां चैवान्त:शरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान् || 6||
verses.transliteration: aśhāstra-vihitaṁ ghoraṁ tapyante ye tapo janāḥdambhāhankāra-sanyuktāḥ kāma-rāga-balānvitāḥkarṣhayantaḥ śharīra-sthaṁ bhūta-grāmam achetasaḥmāṁ chaivāntaḥ śharīra-sthaṁ tān viddhy āsura-niśhchayān
verses.translation: BG 17.5-6:Some people perform stern austerities that are not enjoined by the scriptures, but rather motivated by hypocrisy and egotism. Impelled by desire and attachment, they torment not only the elements of their body, but also I who dwell within them as the Supreme Soul. Know these senseless people to be of demoniacal resolves.

verses.slok: आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रिय: |यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं शृणु || 7||
verses.transliteration: āhāras tv api sarvasya tri-vidho bhavati priyaḥyajñas tapas tathā dānaṁ teṣhāṁ bhedam imaṁ śhṛiṇu
verses.translation: BG 17.7:The food that people prefer is according to their dispositions. The same is true for the sacrifice, austerity, and charity they are inclined (or predisposed) toward. Now hear of the distinctions from Me.

verses.slok: आयु:सत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धना: |रस्या: स्निग्धा: स्थिरा हृद्या आहारा: सात्त्विकप्रिया: || 8||
verses.transliteration: āyuḥ-sattva-balārogya-sukha-prīti-vivardhanāḥrasyāḥ snigdhāḥ sthirā hṛidyā āhārāḥ sāttvika-priyāḥ
verses.translation: BG 17.8:Persons in the mode of goodness prefer foods that promote life span, and increase virtue, strength, health, happiness, and satisfaction. Such foods are juicy, succulent, nourishing, and naturally tasteful.

verses.slok: कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिन: |आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दु:खशोकामयप्रदा: || 9||
verses.transliteration: kaṭv-amla-lavaṇāty-uṣhṇa- tīkṣhṇa-rūkṣha-vidāhinaḥāhārā rājasasyeṣhṭā duḥkha-śhokāmaya-pradāḥ
verses.translation: BG 17.9:Foods that are too bitter, too sour, salty, very hot, pungent, dry, and full of chillies, are dear to persons in the mode of passion. Such foods produce pain, grief, and disease.

verses.slok: यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत् |उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् || 10||
verses.transliteration: yāta-yāmaṁ gata-rasaṁ pūti paryuṣhitaṁ cha yatuchchhiṣhṭam api chāmedhyaṁ bhojanaṁ tāmasa-priyam
verses.translation: BG 17.10:Foods that are overcooked, stale, putrid, polluted, and impure are dear to persons in the mode of ignorance.

verses.slok: अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते |यष्टव्यमेवेति मन: समाधाय स सात्त्विक: || 11||
verses.transliteration: aphalākāṅkṣhibhir yajño vidhi-driṣhṭo ya ijyateyaṣhṭavyam eveti manaḥ samādhāya sa sāttvikaḥ
verses.translation: BG 17.11:Sacrifice that is performed according to scriptural injunctions without expectation of rewards, with the firm conviction of the mind that it is a matter of duty, is of the nature of goodness.

verses.slok: अभिसन्धाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत् |इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम् || 12||
verses.transliteration: abhisandhāya tu phalaṁ dambhārtham api chaiva yatijyate bharata-śhreṣhṭha taṁ yajñaṁ viddhi rājasam
verses.translation: BG 17.12:O best of the Bharatas, know that sacrifice performed for material benefit, or with a hypocritical aim, is in the mode of passion.

verses.slok: विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम् |श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते || 13||
verses.transliteration: vidhi-hīnam asṛiṣhṭānnaṁ mantra-hīnam adakṣhiṇamśhraddhā-virahitaṁ yajñaṁ tāmasaṁ parichakṣhate
verses.translation: BG 17.13:Sacrifice devoid of faith and contrary to the injunctions of the scriptures, in which no food is offered, no mantras chanted, and no donation made, is to be considered in the mode of ignorance.

verses.slok: देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम् |ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते || 14||
verses.transliteration: deva-dwija-guru-prājña- pūjanaṁ śhaucham ārjavambrahmacharyam ahinsā cha śhārīraṁ tapa uchyate
verses.translation: BG 17.14:When worship of the Supreme Lord, the Brahmins, the spiritual master, the wise, and the elders is done with the observance of cleanliness, simplicity, celibacy, and non-violence then this worship is declared as the austerity of the body.

verses.slok: अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत् |स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते || 15||
verses.transliteration: anudvega-karaṁ vākyaṁ satyaṁ priya-hitaṁ cha yatsvādhyāyābhyasanaṁ chaiva vāṅ-mayaṁ tapa uchyate
verses.translation: BG 17.15:Words that do not cause distress, are truthful, inoffensive, and beneficial, as well as regular recitation of the Vedic scriptures—these are declared as austerity of speech.

verses.slok: मन: प्रसाद: सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रह: |भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते || 16||
verses.transliteration: manaḥ-prasādaḥ saumyatvaṁ maunam ātma-vinigrahaḥbhāva-sanśhuddhir ity etat tapo mānasam uchyate
verses.translation: BG 17.16:Serenity of thought, gentleness, silence, self-control, and purity of purpose—all these are declared as austerity of the mind.

verses.slok: श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्त्रिविधं नरै: |अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तै: सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते || 17||
verses.transliteration: śhraddhayā parayā taptaṁ tapas tat tri-vidhaṁ naraiḥaphalākāṅkṣhibhir yuktaiḥ sāttvikaṁ parichakṣhate
verses.translation: BG 17.17:When devout persons with ardent faith practice these three-fold austerities without yearning for material rewards, they are designated as austerities in the mode of goodness.

verses.slok: सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत् |क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम् || 18||
verses.transliteration: satkāra-māna-pūjārthaṁ tapo dambhena chaiva yatkriyate tad iha proktaṁ rājasaṁ chalam adhruvam
verses.translation: BG 17.18:Austerity that is performed with ostentation for the sake of gaining honor, respect, and adoration is in the mode of passion. Its benefits are unstable and transitory.

verses.slok: मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तप: |परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् || 19||
verses.transliteration: mūḍha-grāheṇātmano yat pīḍayā kriyate tapaḥparasyotsādanārthaṁ vā tat tāmasam udāhṛitam
verses.translation: BG 17.19:Austerity that is performed by those with confused notions, and which involves torturing the self or harming others, is described to be in the mode of ignorance.

verses.slok: दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे |देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् || 20||
verses.transliteration: dātavyam iti yad dānaṁ dīyate ‘nupakāriṇedeśhe kāle cha pātre cha tad dānaṁ sāttvikaṁ smṛitam
verses.translation: BG 17.20:Charity given to a worthy person simply because it is right to give, without consideration of anything in return, at the proper time and in the proper place, is stated to be in the mode of goodness.

verses.slok: यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुन: |दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम् || 21||
verses.transliteration: yat tu pratyupakārārthaṁ phalam uddiśhya vā punaḥdīyate cha parikliṣhṭaṁ tad dānaṁ rājasaṁ smṛitam
verses.translation: BG 17.21:But charity given with reluctance, with the hope of a return or in expectation of a reward, is said to be in the mode of passion.

verses.slok: अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते |असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् || 22||
verses.transliteration: adeśha-kāle yad dānam apātrebhyaśh cha dīyateasat-kṛitam avajñātaṁ tat tāmasam udāhṛitam
verses.translation: BG 17.22:And that charity, which is given at the wrong place and wrong time to unworthy persons, without showing respect, or with contempt, is held to be of the nature of nescience.

verses.slok: ॐ तत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविध: स्मृत: |ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिता: पुरा || 23||
verses.transliteration: oṁ tat sad iti nirdeśho brahmaṇas tri-vidhaḥ smṛitaḥbrāhmaṇās tena vedāśh cha yajñāśh cha vihitāḥ purā
verses.translation: BG 17.23:The words “Om Tat Sat” have been declared as symbolic representations of the Supreme Absolute Truth, from the beginning of creation. From them came the priests, scriptures, and sacrifice.

verses.slok: तस्माद् ॐ इत्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतप:क्रिया: |प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ता: सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम् || 24||
verses.transliteration: tasmād oṁ ity udāhṛitya yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-kriyāḥpravartante vidhānoktāḥ satataṁ brahma-vādinām
verses.translation: BG 17.24:Therefore, when performing acts of sacrifice, offering charity, or undertaking penance, expounders of the Vedas always begin by uttering “Om” according to the prescriptions of Vedic injunctions.

verses.slok: तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतप:क्रिया: |दानक्रियाश्च विविधा: क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षिभि: || 25||
verses.transliteration: tad ity anabhisandhāya phalaṁ yajña-tapaḥ-kriyāḥdāna-kriyāśh cha vividhāḥ kriyante mokṣha-kāṅkṣhibhiḥ
verses.translation: BG 17.25:Persons who do not desire fruitive rewards, but seek to be free from material entanglements, utter the word “Tat” along with acts of austerity, sacrifice, and charity.

verses.slok: सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते |प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्द: पार्थ युज्यते || 26||यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थिति: सदिति चोच्यते |कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते || 27||
verses.transliteration: sad-bhāve sādhu-bhāve cha sad ity etat prayujyatepraśhaste karmaṇi tathā sach-chhabdaḥ pārtha yujyateyajñe tapasi dāne cha sthitiḥ sad iti chochyatekarma chaiva tad-arthīyaṁ sad ity evābhidhīyate
verses.translation: BG 17.26-27:The word “Sat” means eternal existence and goodness. O Arjun, it is also used to describe an auspicious action. Being established in the performance of sacrifice, penance, and charity, is also described by the word “Sat.” And so any act for such purposes is named “Sat.”

verses.slok: सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते |प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्द: पार्थ युज्यते || 26||यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थिति: सदिति चोच्यते |कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते || 27||
verses.transliteration: sad-bhāve sādhu-bhāve cha sad ity etat prayujyatepraśhaste karmaṇi tathā sach-chhabdaḥ pārtha yujyateyajñe tapasi dāne cha sthitiḥ sad iti chochyatekarma chaiva tad-arthīyaṁ sad ity evābhidhīyate
verses.translation: BG 17.26-27:The word “Sat” means eternal existence and goodness. O Arjun, it is also used to describe an auspicious action. Being established in the performance of sacrifice, penance, and charity, is also described by the word “Sat.” And so any act for such purposes is named “Sat.”

verses.slok: अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत् |असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह || 28||
verses.transliteration: aśhraddhayā hutaṁ dattaṁ tapas taptaṁ kṛitaṁ cha yatasad ity uchyate pārtha na cha tat pretya no iha
verses.translation: BG 17.28:O son of Pritha, whatever acts of sacrifice, charity, or penance are done without faith, are termed as“Asat.”They are useless both in this world and the next.

verses.slok: अर्जुन उवाच |सन्न्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वमिच्छामि वेदितुम् |त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केशिनिषूदन || 1||
verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāchasannyāsasya mahā-bāho tattvam ichchhāmi veditumtyāgasya cha hṛiṣhīkeśha pṛithak keśhi-niṣhūdana
verses.translation: BG 18.1:Arjun said: O mighty-armed Krishna, I wish to understand the nature ofsanyās(renunciation of actions) andtyāg(renunciation of desire for the fruits of actions). O Hrishikesh, I also wish to know the distinction between the two, O Keshinisudan.

verses.slok: श्रीभगवानुवाच |काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं सन्न्यासं कवयो विदु: |सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणा: || 2||
verses.transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvāchakāmyānāṁ karmaṇāṁ nyāsaṁ sannyāsaṁ kavayo viduḥsarva-karma-phala-tyāgaṁ prāhus tyāgaṁ vichakṣhaṇāḥ
verses.translation: BG 18.2:The Supreme Divine Personality said: Giving up of actions motivated by desire is what the learned understand assanyās. Relinquishing the fruits of all actions is what the wise declare to betyāg.

verses.slok: त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म प्राहुर्मनीषिण: |यज्ञदानतप:कर्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे || 3||
verses.transliteration: tyājyaṁ doṣha-vad ity eke karma prāhur manīṣhiṇaḥyajña-dāna-tapaḥ-karma na tyājyam iti chāpare
verses.translation: BG 18.3:Some learned people declare that all kinds of actions should be given up as evil, while others maintain that acts of sacrifice, charity, and penance should never be abandoned.

verses.slok: निश्चयं शृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम |त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविध: सम्प्रकीर्तित: || 4||
